content.content:
1	name: Nightly upstream merge
2	
3	# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
4	# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
5	# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
6	# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
7	# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the dependency gates, then
8	# (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) an Opus review of the WHOLE merged delta — clean or
9	# conflicted — against the fork feature manifest (a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
10	# can semantically break a fork feature with zero conflicts; v1.14.2 dropped the
11	# sidebar snooze/bin fields that way). Only then the deterministic ARM64 build.
12	# On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
13	# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
14	# semantic breakage verdict, build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline
15	# untouched -> recovery loop.
16	#
17	# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
18	# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
19	#
20	# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
21	# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.
22	
23	on:
24	  schedule:
25	    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
26	  workflow_dispatch:
27	    inputs:
28	      force_tag:
29	        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
30	        required: false
31	        default: ''
32	      rehearse:
33	        description: 'Dry-run: full merge + resolver + every gate, but NO candidate push, NO build, NO advance (emulates the overnight run safely)'
34	        required: false
35	        default: ''
36	      rehearse_base:
37	        description: 'Rehearse from this historical main SHA (requires rehearse=true + force_tag; replays a past nightly exactly)'
38	        required: false
39	        default: ''
40	
41	permissions:
42	  contents: write
43	
44	# (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) Never let two nightly instances interleave: an overlapping
45	# schedule + manual dispatch can delete/replace the mutable candidate branch
46	# between one run's merge, build checkout and advance. Queue, never cancel — a
47	# running merge/build must finish or fail on its own.
48	concurrency:
49	  group: nightly-merge
50	  cancel-in-progress: false
51	
52	jobs:
53	  merge:
54	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
55	    outputs:
56	      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
57	      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
58	      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
59	      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
60	      sha: ${{ steps.do.outputs.sha }}
61	    env:
62	      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
63	      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: '1'
64	      CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: '1'
65	      # Conflict resolution can touch large files; give Opus headroom.
66	      CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: '128000'
67	    steps:
68	      - name: Checkout fork (full history)
69	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
70	        with:
71	          fetch-depth: 0
72	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) Always merge from CURRENT main, not the event
73	          # SHA: a queued run (concurrency holds one pending) otherwise checks
74	          # out the commit main pointed at when it was TRIGGERED — pre-advance
75	          # — re-merges an already-merged tag into a stale tree, replaces the
76	          # freshly published release with a sibling merge, and its final
77	          # main push dies non-fast-forward AFTER publishing.
78	          ref: main
79	
80	      - name: Setup Node.js
81	        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
82	        with:
83	          node-version: '22'
84	
85	      # (LOCK-REGEN) Lock regeneration below needs bun; pin the EXACT version
86	      # the build uses so the lock we commit is byte-identical to what the
87	      # frozen build install consumes.
88	      - name: Setup Bun (match the build's pinned version)
89	        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
90	        with:
91	          bun-version: '1.3.14'
92	
93	      - name: Merge upstream + resolve + gate
94	        id: do
95	        shell: bash
96	        # Dispatch inputs come in as ENV, never inline ${{ }} in the script:
97	        # (a) GitHub caps any expression-bearing scalar at 21000 chars — an
98	        # inline expression in this large script made the whole workflow
99	        # unparseable (the 2026-07-17 push-validation failure: schedule would
100	        # silently not fire); (b) env is the non-injectable way to consume
101	        # user input in shell.
102	        env:
103	          FORCE_TAG: ${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}
104	          REHEARSE: ${{ github.event.inputs.rehearse }}
105	          REHEARSE_BASE: ${{ github.event.inputs.rehearse_base }}
106	        run: |
107	          set -euo pipefail
108	          git config user.name "superset-arm64-bot"
109	          git config user.email "actions@users.noreply.github.com"
110	
111	          # --- validate dispatch inputs at the boundary ------------------------
112	          FORCE="${FORCE_TAG:-}"
113	          REHEARSE="${REHEARSE:-}"
114	          REHEARSE_BASE="${REHEARSE_BASE:-}"
115	          if [ -n "$REHEARSE" ] && [ "$REHEARSE" != "true" ] && [ "$REHEARSE" != "false" ]; then
116	            echo "::error::rehearse must be '', 'true' or 'false' (got '$REHEARSE')"; exit 1
117	          fi
118	          if [ -n "$REHEARSE_BASE" ]; then
119	            if [ "$REHEARSE" != "true" ] || [ -z "$FORCE" ]; then
120	              echo "::error::rehearse_base requires rehearse=true AND force_tag (replaying a past nightly needs both the base and the tag)"; exit 1
121	            fi
122	            if ! REHEARSE_SHA=$(git rev-parse -q --verify "$REHEARSE_BASE^{commit}"); then
123	              echo "::error::rehearse_base '$REHEARSE_BASE' is not a commit in this repo"; exit 1
124	            fi
125	            echo "REHEARSAL: replaying from historical base $REHEARSE_SHA"
126	            git checkout -q "$REHEARSE_SHA"
127	          fi
128	
129	          # --- detect the upstream tag to merge --------------------------------
130	          git remote add upstream https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git 2>/dev/null || true
131	          # Fetch upstream's desktop-v* tags into an ISOLATED ref namespace.
132	          # Our OWN one-version release tags live in refs/tags/desktop-v<version>
133	          # (gh release create makes them at our build commit). A plain fetch into
134	          # refs/tags/ collides with every released version ("would clobber existing
135	          # tag" -> non-zero fetch -> set -e abort). The isolated namespace + --force
136	          # keeps upstream's source tags cleanly separate from our release tags.
137	          git fetch --no-recurse-submodules --force upstream \
138	            'refs/tags/desktop-v*:refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*'
139	          # Validate the dispatch input at the boundary: it is used in ref
140	          # names — reject anything that is not a plain desktop-v<semver-ish>
141	          # tag.
142	          if [ -n "$FORCE" ] && ! [[ "$FORCE" =~ ^desktop-v[0-9][0-9.]*$ ]]; then
143	            echo "::error::force_tag '$FORCE' is not a desktop-v<version> tag"; exit 1
144	          fi
145	          if [ -n "$FORCE" ]; then
146	            NEWTAG="$FORCE"
147	          else
148	            LATEST=$(git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:lstrip=2)' 'refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*' | sed -n 's/^desktop-v\([0-9][0-9.]*\)$/\1/p' | sort -V | tail -1)
149	            NEWTAG="desktop-v${LATEST}"
150	          fi
151	          BASE=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .fork/upstream-baseline.txt)
152	          echo "baseline=$BASE  latest_upstream=$NEWTAG"
153	          if [ "$NEWTAG" = "$BASE" ]; then
154	            echo "Already at $BASE — nothing to merge."
155	            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
156	            exit 0
157	          fi
158	          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
159	            echo "::error::upstream tag $NEWTAG not found"; exit 1
160	          fi
161	
162	          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------
163	          CAND="nightly/${NEWTAG}"
164	          git switch -c "$CAND"
165	          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
166	          set +e
167	          git merge --no-commit --no-ff "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}"
168	          set -e
169	
170	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) `set +e` above accepts EVERY nonzero merge exit,
171	          # not just "conflicts". A fatal merge failure (lock error, corrupt
172	          # object, OOM) leaves NO MERGE_HEAD and possibly no conflicts either —
173	          # without this check it would read as a clean merge and we'd commit a
174	          # single-parent non-merge with a bumped baseline.
175	          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
176	            echo "::error::git merge of $NEWTAG failed fatally (no MERGE_HEAD) — aborting. Baseline untouched."
177	            exit 1
178	          fi
179	
180	          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) Surface — never silently discard — upstream's
181	          # workflow-tree delta before we drop it: upstream sometimes moves
182	          # build-relevant logic INTO CI (a codegen step, a required env var).
183	          # The delta goes to the job summary + log and is handed to the
184	          # semantic review below so build-relevant CI changes get flagged.
185	          WFDELTA_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/upstream-workflow-delta.diff"
186	          : > "$WFDELTA_FILE"
187	          if git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
188	            git diff "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows > "$WFDELTA_FILE" || true
189	          fi
190	          if [ -s "$WFDELTA_FILE" ]; then
191	            {
192	              echo "## Upstream workflow-tree delta ($BASE → $NEWTAG) — DROPPED, .github/workflows is fork-owned"
193	              echo '```'
194	              git diff --name-status "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows
195	              echo '```'
196	              echo "Full patch in the job log (group: dropped upstream workflow delta). Port anything build-relevant by hand."
197	            } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
198	            echo "::group::dropped upstream workflow delta"
199	            cat "$WFDELTA_FILE"
200	            echo "::endgroup::"
201	          fi
202	
203	          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows is FORK-OWNED: the fork
204	          # carries ZERO upstream workflow files (upstream CI targets upstream's
205	          # infra/secrets and is meaningless here), and the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN
206	          # can NEVER push a .github/workflows/* change — GitHub rejects both
207	          # the candidate push and the main fast-forward with "refusing to allow
208	          # a GitHub App to create or update workflow … without `workflows`
209	          # permission" (desktop-v1.14.3 deploy-relay.yml incident: the merge,
210	          # gates and review all passed, then the push died). Restore our exact
211	          # workflow set BEFORE conflict detection: upstream-ADDED files are
212	          # dropped from index+worktree (--no-overlay), upstream edits to files
213	          # we long deleted resolve as ours, and the AI resolver never sees a
214	          # workflow path it could resurrect.
215	          #
216	          # UNMERGED paths must be cleared FIRST: `git checkout <tree> -- <dir>`
217	          # hard-errors ("path … is unmerged") on a modify/delete entry whose
218	          # file is absent in HEAD — upstream modifying a workflow we deleted
219	          # (desktop-v1.15.0 release-cli.yml incident: the nightly died right
220	          # here, before the resolver, two nights running). Resolve each
221	          # unmerged workflow path to OUR side: restore from HEAD when we carry
222	          # it, git rm when we deleted it.
223	          git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U -- .github/workflows | while IFS= read -r p; do
224	            if git cat-file -e "HEAD:$p" 2>/dev/null; then
225	              git checkout HEAD -- "$p"
226	            else
227	              git rm -q -f -- "$p"
228	            fi
229	          done
230	          git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows
231	
232	          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
233	          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
234	            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
235	            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
236	            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
237	            attempt=1
238	            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
239	              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
240	              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
241	              echo "::endgroup::"
242	              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
243	              attempt=$((attempt+1))
244	            done
245	            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
246	            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
247	              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
248	              exit 1
249	            fi
250	          else
251	            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
252	          fi
253	
254	          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
255	          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
256	          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
257	          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
258	          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
259	          # runner spins up), not here:
260	          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
261	          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
262	          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
263	          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
264	          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
265	          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
266	          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
267	          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
268	          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
269	          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
270	          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
271	          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
272	          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
273	          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
274	          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
275	          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
276	          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
277	          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
278	          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
279	          echo "::endgroup::"
280	
281	          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
282	          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
283	          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
284	          set +e
285	          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
286	          rc=$?
287	          set -e
288	          cat "$LOCKLOG"
289	          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
290	            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
291	              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
292	            else
293	              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
294	            fi
295	            exit 1
296	          fi
297	          echo "::endgroup::"
298	
299	          # --- (REFERR-REPAIR) run the build's ReferenceError gate HERE and let
300	          # Opus repair what it catches, so an import-dropping conflict
301	          # resolution self-heals tonight instead of failing the build and
302	          # costing a red night + manual recovery (desktop-v1.15.1
303	          # useChangesTab incident: the resolver took upstream's rewritten
304	          # import block, kept the fork's JSX, dropped Button/Tooltip/cn).
305	          # Repair is scoped HARD to cannot-find-name diagnostics; anything
306	          # still dirty after 2 attempts aborts exactly as before (the build
307	          # would have rejected it anyway — this just moves the gate earlier
308	          # and adds a bounded fix loop). --ignore-scripts keeps the linux
309	          # install pure-JS resolution (no native/postinstall platform quirks,
310	          # the reason this job deliberately avoids a full install elsewhere).
311	          echo "::group::(REFERR-REPAIR) ReferenceError gate + bounded AI repair"
312	          bun install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
313	          REFERRLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/referr.log"
314	          run_referr_gate() {
315	            set +e
316	            node scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs > "$REFERRLOG" 2>&1
317	            REFERR_RC=$?
318	            set -e
319	            cat "$REFERRLOG"
320	          }
321	          run_referr_gate
322	          # The repair agent must only EDIT the work tree — record the commit
323	          # state so an agent that disobeys "Do NOT commit" (advancing HEAD
324	          # and vacating the merge) is caught, not silently laundered into a
325	          # clean-looking tree.
326	          HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
327	          attempt=1
328	          while [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ] && [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
329	            DIAGS=$(grep -F '(REFERR-GATE)' "$REFERRLOG" | head -40 || true)
330	            if [ -z "$DIAGS" ]; then
331	              # Gate failed WITHOUT naming diagnostics = the checker itself
332	              # broke (install/tsc infra). AI repair has nothing to act on.
333	              echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) gate failed without diagnostics — checker infrastructure problem, not repairable. Log above. Baseline untouched."
334	              exit 1
335	            fi
336	            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
337	            REPAIR_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository holding a staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG}. The typecheck gate found cannot-find-name diagnostics — identifiers that would throw ReferenceError at runtime, almost always imports dropped during conflict resolution. Diagnostics: ${DIAGS} — Fix ONLY these diagnostics, and ONLY by ADDING code: add the missing import lines (match how the same symbols are imported elsewhere in this repo, e.g. @superset/ui/*) or restore the dropped declaration. NEVER delete, comment out, or rewrite existing code, JSX, or use-sites to silence a diagnostic — removing the code that uses the identifier is feature loss and is forbidden. Do NOT touch any file not named in the diagnostics. After editing each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Output nothing."
338	            echo "::group::claude referr-repair attempt $attempt"
339	            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REPAIR_PROMPT" || true
340	            echo "::endgroup::"
341	            run_referr_gate
342	            attempt=$((attempt+1))
343	          done
344	          if [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
345	            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) cannot-find-name diagnostics persist after repair attempts — aborting (the build would reject this tree). Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Baseline untouched."
346	            exit 1
347	          fi
348	          if [ "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" != "$HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR" ] || ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
349	            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) the repair run committed or destroyed the merge state — refusing to trust it. Baseline untouched."
350	            exit 1
351	          fi
352	          # Stage any repair edits NOW: the semantic review below reads the
353	          # STAGED diff ('git diff --cached'); an unstaged repair would be
354	          # invisible to the review yet still swept into the final commit's
355	          # 'git add -A' — reviewed tree and shipped tree must be identical.
356	          git add -A
357	          echo "::endgroup::"
358	
359	          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
360	          # Runs AFTER conflict resolution AND the REFERR repair loop, so it
361	          # judges the FINAL tree — a repair that "fixed" a diagnostic by
362	          # deleting fork code (forbidden, but prompts are not proofs) still
363	          # has to get past the marker grep here.
364	          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
365	          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
366	          # Guard the guard: an empty/renamed block would pass VACUOUSLY (zero
367	          # markers checked == zero missing). Fail loud instead.
368	          MARKER_LINES=$(awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md)
369	          if ! printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | grep -q '[^[:space:]]'; then
370	            echo "::error::FEATURES.md markers block is missing or empty — the marker gate would pass vacuously. Refusing to trust this merge."
371	            exit 1
372	          fi
373	          printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
374	            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
375	            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
376	              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
377	            else
378	              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
379	              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
380	            fi
381	          done
382	          echo "::endgroup::"
383	          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
384	            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
385	            exit 1
386	          fi
387	
388	          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
389	          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
390	          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
391	          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
392	          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
393	          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
394	          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
395	          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
396	          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
397	          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
398	          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
399	          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
400	          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
401	          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
402	          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
403	          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
404	          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
405	          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
406	          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
407	          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
408	          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. (5) If the file at ${WFDELTA_FILE} is non-empty, read it — it is upstream's CI/workflow delta we deliberately did NOT merge (.github/workflows is fork-owned). If it moves BUILD-RELEVANT logic into upstream CI (a codegen/generate step, a newly required env var, a packaging/signing step) that our fork's own build workflow would now silently miss, append one line per item after the verdict: 'CI-NOTE: <what upstream CI now does that the fork build may need>'. CI-NOTEs alone never change the verdict. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
409	          attempt=1
410	          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
411	            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
412	            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
413	            echo "::endgroup::"
414	            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
415	            attempt=$((attempt+1))
416	          done
417	          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
418	          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
419	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
420	            exit 1
421	          fi
422	          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
423	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
424	            exit 1
425	          fi
426	          echo "--- review verdict ---"
427	          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
428	          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
429	            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
430	            exit 1
431	          fi
432	          echo "::endgroup::"
433	
434	          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
435	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) The release tag is derived SOLELY from
436	          # apps/desktop/package.json by the build — if the merged version
437	          # doesn't match the tag being merged, the build would delete and
438	          # rebuild the WRONG release (e.g. a bad package.json conflict
439	          # resolution keeping 1.14.3 while merging desktop-v1.15.0), then
440	          # advance the baseline past a version that no longer has a correct
441	          # release. Assert the invariant here instead.
442	          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
443	          if [ "desktop-v${VERSION}" != "$NEWTAG" ]; then
444	            echo "::error::merged apps/desktop/package.json version ($VERSION) does not match $NEWTAG — bad version resolution; refusing to commit. Baseline untouched."
445	            exit 1
446	          fi
447	          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) postcondition: the tree about to be committed
448	          # must contain ZERO workflow-dir changes — not from the merge, not
449	          # from the AI resolver, not from the review run. The restore step
450	          # above is the mechanism; this is the proof (a workflow delta would be
451	          # rejected by the push anyway — fail here, loud, with the baseline
452	          # untouched).
453	          if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows)" ]; then
454	            echo "::error::(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows differs from HEAD after resolve/review — refusing to commit:"
455	            git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows
456	            exit 1
457	          fi
458	          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
459	          git add -A
460	          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
461	          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
462	          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
463	          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
464	          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
465	          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
466	          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
467	          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
468	          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
469	          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
470	          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
471	          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
472	          # (REHEARSAL) Everything above — merge, resolver, repair, every
473	          # gate, even the local merge commit — ran for real; stop before
474	          # anything leaves this runner. This is the on-demand emulation of
475	          # the overnight run: after ANY change to this workflow, dispatch
476	          # rehearse=true (+ force_tag / rehearse_base to replay a past
477	          # night) and get tonight's answer today, with zero side effects.
478	          if [ "$REHEARSE" = "true" ]; then
479	            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
480	            {
481	              echo "## REHEARSAL PASSED — $NEWTAG"
482	              echo "Merge + conflict resolution + REFERR-REPAIR + marker gate + dependency gates + semantic review all green."
483	              echo "Merge commit (local only, discarded): $(git rev-parse HEAD)"
484	              echo "No candidate pushed, no build, no advance, baseline untouched."
485	            } | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
486	            exit 0
487	          fi
488	          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
489	          git push origin "$CAND"
490	
491	          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
492	          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
493	          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
494	          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
495	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
496	          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
497	          # advanced unnoticed.
498	          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
499	          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."
500	
501	  build:
502	    needs: merge
503	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
504	    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
505	    with:
506	      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
507	      # branch mutation between jobs.
508	      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
509	      publish: true
510	
511	  advance:
512	    needs: [merge, build]
513	    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
514	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
515	    steps:
516	      - name: Checkout
517	        uses: actions/checkout@v5
518	        with:
519	          fetch-depth: 0
520	      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
521	        shell: bash
522	        run: |
523	          set -euo pipefail
524	          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
525	          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
526	          git fetch origin "$CAND"
527	          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
528	          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
529	            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
530	            exit 1
531	          fi
532	          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
533	          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
534	          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
535	          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."
536

file.content:
name: Nightly upstream merge

# The ongoing half of the architecture. Nightly: if upstream superset published a
# newer desktop-v* tag than .fork/upstream-baseline.txt, merge that tag into the
# fork. git's 3-way merge handles the non-conflicting majority deterministically;
# Opus (--effort high) resolves ONLY the conflicted files, preserving every
# feature. Then the FEATURES.md marker gate runs, then the dependency gates, then
# (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) an Opus review of the WHOLE merged delta — clean or
# conflicted — against the fork feature manifest (a cleanly-merging upstream hunk
# can semantically break a fork feature with zero conflicts; v1.14.2 dropped the
# sidebar snooze/bin fields that way). Only then the deterministic ARM64 build.
# On a clean merge + green build the build publishes a full Release and we
# fast-forward the baseline. Any failure (unresolvable conflict, lost marker,
# semantic breakage verdict, build failure) hard-aborts and leaves the baseline
# untouched -> recovery loop.
#
# AI cost scales with the UPSTREAM delta per release, NOT with how large the fork
# grows. The build itself stays 100% AI-free.
#
# NOTE: schedule only fires from the DEFAULT branch, so this is dormant until the
# fork-baseline branch is promoted to main.

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 5 * * *' # 05:00 UTC daily
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      force_tag:
        description: 'Merge a specific upstream desktop-v* tag (blank = latest)'
        required: false
        default: ''
      rehearse:
        description: 'Dry-run: full merge + resolver + every gate, but NO candidate push, NO build, NO advance (emulates the overnight run safely)'
        required: false
        default: ''
      rehearse_base:
        description: 'Rehearse from this historical main SHA (requires rehearse=true + force_tag; replays a past nightly exactly)'
        required: false
        default: ''

permissions:
  contents: write

# (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) Never let two nightly instances interleave: an overlapping
# schedule + manual dispatch can delete/replace the mutable candidate branch
# between one run's merge, build checkout and advance. Queue, never cancel — a
# running merge/build must finish or fail on its own.
concurrency:
  group: nightly-merge
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  merge:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      should_build: ${{ steps.do.outputs.should_build }}
      newtag: ${{ steps.do.outputs.newtag }}
      version: ${{ steps.do.outputs.version }}
      candidate: ${{ steps.do.outputs.candidate }}
      sha: ${{ steps.do.outputs.sha }}
    env:
      CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN }}
      DISABLE_NON_ESSENTIAL_MODEL_CALLS: '1'
      CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC: '1'
      # Conflict resolution can touch large files; give Opus headroom.
      CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS: '128000'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout fork (full history)
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) Always merge from CURRENT main, not the event
          # SHA: a queued run (concurrency holds one pending) otherwise checks
          # out the commit main pointed at when it was TRIGGERED — pre-advance
          # — re-merges an already-merged tag into a stale tree, replaces the
          # freshly published release with a sibling merge, and its final
          # main push dies non-fast-forward AFTER publishing.
          ref: main

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '22'

      # (LOCK-REGEN) Lock regeneration below needs bun; pin the EXACT version
      # the build uses so the lock we commit is byte-identical to what the
      # frozen build install consumes.
      - name: Setup Bun (match the build's pinned version)
        uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
        with:
          bun-version: '1.3.14'

      - name: Merge upstream + resolve + gate
        id: do
        shell: bash
        # Dispatch inputs come in as ENV, never inline ${{ }} in the script:
        # (a) GitHub caps any expression-bearing scalar at 21000 chars — an
        # inline expression in this large script made the whole workflow
        # unparseable (the 2026-07-17 push-validation failure: schedule would
        # silently not fire); (b) env is the non-injectable way to consume
        # user input in shell.
        env:
          FORCE_TAG: ${{ github.event.inputs.force_tag }}
          REHEARSE: ${{ github.event.inputs.rehearse }}
          REHEARSE_BASE: ${{ github.event.inputs.rehearse_base }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          git config user.name "superset-arm64-bot"
          git config user.email "actions@users.noreply.github.com"

          # --- validate dispatch inputs at the boundary ------------------------
          FORCE="${FORCE_TAG:-}"
          REHEARSE="${REHEARSE:-}"
          REHEARSE_BASE="${REHEARSE_BASE:-}"
          if [ -n "$REHEARSE" ] && [ "$REHEARSE" != "true" ] && [ "$REHEARSE" != "false" ]; then
            echo "::error::rehearse must be '', 'true' or 'false' (got '$REHEARSE')"; exit 1
          fi
          if [ -n "$REHEARSE_BASE" ]; then
            if [ "$REHEARSE" != "true" ] || [ -z "$FORCE" ]; then
              echo "::error::rehearse_base requires rehearse=true AND force_tag (replaying a past nightly needs both the base and the tag)"; exit 1
            fi
            if ! REHEARSE_SHA=$(git rev-parse -q --verify "$REHEARSE_BASE^{commit}"); then
              echo "::error::rehearse_base '$REHEARSE_BASE' is not a commit in this repo"; exit 1
            fi
            echo "REHEARSAL: replaying from historical base $REHEARSE_SHA"
            git checkout -q "$REHEARSE_SHA"
          fi

          # --- detect the upstream tag to merge --------------------------------
          git remote add upstream https://github.com/superset-sh/superset.git 2>/dev/null || true
          # Fetch upstream's desktop-v* tags into an ISOLATED ref namespace.
          # Our OWN one-version release tags live in refs/tags/desktop-v<version>
          # (gh release create makes them at our build commit). A plain fetch into
          # refs/tags/ collides with every released version ("would clobber existing
          # tag" -> non-zero fetch -> set -e abort). The isolated namespace + --force
          # keeps upstream's source tags cleanly separate from our release tags.
          git fetch --no-recurse-submodules --force upstream \
            'refs/tags/desktop-v*:refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*'
          # Validate the dispatch input at the boundary: it is used in ref
          # names — reject anything that is not a plain desktop-v<semver-ish>
          # tag.
          if [ -n "$FORCE" ] && ! [[ "$FORCE" =~ ^desktop-v[0-9][0-9.]*$ ]]; then
            echo "::error::force_tag '$FORCE' is not a desktop-v<version> tag"; exit 1
          fi
          if [ -n "$FORCE" ]; then
            NEWTAG="$FORCE"
          else
            LATEST=$(git for-each-ref --format='%(refname:lstrip=2)' 'refs/upstream-tags/desktop-v*' | sed -n 's/^desktop-v\([0-9][0-9.]*\)$/\1/p' | sort -V | tail -1)
            NEWTAG="desktop-v${LATEST}"
          fi
          BASE=$(tr -d '[:space:]' < .fork/upstream-baseline.txt)
          echo "baseline=$BASE  latest_upstream=$NEWTAG"
          if [ "$NEWTAG" = "$BASE" ]; then
            echo "Already at $BASE — nothing to merge."
            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::upstream tag $NEWTAG not found"; exit 1
          fi

          # --- candidate branch + merge ---------------------------------------
          CAND="nightly/${NEWTAG}"
          git switch -c "$CAND"
          echo "Merging $NEWTAG into the fork (no auto-commit)…"
          set +e
          git merge --no-commit --no-ff "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG^{commit}"
          set -e

          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) `set +e` above accepts EVERY nonzero merge exit,
          # not just "conflicts". A fatal merge failure (lock error, corrupt
          # object, OOM) leaves NO MERGE_HEAD and possibly no conflicts either —
          # without this check it would read as a clean merge and we'd commit a
          # single-parent non-merge with a bumped baseline.
          if ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::git merge of $NEWTAG failed fatally (no MERGE_HEAD) — aborting. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi

          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) Surface — never silently discard — upstream's
          # workflow-tree delta before we drop it: upstream sometimes moves
          # build-relevant logic INTO CI (a codegen step, a required env var).
          # The delta goes to the job summary + log and is handed to the
          # semantic review below so build-relevant CI changes get flagged.
          WFDELTA_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/upstream-workflow-delta.diff"
          : > "$WFDELTA_FILE"
          if git rev-parse -q --verify "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE^{commit}" >/dev/null; then
            git diff "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows > "$WFDELTA_FILE" || true
          fi
          if [ -s "$WFDELTA_FILE" ]; then
            {
              echo "## Upstream workflow-tree delta ($BASE → $NEWTAG) — DROPPED, .github/workflows is fork-owned"
              echo '```'
              git diff --name-status "refs/upstream-tags/$BASE" "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- .github/workflows
              echo '```'
              echo "Full patch in the job log (group: dropped upstream workflow delta). Port anything build-relevant by hand."
            } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            echo "::group::dropped upstream workflow delta"
            cat "$WFDELTA_FILE"
            echo "::endgroup::"
          fi

          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows is FORK-OWNED: the fork
          # carries ZERO upstream workflow files (upstream CI targets upstream's
          # infra/secrets and is meaningless here), and the Actions GITHUB_TOKEN
          # can NEVER push a .github/workflows/* change — GitHub rejects both
          # the candidate push and the main fast-forward with "refusing to allow
          # a GitHub App to create or update workflow … without `workflows`
          # permission" (desktop-v1.14.3 deploy-relay.yml incident: the merge,
          # gates and review all passed, then the push died). Restore our exact
          # workflow set BEFORE conflict detection: upstream-ADDED files are
          # dropped from index+worktree (--no-overlay), upstream edits to files
          # we long deleted resolve as ours, and the AI resolver never sees a
          # workflow path it could resurrect.
          #
          # UNMERGED paths must be cleared FIRST: `git checkout <tree> -- <dir>`
          # hard-errors ("path … is unmerged") on a modify/delete entry whose
          # file is absent in HEAD — upstream modifying a workflow we deleted
          # (desktop-v1.15.0 release-cli.yml incident: the nightly died right
          # here, before the resolver, two nights running). Resolve each
          # unmerged workflow path to OUR side: restore from HEAD when we carry
          # it, git rm when we deleted it.
          git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U -- .github/workflows | while IFS= read -r p; do
            if git cat-file -e "HEAD:$p" 2>/dev/null; then
              git checkout HEAD -- "$p"
            else
              git rm -q -f -- "$p"
            fi
          done
          git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows

          CONFLICTS=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
          if [ -n "$CONFLICTS" ]; then
            echo "::group::conflicts to resolve"; echo "$CONFLICTS"; echo "::endgroup::"
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository mid-merge: upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} is being merged into our Windows-ARM64 fork. ONLY the files listed by 'git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U' are conflicted. Resolve EVERY conflicted file: combine upstream's change with our fork's feature so BOTH intents survive and our feature keeps working; where upstream deliberately removed or refactored something we had only lightly touched, follow upstream. Do NOT edit any non-conflicted file. Do NOT weaken or remove any feature marker tracked in FEATURES.md. After resolving each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Do NOT run any other git command. When every conflict is resolved and staged, stop. Output nothing."
            attempt=1
            while [ $attempt -le 3 ]; do
              echo "::group::claude resolve attempt $attempt"
              claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PROMPT" || true
              echo "::endgroup::"
              if [ -z "$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)" ]; then break; fi
              attempt=$((attempt+1))
            done
            REMAIN=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=U || true)
            if [ -n "$REMAIN" ]; then
              echo "::error::Opus could not resolve all conflicts for $NEWTAG — recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Remaining:"; echo "$REMAIN"
              exit 1
            fi
          else
            echo "Clean merge — no conflicts."
          fi

          # --- dependency-consistency gate + lock regen (LOCK-REGEN) ----------
          # The build installs --frozen-lockfile and electron-builder's
          # dependency traversal requires each workspace's DECLARED dep version
          # to be the one actually installed. A merge introduces two kinds of
          # drift that both kill the build ~25 min in (after a windows-arm64
          # runner spins up), not here:
          #   (1) a fork root `overrides` entry pins a package to a version
          #       DIFFERENT from the merged workspace pin (this is exactly how a
          #       stale {@mastra/core, mastracode} override broke every mastra
          #       bump: override forced 0.18.0, chat declared 0.18.1 ->
          #       electron-builder "production dependency not found 0.18.1"); and
          #   (2) the merge bumps a package.json dep but bun.lock still pins the
          #       old version (a stale frozen lock).
          # Catch (1) loud here (pure manifest analysis, platform-independent);
          # fix (2) by regenerating the lock with the build's exact bun version,
          # which ALSO fails loud if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin
          # is age-blocked. Both run in this 2-min job, so the windows-arm64
          # build never spins up on a broken tree. We deliberately do NOT run a
          # full `--frozen-lockfile` install here: this is a linux runner, so a
          # platform-specific install/native-build quirk would be a false abort,
          # and after a successful --lockfile-only the lock is consistent with
          # the manifests by construction (same bun version the build uses).
          # Any failure below hard-aborts with the baseline untouched.
          echo "::group::dependency-consistency gate"
          node scripts/check-override-consistency.mjs
          echo "::endgroup::"

          echo "::group::regenerate bun.lock to match merged manifests"
          export SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1
          LOCKLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/bun-lockfile.log"
          set +e
          bun install --lockfile-only > "$LOCKLOG" 2>&1
          rc=$?
          set -e
          cat "$LOCKLOG"
          if [ "$rc" -ne 0 ]; then
            if grep -q "blocked by minimum-release-age" "$LOCKLOG"; then
              echo "::error::Lock regen blocked by bunfig minimumReleaseAge — an upstream dep pin in $NEWTAG is <72h old. This is a timing race, not a breakage: the nightly will succeed once the version ages past the gate (re-run after it ages, or wait for tomorrow's schedule). Baseline left untouched."
            else
              echo "::error::bun install --lockfile-only failed (rc=$rc) after merging $NEWTAG — the merged manifests don't resolve. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            fi
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- (REFERR-REPAIR) run the build's ReferenceError gate HERE and let
          # Opus repair what it catches, so an import-dropping conflict
          # resolution self-heals tonight instead of failing the build and
          # costing a red night + manual recovery (desktop-v1.15.1
          # useChangesTab incident: the resolver took upstream's rewritten
          # import block, kept the fork's JSX, dropped Button/Tooltip/cn).
          # Repair is scoped HARD to cannot-find-name diagnostics; anything
          # still dirty after 2 attempts aborts exactly as before (the build
          # would have rejected it anyway — this just moves the gate earlier
          # and adds a bounded fix loop). --ignore-scripts keeps the linux
          # install pure-JS resolution (no native/postinstall platform quirks,
          # the reason this job deliberately avoids a full install elsewhere).
          echo "::group::(REFERR-REPAIR) ReferenceError gate + bounded AI repair"
          bun install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
          REFERRLOG="$RUNNER_TEMP/referr.log"
          run_referr_gate() {
            set +e
            node scripts/check-dangerous-diagnostics.mjs > "$REFERRLOG" 2>&1
            REFERR_RC=$?
            set -e
            cat "$REFERRLOG"
          }
          run_referr_gate
          # The repair agent must only EDIT the work tree — record the commit
          # state so an agent that disobeys "Do NOT commit" (advancing HEAD
          # and vacating the merge) is caught, not silently laundered into a
          # clean-looking tree.
          HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
          attempt=1
          while [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ] && [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            DIAGS=$(grep -F '(REFERR-GATE)' "$REFERRLOG" | head -40 || true)
            if [ -z "$DIAGS" ]; then
              # Gate failed WITHOUT naming diagnostics = the checker itself
              # broke (install/tsc infra). AI repair has nothing to act on.
              echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) gate failed without diagnostics — checker infrastructure problem, not repairable. Log above. Baseline untouched."
              exit 1
            fi
            npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
            REPAIR_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are inside a git repository holding a staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG}. The typecheck gate found cannot-find-name diagnostics — identifiers that would throw ReferenceError at runtime, almost always imports dropped during conflict resolution. Diagnostics: ${DIAGS} — Fix ONLY these diagnostics, and ONLY by ADDING code: add the missing import lines (match how the same symbols are imported elsewhere in this repo, e.g. @superset/ui/*) or restore the dropped declaration. NEVER delete, comment out, or rewrite existing code, JSX, or use-sites to silence a diagnostic — removing the code that uses the identifier is feature loss and is forbidden. Do NOT touch any file not named in the diagnostics. After editing each file run 'git add <file>'. Do NOT commit. Output nothing."
            echo "::group::claude referr-repair attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REPAIR_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            run_referr_gate
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          if [ "$REFERR_RC" -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) cannot-find-name diagnostics persist after repair attempts — aborting (the build would reject this tree). Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" != "$HEAD_BEFORE_REPAIR" ] || ! git rev-parse -q --verify MERGE_HEAD >/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::(REFERR-REPAIR) the repair run committed or destroyed the merge state — refusing to trust it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          # Stage any repair edits NOW: the semantic review below reads the
          # STAGED diff ('git diff --cached'); an unstaged repair would be
          # invisible to the review yet still swept into the final commit's
          # 'git add -A' — reviewed tree and shipped tree must be identical.
          git add -A
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- feature-marker gate (FEATURES.md) ------------------------------
          # Runs AFTER conflict resolution AND the REFERR repair loop, so it
          # judges the FINAL tree — a repair that "fixed" a diagnostic by
          # deleting fork code (forbidden, but prompts are not proofs) still
          # has to get past the marker grep here.
          echo "::group::feature-marker gate"
          # read the fenced ```markers block: lines are "<token><TAB><glob-root>"
          # Guard the guard: an empty/renamed block would pass VACUOUSLY (zero
          # markers checked == zero missing). Fail loud instead.
          MARKER_LINES=$(awk '/^```markers$/{f=1;next} /^```$/{f=0} f' FEATURES.md)
          if ! printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | grep -q '[^[:space:]]'; then
            echo "::error::FEATURES.md markers block is missing or empty — the marker gate would pass vacuously. Refusing to trust this merge."
            exit 1
          fi
          printf '%s\n' "$MARKER_LINES" | while IFS=$'\t' read -r tok root; do
            [ -n "$tok" ] || continue
            if grep -rqsF -- "$tok" "$root" 2>/dev/null; then
              echo "  OK   $tok ($root)"
            else
              echo "::error::feature marker MISSING after merge: '$tok' under $root"
              echo missing >> "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing"
            fi
          done
          echo "::endgroup::"
          if [ -f "$RUNNER_TEMP/marker-missing" ]; then
            echo "::error::A feature was dropped by the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi

          # --- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) AI review of the WHOLE merged delta -------
          # The marker gate only proves feature TOKENS still exist, and the
          # build's (REFERR-GATE) only catches cannot-find-name diagnostics. A
          # cleanly-merging upstream hunk can still SEMANTICALLY break a fork
          # feature with zero conflicts — v1.14.2 rebuilt the sidebar row
          # projection in a non-conflicting hunk and silently dropped the fork's
          # snooze/archive/completed/deleted fields: merged clean, compiled
          # clean, broke Snooze + Recycle Bin + archived masters. EVERY merge —
          # clean or conflicted — is therefore reviewed by Opus against
          # FEATURES.md before it may commit/build/publish. Fail CLOSED: no
          # parsable verdict (rate limit, malformed output) aborts rather than
          # ships an unreviewed merge; a BREAKAGE verdict hard-aborts with the
          # findings in the log (baseline untouched -> recovery loop).
          echo "::group::semantic feature review (Opus)"
          npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
          REVIEW_FILE="$RUNNER_TEMP/merge-review.txt"
          rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"
          # The reviewer must not modify the tree; snapshot the work-tree state
          # and refuse to continue if the review run changed ANYTHING.
          STATUS_BEFORE=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          REVIEW_PROMPT="IMPORTANT: Do NOT enter plan mode. You are reviewing an ALREADY-RESOLVED, staged, uncommitted merge of upstream superset tag ${NEWTAG} into our Windows-ARM64 fork. Your ONLY job is to decide whether the merged result SEMANTICALLY breaks any fork feature. Steps: (1) Read FEATURES.md — the fork feature manifest (feature descriptions + marker tokens). (2) Run 'git --no-pager diff --cached --name-only' to list every file the merge changed. (3) For each changed file that participates in a fork feature — it contains a FEATURES.md marker token, or it is in the blast radius of one (e.g. a projection/select/mapping that a feature's fields flow through, a component a feature's props pass through, a hook a feature's guard lives in) — read the staged diff ('git --no-pager diff --cached -- <file>') AND the surrounding file content, and verify the fork behaviour still works end-to-end: fork-added fields still projected/carried, fork branches and guards still reachable, fork props still passed, fork call sites still invoked. An upstream refactor that preserves behaviour is fine; flag only real breakage. (4) Write your verdict to the file at ${REVIEW_FILE} — the FIRST line must be exactly 'VERDICT: OK' or 'VERDICT: BREAKAGE'; for BREAKAGE add one line per finding: '<file> — <feature/marker> — <what broke>'. (5) If the file at ${WFDELTA_FILE} is non-empty, read it — it is upstream's CI/workflow delta we deliberately did NOT merge (.github/workflows is fork-owned). If it moves BUILD-RELEVANT logic into upstream CI (a codegen/generate step, a newly required env var, a packaging/signing step) that our fork's own build workflow would now silently miss, append one line per item after the verdict: 'CI-NOTE: <what upstream CI now does that the fork build may need>'. CI-NOTEs alone never change the verdict. Rules: do NOT edit ANY repository file; do NOT run git add / commit / checkout / merge / restore; only read files, run read-only git diff/log/show commands, and write the verdict file. Output nothing else."
          attempt=1
          while [ $attempt -le 2 ]; do
            echo "::group::claude review attempt $attempt"
            claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$REVIEW_PROMPT" || true
            echo "::endgroup::"
            if [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] && head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then break; fi
            attempt=$((attempt+1))
          done
          STATUS_AFTER=$(git status --porcelain=v1 | sha256sum)
          if [ "$STATUS_BEFORE" != "$STATUS_AFTER" ]; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) the review run modified the work tree — refusing to trust or commit it. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          if ! [ -s "$REVIEW_FILE" ] || ! head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -qE '^VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)$'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) no parsable review verdict for $NEWTAG — refusing to ship an UNREVIEWED merge (fail closed). Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "--- review verdict ---"
          cat "$REVIEW_FILE"
          if head -1 "$REVIEW_FILE" | grep -q 'BREAKAGE'; then
            echo "::error::(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus found fork-feature breakage in the $NEWTAG merge — rejecting. Findings above. Recovery: fix locally and re-baseline."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "::endgroup::"

          # --- record new baseline + commit the merge -------------------------
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) The release tag is derived SOLELY from
          # apps/desktop/package.json by the build — if the merged version
          # doesn't match the tag being merged, the build would delete and
          # rebuild the WRONG release (e.g. a bad package.json conflict
          # resolution keeping 1.14.3 while merging desktop-v1.15.0), then
          # advance the baseline past a version that no longer has a correct
          # release. Assert the invariant here instead.
          VERSION=$(node -p "require('./apps/desktop/package.json').version")
          if [ "desktop-v${VERSION}" != "$NEWTAG" ]; then
            echo "::error::merged apps/desktop/package.json version ($VERSION) does not match $NEWTAG — bad version resolution; refusing to commit. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
          # (WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) postcondition: the tree about to be committed
          # must contain ZERO workflow-dir changes — not from the merge, not
          # from the AI resolver, not from the review run. The restore step
          # above is the mechanism; this is the proof (a workflow delta would be
          # rejected by the push anyway — fail here, loud, with the baseline
          # untouched).
          if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows)" ]; then
            echo "::error::(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED) .github/workflows differs from HEAD after resolve/review — refusing to commit:"
            git status --porcelain=v1 --untracked-files=all -- .github/workflows
            exit 1
          fi
          printf '%s\n' "$NEWTAG" > .fork/upstream-baseline.txt
          git add -A
          git commit -m "merge upstream ${NEWTAG}" -m "Auto-merged by nightly-merge; conflicts resolved by Opus where present; all FEATURES.md markers verified present; merged delta passed the (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) Opus feature review."
          # (CANDIDATE-IDEMPOTENT-PUSH) The candidate is a disposable CI branch:
          # re-derived from main each run, consumed by `build`, deleted by
          # `advance` ON SUCCESS only. A FAILED build never reaches `advance`, so
          # it strands the candidate on the remote. The next night re-merges the
          # SAME upstream version into a NEW commit (AI conflict resolution is
          # non-deterministic AND main may have advanced), whose history diverges
          # from the stale branch — a plain push is then rejected non-fast-forward
          # and EVERY subsequent nightly deadlocks on that stale ref. Drop any
          # stale remote copy first so the push is always clean. This is pure CI
          # scratch (never a feature/history branch), so overwriting it is safe;
          # we delete-then-push rather than --force a real branch.
          # (REHEARSAL) Everything above — merge, resolver, repair, every
          # gate, even the local merge commit — ran for real; stop before
          # anything leaves this runner. This is the on-demand emulation of
          # the overnight run: after ANY change to this workflow, dispatch
          # rehearse=true (+ force_tag / rehearse_base to replay a past
          # night) and get tonight's answer today, with zero side effects.
          if [ "$REHEARSE" = "true" ]; then
            echo "should_build=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            {
              echo "## REHEARSAL PASSED — $NEWTAG"
              echo "Merge + conflict resolution + REFERR-REPAIR + marker gate + dependency gates + semantic review all green."
              echo "Merge commit (local only, discarded): $(git rev-parse HEAD)"
              echo "No candidate pushed, no build, no advance, baseline untouched."
            } | tee -a "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
            exit 0
          fi
          git push origin --delete "$CAND" 2>/dev/null || true
          git push origin "$CAND"

          echo "should_build=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "newtag=$NEWTAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=$VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "candidate=$CAND" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build + advance consume this immutable SHA, not
          # the mutable branch name — a replaced candidate can't be built or
          # advanced unnoticed.
          echo "sha=$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "Merged $NEWTAG -> candidate $CAND @ $(git rev-parse HEAD) (version $VERSION). Building next."

  build:
    needs: merge
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    uses: ./.github/workflows/build-arm64.yml
    with:
      # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) build the exact merge commit, immune to candidate-
      # branch mutation between jobs.
      ref: ${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}
      publish: true

  advance:
    needs: [merge, build]
    if: needs.merge.outputs.should_build == 'true'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - name: Fast-forward main to the verified candidate + clean up
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          CAND='${{ needs.merge.outputs.candidate }}'
          SHA='${{ needs.merge.outputs.sha }}'
          git fetch origin "$CAND"
          # (NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY) advance ONLY the commit that was gated + built.
          if [ "$(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)" != "$SHA" ]; then
            echo "::error::candidate $CAND moved (expected $SHA, found $(git rev-parse FETCH_HEAD)) — another run interfered; refusing to advance main."
            exit 1
          fi
          # The candidate is main + the merge commit, so this is a fast-forward.
          git push origin "$SHA:refs/heads/main"
          git push origin --delete "$CAND"
          echo "Baseline advanced to ${{ needs.merge.outputs.newtag }}; Release published by the build job."
