Reviewed commit c5401ffa3 (MERGE-ADAPT redesign) for publish-broken-build and wedge/deadlock bugs.

**VERDICT: FIX-FIRST** — safe for the current no-side-effect rehearsal, but two cheap guards should land before the first REAL (publishing) run.

**Findings**

1. **MEDIUM-HIGH — FEATURES.md is self-certifying: an AI pass can tamper the manifest the gates trust.** `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml:404` (`run_marker_gate` reads worktree `FEATURES.md`) + `:445` (reviewer reads the same worktree copy). The port/adapt/REFERR agents run with `--dangerously-skip-permissions` before these gates; nothing deterministic stops one from deleting a marker LINE from FEATURES.md to silence a feature it broke — the marker gate only fails vacuously on an *empty* block, and the semantic reviewer would be reading the tampered manifest. Scenario: adapt round can't port Kanban, deletes the `v2KanbanCards` marker line + the broken call sites → marker gate green, review green, unattended publish of a fork missing a feature (violates "whole feature set or fail loud"). The hole pre-existed for REFERR-REPAIR, but this commit triples AI-pass exposure. Fix is one deterministic assert next to the WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED postcondition (and ideally after each AI pass): `git diff HEAD --quiet -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md || abort` — legitimate merges can never change these files (upstream doesn't carry them).

2. **MEDIUM — restore-then-stage order re-dirties `.github/workflows`, converting agent junk into a hard-abort red night.** `:285-286` (port) and `:511-512` (adapt): `git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows` runs BEFORE `git add -A`. `--no-overlay` only removes paths present in the *index*+worktree; an **untracked** scratch file an agent dropped under `.github/workflows/` survives the restore, is then staged by `add -A`, and trips the `:555` postcondition → abort after ~40 min of AI work. Fail-closed, not a bad publish — but avoidable. Fix: swap to `git add -A` **then** `git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows` (once staged, the junk is in the index, so `--no-overlay` removes it from both index and worktree).

3. **LOW — adapt-round manifest edits bypass LOCK-REGEN.** `:316-331` regenerates `bun.lock` once, before the loop. A repair round (`:504`) that edits any `package.json` ships a stale lock → the windows-arm64 build fails ~25 min in ("production dependency not found" class). Fails loud, no bad publish; costs the night + a runner. Optional fix: after a repair round, if `git diff --cached --name-only | grep -q 'package.json$'`, re-run `bun install --lockfile-only` + re-stage.

**Verified clean** (concrete traps checked, no bugs): REFERR_RC/REFERRLOG/DIAGS scoping — `run_referr_gate` (`:349-355`) always sets `REFERR_RC` before both consumers, loop reuse at `:513/:519-529` reads fresh values; loop bounds exact (3 reviews / 2 repairs; `:484` aborts round-3 BREAKAGE; `:525` `round >= MAX-1` correctly forbids a dirty gate surviving into the last review; `:474-478` OK-verdict-over-pending-diags aborts); `ADAPT_DIAGS_FILE` lifecycle correct (`:444` init-clean, `:518` cleared only after gates re-run, `:529` re-written only when still dirty); fork-delta pathspec (`:264-270`) is index-vs-NEWTAG mid-merge with no unmerged entries possible at that point, exclusions effective under git's default wildmatch; `${FINDINGS}`/`${DIAGS}` interpolation is single-expansion (no re-eval of backticks/`$()` from reviewer text); an agent running `git stash` (vacating the merged tree while keeping HEAD/MERGE_HEAD) is caught by the `:544` version↔tag assert; reviewer mutations caught per-round by the porcelain hash (`:454/:463`); group/endgroup imbalance is cosmetic only — GitHub `::error::` annotations surface regardless of open groups; rehearsal path (`:580-589`) still exercises every new phase with zero side effects; no path waits or loops unbounded, so no wedge/deadlock.

Scratch file `tmp/review-nm.yml` removed. No files edited, no commits.</result>

input.message:
A Fable review of your MERGE-ADAPT commit (c5401ffa3) returned FIX-FIRST with 3 findings. Apply all 3 to .github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml. EDIT ONLY — no commit, no push. Keep LF endings, no inline ${{ }} in the run script, match existing comment style (short comment per fix referencing the reason).

1. MEDIUM-HIGH — FEATURES.md is self-certifying. The port/adapt/REFERR AI passes run with skip-permissions BEFORE the gates that read worktree FEATURES.md; an agent could delete a marker line to silence a feature it broke (marker gate only fails vacuously on an EMPTY block, and the semantic reviewer reads the same tampered manifest). Add a deterministic assert: `git diff HEAD --quiet -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md || { ::error + exit 1; }` — run it after EACH AI pass (proactive port pass, each adapt repair round, and also after the REFERR-REPAIR loop finishes its edits) plus once next to the WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED postcondition before the commit. Legitimate merges can never change these files (upstream doesn't carry them). Note: `git diff HEAD` covers both staged+unstaged vs HEAD, which is what we want. Factor as a small bash function (e.g. assert_manifest_untampered) to avoid repetition.

2. MEDIUM — restore-then-stage order re-dirties .github/workflows. At both the port pass and each adapt round: `git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows` currently runs BEFORE `git add -A`; an UNTRACKED scratch file an agent dropped under .github/workflows/ survives --no-overlay (it only removes index-present paths), gets staged by add -A, and trips the pre-commit postcondition → avoidable hard-abort after ~40 min of AI work. Swap the order at BOTH sites: `git add -A` FIRST, then `git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows` (once staged, the junk is in the index so --no-overlay removes it from index+worktree).

3. LOW — adapt-round manifest edits bypass LOCK-REGEN. After each adapt repair round's `git add -A`, if `git diff --cached --name-only | grep -qE '(^|/)package\.json$'` relative to... simplest correct check: compare staged tree vs the state before the adapt round — capture `git diff --name-only HEAD -- '**/package.json' 'package.json'` non-empty after staging, OR just: if any package.json is among the files the round staged, re-run the existing lock-regen block (`SUPERSET_POSTINSTALL_RUNNING=1 bun install --lockfile-only`, with the same minimum-release-age vs generic error handling as the LOCK-REGEN section — factor that block into a function run_lock_regen and call it from both sites), then `git add bun.lock`. Keep the failure messages' meaning intact.

After edits: re-validate exactly as before — `uv run --with pyyaml python` YAML parse + assert no '\r' + no '${{' in the run scalar, extract run script and `bash -n` it, `git diff --check`. Report a summary + validation results.

input.message:
One real bug in your finding-1 implementation — must fix before commit. AGENTS.md is NOT fork-only: upstream carries its own AGENTS.md (our file = fork-maintenance header + upstream's guide below the divider), and I verified upstream changed AGENTS.md by 13 lines between the current baseline and desktop-v1.16.0. A legitimate merge therefore makes `git diff HEAD -- AGENTS.md` non-empty and assert_manifest_untampered would false-abort the real v1.16.0 run right after the port pass. FEATURES.md is truly fork-owned (upstream never carries it).

Fix in .github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml, EDIT ONLY (no commit/push):
- Immediately after conflict resolution completes (right where assert_manifest_untampered is defined — i.e., BEFORE any AI pass that could tamper), capture a baseline snapshot: `MANIFEST_BASELINE=$(git diff HEAD -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md | sha256sum)`. At that point the diff contains only what the MERGE itself (plus conflict resolution) legitimately did to these files.
- Change assert_manifest_untampered to recompute the same sha and compare against MANIFEST_BASELINE; mismatch → same ::error + exit 1. Update the comment: legitimate upstream AGENTS.md hunks are in the baseline snapshot; any CHANGE after it means an AI pass edited the manifest (FEATURES.md can also never legitimately change post-resolution — upstream doesn't carry it). Keep guarding BOTH files — AGENTS.md matters because every claude CI invocation loads it via CLAUDE.md @include, so a tampered AGENTS.md is a prompt-injection persistence vector for subsequent passes.
- Keep all call sites as they are.
- Note the conflict-resolver runs before the snapshot: FEATURES.md cannot conflict (upstream lacks it), and a resolver edit to AGENTS.md on a genuine conflict is legitimate resolution — acceptable.

Everything else in your fix diff I've verified clean (stage-then-restore swap both sites, run_lock_regen factoring + package.json sha detection + git add bun.lock, assert placement incl. pre-commit). Re-validate after the change (pyyaml parse, no CR, no inline expressions, bash -n) and report.

cd "/c/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64" && git status --short && uv run --with pyyaml python - <<'EOF'
import yaml
src = open('.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml', encoding='utf-8').read()
assert '\r' not in src, 'CR FOUND'
doc = yaml.safe_load(src)
run = [s for s in doc['jobs']['merge']['steps'] if s.get('id')=='do'][0]['run']
assert '${{' not in run, 'inline expression found'
open('tmp/do-script.sh','w',encoding='utf-8',newline='\n').write(run)
print('yaml OK, no CR, no inline expr; chars:', len(run))
EOF
bash -n tmp/do-script.sh && echo "bash -n OK" && rm -f tmp/do-script.sh

cd "/c/Users/khair/Code Projects/fork-superset-windows-arm64" && git add .github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml && n=$(git cat-file -p ":.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml" | tr -cd '\r' | wc -c) && echo "staged CRs: $n" && cat > tmp/commit-msg.txt <<'EOF'
ci(nightly): harden MERGE-ADAPT per Fable review (MANIFEST-TAMPER-GUARD)

- assert_manifest_untampered: snapshot-based FEATURES.md/AGENTS.md
  tamper check after every AI pass + pre-commit. Snapshot taken after
  conflict resolution so upstream's legitimate AGENTS.md hunks (13
  lines in v1.16.0 alone) never false-abort; any later change means an
  AI pass edited its own acceptance criteria or persisted prompt text.
- Stage-then-restore at both AI sites so untracked agent junk under
  .github/workflows/ is dropped by --no-overlay instead of tripping
  the pre-commit postcondition ~40 min in.
- run_lock_regen factored; re-runs after any adapt round that changes
  a package.json, then stages bun.lock — stale-lock build deaths.

Implemented by Codex GPT-5.6 Sol; reviewed by Fable.
EOF
sed -i 's/\r$//' tmp/commit-msg.txt && git commit -F tmp/commit-msg.txt && rm -f tmp/commit-msg.txt && git push origin main && git log -1 --oneline

input.content:
---
name: merge-adapt-redesign
description: MERGE-ADAPT nightly redesign (2026-07-21) — proactive port pass + bounded review/adapt loop fixed the "clean-merge breaks fork-only callers" failure class; first proof = desktop-v1.16.0
metadata:
  type: project
---

**The failure class (now closed):** upstream refactors of APIs only fork files call merge with ZERO conflicts → conflict resolver never sees them; old (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) could only detect+abort. Every real refactor night died (v1.14.0 layout, v1.14.2 sidebar, v1.15.1 useChangesTab, v1.16.0 local-first-projects: v2Projects deleted, registerCloudAndLocal privatized, startHostEnsure removed → Kanban + multi-repo create dead).

**Redesign (commits c5401ffa3 + hardening 12a811303, shipped 2026-07-21):**
- Proactive `(MERGE-ADAPT)` port pass after conflict resolution: Opus gets the fork-delta file list (`git diff --cached refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG` minus workflows/lock/manifests) + both upstream tag refs, ports fork call sites across clean refactors BEFORE the gates.
- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) is now a bounded loop: max 3 fresh reviews / 2 repairs; each repair re-runs REFERR + marker gates; carried REFERR diags or OK-verdict-over-dirty-gate abort; round-3 BREAKAGE aborts as before.
- `assert_manifest_untampered` (MANIFEST-TAMPER-GUARD): sha256 SNAPSHOT of `git diff HEAD -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md` taken post-resolution, re-compared after every AI pass + pre-commit. MUST be snapshot-based: AGENTS.md is NOT fork-only (upstream carries it below the divider; v1.16.0 changed it 13 lines — a strict `diff --quiet` assert false-aborts legit nights). FEATURES.md alone is truly fork-owned.
- Stage-then-restore order at AI sites (`git add -A` THEN `checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows`) so untracked agent junk is dropped, not staged into the postcondition.
- `run_lock_regen` re-runs after any adapt round that touches a package.json (sha of `git diff HEAD -- package.json '**/package.json'` before/after), then stages bun.lock.

**Proof:** NIGHTLY-REHEARSE run 29847958945 (rehearse=true force_tag=desktop-v1.16.0) — port pass ALONE fixed the entire v1.16.0 breakage, review round 1 VERDICT: OK, zero repair rounds. Real run 29851535524 then went fully green unattended: merge+build+publish `desktop-v1.16.0` (Superset-1.16.0-arm64.exe, 2026-07-21T18:14Z) + baseline advanced. First-ever real-refactor night handled end-to-end by the pipeline.

**Process:** Sol (gpt-5.6-sol) implemented; Fable fork reviewed (3 findings, all landed); I caught the AGENTS.md false-abort in the fix itself. Test principle honored: no hand-patching of the specific breakage — the mechanism fixed it.

Related: [[project-workflow-fork-owned]], [[project_sidebar_v1142_regression_and_semantic_gate]]

content.text:
Installer download (~200MB) moved to background — will confirm when it lands in Downloads.

**Done — full summary:**

**Why every upstream release failed:** the resolver only saw *conflicted* files, but upstream refactors of APIs that only fork files call merge with **zero conflicts** — so breakage sailed through to the semantic gate, which could only detect and abort, never fix. v1.16.0 was the textbook case (deleted `v2Projects`, privatized/removed workspace exports → Kanban + multi-repo create dead).

**The rethink (shipped):**
- **Proactive port pass** — after conflict resolution, Opus diffs the old→new upstream trees against the fork-delta file set and ports fork call sites to upstream's new intent, exactly the mechanism you described.
- **Bounded review→adapt→re-verify loop** — max 3 fresh reviews / 2 repairs, every repair re-gated (REFERR + markers), fail-closed at every edge.
- **Hardening from review:** manifest-tamper snapshot guard (AI can't edit its own acceptance criteria; snapshot-based because upstream legitimately edits AGENTS.md — a strict check would have false-aborted tonight), stage-then-restore ordering, lock-regen after dep-touching repairs.

**Proof, no hand-patching:** rehearsal of the exact failed night → port pass alone fixed everything, review verdict OK round 1. Real run then went green unattended end-to-end: `desktop-v1.16.0` published 18:14 UTC, baseline advanced. First real-refactor night the pipeline has ever survived — and it fixed the breakage itself.

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   - User operates in BREVITY mode: drop filler/hedging/pleasantries, fragments + arrows OK, technical substance exact, code blocks/error messages/identifiers literal.
   - Main request (verbatim core): "use sol to do all implementation work you just review and co-ordinate. Now - can you tell me why the overnight run failed again - I've never had a single upstream work - the logic is supposed to be that the AI will merge the upstream changes into our fork whilst keeping intent (so it diffs the upstream old version and new version and figures out how to get it into our work) - literally never works has been broken from the start I need a complete rethink on how it works then you test it by basically running the workflow - nothing should come from you patching whatever was specifically broken on this change otherwise that defeats the objective"
   - Via AskUserQuestion the user chose: (a) "Proactive port pass + loop" architecture (proactive upstream-API-delta port pass in addition to the review-driven repair loop); (b) "yes dispatch a real session of what would run overnight so you can prove it will work" (real publishing run after green rehearsal); (c) for the shipped installer: "Just put it in my downloads folder. I will install it later."
   - Standing constraints (from user global/project instructions + memory): Sol (Codex gpt-5.6-sol) does implementation, Fable reviews; never install Superset on own initiative (closes the running session — user triggers install); work directly on main (no feature branches/PRs); all shipping via the full CI build (no hot patches); use AskUserQuestion for every decision/attention moment; CI's GITHUB_TOKEN can never push `.github/workflows/*` (local user-token push required); LF-only commits (verify staged blob has 0 CRs).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Vendored fork architecture: fork = source of truth, upstream tracked by `git merge` of `desktop-v*` tags; `.fork/upstream-baseline.txt` records baseline; deterministic AI-free build; AI only in nightly merge.
   - The failure class (now closed): upstream refactors of APIs that only fork-only files call merge with ZERO conflicts → conflict resolver (conflicted-files-only) never sees them → old `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)` could only detect+abort. Instances: v1.14.0 layout.tsx, v1.14.2 sidebar fields, v1.15.1 useChangesTab, v1.16.0 local-first-projects.
   - MERGE-ADAPT redesign: (1) proactive port pass — after conflict resolution, Opus receives fork-delta file list (`git diff --name-only --cached refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG` excluding `.github/workflows/**`, `bun.lock`, `FEATURES.md`, `AGENTS.md`, `.fork/**`) + both upstream tag refs (`refs/upstream-tags/$BASE`, `$NEWTAG`) and ports fork call sites across clean refactors; (2) `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)` became a bounded loop: max 3 fresh review rounds / 2 repair rounds; each repair re-runs REFERR + marker gates; carried REFERR diagnostics or an OK-verdict-over-dirty-gate abort; round-3 BREAKAGE aborts.
   - MANIFEST-TAMPER-GUARD: `assert_manifest_untampered` compares sha256 of `git diff HEAD -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md` against a `MANIFEST_BASELINE` snapshot taken immediately after conflict resolution. MUST be snapshot-based, not strict-empty: AGENTS.md is NOT fork-only (upstream carries its guide below the divider; upstream changed it 13 lines in v1.16.0) — strict `--quiet` assert would false-abort legit nights. FEATURES.md alone is truly fork-owned. AGENTS.md guarded because CI claude invocations load it via CLAUDE.md @include (prompt-injection persistence vector).
   - Stage-then-restore ordering: `git add -A` BEFORE `git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows` so untracked agent junk enters the index and `--no-overlay` drops it (restore-first leaves untracked files → trips the pre-commit postcondition).
   - `run_lock_regen` factored; re-run after any adapt round whose `git diff HEAD -- package.json '**/package.json' | sha256sum` changed, then `git add bun.lock`.
   - NIGHTLY-REHEARSE: `rehearse=true` (+ `force_tag`) runs merge/resolver/port/gates/review-loop for real, stops before push/build/advance — zero side effects.
   - Traps honored: 21k GitHub expression cap (no inline `${{ }}` in the big run script — inputs via `env:`), LF-only commits, `MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1` for `git show ref:path`, `gh --repo khairm/superset-windows-arm64` explicit.
   - Team pattern: Sol implements (edit-only, no commit/push), Fable fork reviews (inherits context), I coordinate/review/commit/push; persistent Monitor for CI runs; teammate messages ≠ user approval (no permission laundering).

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - `.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml` (was 535 lines; the central artifact, edited twice by Sol, committed by me as c5401ffa3 then 12a811303):
      - Structure: merge job (`id: do` single big bash step) → build (reusable build-arm64.yml on merged SHA, publish) → advance (fast-forward main, delete candidate).
      - New proactive port pass (after conflict resolution, before dep gates):
        ```bash
        MANIFEST_BASELINE=$(git diff HEAD -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md | sha256sum)
        assert_manifest_untampered() {
          MANIFEST_CURRENT=$(git diff HEAD -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md | sha256sum)
          if [ "$MANIFEST_CURRENT" != "$MANIFEST_BASELINE" ]; then
            echo "::error::AI editing changed FEATURES.md or AGENTS.md — refusing to trust a self-modified feature manifest. Baseline untouched."
            exit 1
          fi
        }
        # port pass: fork-delta list
        git diff --name-only --cached "refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG" -- \
          . ':(exclude).github/workflows/**' ':(exclude)bun.lock' \
          ':(exclude)FEATURES.md' ':(exclude)AGENTS.md' ':(exclude).fork/**' > "$FORK_DELTA_FILE"
        claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --model opus --effort high -p "$PORT_PROMPT" || true
        # guards: HEAD unchanged + MERGE_HEAD exists, else exit 1
        assert_manifest_untampered
        git add -A
        git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows
        ```
      - Review/adapt loop: `MAX_REVIEW_ROUNDS=3`; per round: `rm -f "$REVIEW_FILE"`, porcelain-hash reviewer-mutation check, 2 attempts for parsable `VERDICT: (OK|BREAKAGE)`; OK + non-empty `ADAPT_DIAGS_FILE` → abort; BREAKAGE on round 3 → abort; else repair round: findings = `tail -n +2 "$REVIEW_FILE"` (+ carried REFERR diags), ADAPT_PROMPT (never delete/stub features, edit only files needed, no workflows, `git add` each, no commit), HEAD/MERGE_HEAD guard, `assert_manifest_untampered`, `git add -A` then workflow restore, package.json sha before/after → `run_lock_regen` + `git add bun.lock` if changed, `run_referr_gate`, `run_marker_gate` (abort on marker loss), REFERR-dirty handling (`round >= MAX-1` → abort; else carry diags).
      - `run_marker_gate` and `run_lock_regen` factored into functions; `run_referr_gate`/`REFERRLOG`/`REFERR_RC` from the REFERR-REPAIR section reused in-loop.
      - Final pre-commit: version↔tag assert, workflow-dir postcondition, `assert_manifest_untampered`, baseline write, commit.
   - `FEATURES.md`: added marker row `(MERGE-ADAPT)\t.github` to the ```markers block + prose row "Nightly proactive port + bounded adaptation"; updated MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE prose to "fresh after every adaptation… final BREAKAGE verdict".
   - `AGENTS.md`: updated Nightly/Gates/AI-only/key-files sections describing port pass + bounded review/adapt loop (Sol's edit; user-visible in system reminder).
   - `C:\Users\khair\.claude\projects\C--Users-khair-Code-Projects-fork-superset-windows-arm64\memory\project_merge_adapt_redesign.md` (created) + MEMORY.md index line: full redesign record incl. the snapshot-assert lesson and proof runs.
   - Validation recipe used twice:
     ```bash
     uv run --with pyyaml python - <<'EOF'
     import yaml; src=open('.github/workflows/nightly-merge.yml',encoding='utf-8').read()
     assert '\r' not in src; doc=yaml.safe_load(src)
     run=[s for s in doc['jobs']['merge']['steps'] if s.get('id')=='do'][0]['run']
     assert '${{' not in run; open('tmp/do-script.sh','w',newline='\n').write(run)
     EOF
     bash -n tmp/do-script.sh
     ```

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Overnight failure root cause** (the investigation target): run 29810779837 aborted with `(MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE)` BREAKAGE on desktop-v1.16.0 — clean-merge deleted `v2Projects` (breaking `useKanbanData.ts:128`, `useKanbanActions.ts:656`, `PromoteCardDialog.tsx:75`) and privatized/deleted `registerCloudAndLocal`/`startHostEnsure` (breaking `multi-repo-create.ts`). Fixed structurally by MERGE-ADAPT (not by hand-patching, per explicit user constraint).
   - **node js-yaml validation failed** (`Cannot find module 'js-yaml'`): switched to `uv run --with pyyaml python` for YAML validation.
   - **Fork review FIX-FIRST findings on c5401ffa3**: (1) FEATURES.md self-certifying (AI could tamper manifest); (2) restore-then-stage order stages untracked workflow junk → false hard-abort; (3) adapt-round package.json edits bypass LOCK-REGEN → stale-lock build death. All three fixed by Sol per my instructions.
   - **Bug I caught in Sol's finding-1 fix**: strict `git diff HEAD --quiet -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md` assert would false-abort — AGENTS.md is upstream-carried and upstream changed it 13 lines in v1.16.0 (verified live via `git diff --stat` between tags). Fixed with the MANIFEST_BASELINE snapshot approach.
   - **User feedback shaping work**: architecture must include proactive port pass (not just reactive loop); real run must be dispatched to prove it; no patching the specific break; installer to Downloads instead of installing.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Diagnosed that "nightly never works" = detection-without-adaptation: every real upstream refactor night died because the resolver only saw conflicts and the gate could only abort.
   - Redesigned, implemented (via Sol), double-reviewed (Fable fork + my line-by-line), hardened, and PROVED the pipeline: rehearsal run 29847958945 (rehearse=true, force_tag=desktop-v1.16.0) — port pass alone fixed the entire v1.16.0 breakage, review round 1 `VERDICT: OK` ("Every fork touchpoint was ported to the `useHostProjects` host fan-out with behaviour preserved; no dangling references"), zero repair rounds. Real run 29851535524 then went fully green unattended: merge→build→advance all success; Release `desktop-v1.16.0` published (Superset-1.16.0-arm64.exe, 2026-07-21T18:14:36Z); origin/main baseline advanced to desktop-v1.16.0. First real-refactor night ever handled end-to-end.
   - Commits: c5401ffa3 (MERGE-ADAPT) and 12a811303 (MANIFEST-TAMPER-GUARD hardening), both pushed directly to main with verified 0-CR staged blobs.

6. All user messages:
   - BREVITY mode instruction block (output style rules: drop filler/hedging/pleasantries, fragments, arrows, exact technical substance; boundaries: code blocks unchanged, error messages quoted exact).
   - (Local command stdout, not requests: /model set Fable 5; /effort set high.)
   - Main request: "use sol to do all implementation work you just review and co-ordinate. Now - can you tell me why the overnight run failed again - I've never had a single upstream work - the logic is supposed to be that the AI will merge the upstream changes into our fork whilst keeping intent (so it diffs the upstream old version and new version and figures out how to get it into our work) - literally never works has been broken from the start I need a complete rethink on how it works then you test it by basically running the workflow - nothing should come from you patching whatever was specifically broken on this change otherwise that defeats the objective"
   - AskUserQuestion answers: Architecture = "Proactive port pass + loop"; Publish = "yes dispatch a real session of what would run overnight so you can prove it will work".
   - AskUserQuestion answer (install): "Just put it in my downloads folder. I will install it later."
   - (Teammate messages from sol-merge-adapt and system task-notifications are NOT user messages.)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - None. All four tracked tasks completed (#1 Sol implementation, #2 review+commit+push, #3 rehearsal, #4 real nightly run). Installer downloaded per user's instruction.

8. Current Work:
   Final wrap-up. After the real nightly run 29851535524 succeeded end-to-end and the user answered "Just put it in my downloads folder. I will install it later.", I ran `gh release download desktop-v1.16.0 --repo khairm/superset-windows-arm64 --pattern "Superset-1.16.0-arm64.exe" --dir "/c/Users/khair/Downloads" --clobber` (backgrounded due to 120s timeout, completed exit 0) and confirmed: `/c/Users/khair/Downloads/Superset-1.16.0-arm64.exe  461.5M`. Memory file `project_merge_adapt_redesign.md` + MEMORY.md index already saved.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Only remaining action: deliver the brief final confirmation to the user that `Superset-1.16.0-arm64.exe` (461.5M) is in `C:\Users\khair\Downloads\` ready for them to install whenever they choose (per their instruction "Just put it in my downloads folder. ---
name: merge-adapt-redesign
description: "MERGE-ADAPT nightly redesign (2026-07-21) — proactive port pass + bounded review/adapt loop fixed the \"clean-merge breaks fork-only callers\" failure class; first proof = desktop-v1.16.0"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: cc93a2e8-3460-4ccb-967b-8843019f99fb
  modified: 2026-07-21T18:16:30.498Z
---

**The failure class (now closed):** upstream refactors of APIs only fork files call merge with ZERO conflicts → conflict resolver never sees them; old (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) could only detect+abort. Every real refactor night died (v1.14.0 layout, v1.14.2 sidebar, v1.15.1 useChangesTab, v1.16.0 local-first-projects: v2Projects deleted, registerCloudAndLocal privatized, startHostEnsure removed → Kanban + multi-repo create dead).

**Redesign (commits c5401ffa3 + hardening 12a811303, shipped 2026-07-21):**
- Proactive `(MERGE-ADAPT)` port pass after conflict resolution: Opus gets the fork-delta file list (`git diff --cached refs/upstream-tags/$NEWTAG` minus workflows/lock/manifests) + both upstream tag refs, ports fork call sites across clean refactors BEFORE the gates.
- (MERGE-SEMANTIC-GATE) is now a bounded loop: max 3 fresh reviews / 2 repairs; each repair re-runs REFERR + marker gates; carried REFERR diags or OK-verdict-over-dirty-gate abort; round-3 BREAKAGE aborts as before.
- `assert_manifest_untampered` (MANIFEST-TAMPER-GUARD): sha256 SNAPSHOT of `git diff HEAD -- FEATURES.md AGENTS.md` taken post-resolution, re-compared after every AI pass + pre-commit. MUST be snapshot-based: AGENTS.md is NOT fork-only (upstream carries it below the divider; v1.16.0 changed it 13 lines — a strict `diff --quiet` assert false-aborts legit nights). FEATURES.md alone is truly fork-owned.
- Stage-then-restore order at AI sites (`git add -A` THEN `checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows`) so untracked agent junk is dropped, not staged into the postcondition.
- `run_lock_regen` re-runs after any adapt round that touches a package.json (sha of `git diff HEAD -- package.json '**/package.json'` before/after), then stages bun.lock.

**Proof:** NIGHTLY-REHEARSE run 29847958945 (rehearse=true force_tag=desktop-v1.16.0) — port pass ALONE fixed the entire v1.16.0 breakage, review round 1 VERDICT: OK, zero repair rounds. Real run 29851535524 then went fully green unattended: merge+build+publish `desktop-v1.16.0` (Superset-1.16.0-arm64.exe, 2026-07-21T18:14Z) + baseline advanced. First-ever real-refactor night handled end-to-end by the pipeline.

**Process:** Sol (gpt-5.6-sol) implemented; Fable fork reviewed (3 findings, all landed); I caught the AGENTS.md false-abort in the fix itself. Test principle honored: no hand-patching of the specific breakage — the mechanism fixed it.

Related: [[project-workflow-fork-owned]], [[project_sidebar_v1142_regression_and_semantic_gate]]

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- codex:codex-rescue: Proactively use when Claude Code is stuck, wants a second implementation or diagnosis pass, needs a deeper root-cause investigation, or should hand a substantial coding task to Codex through the shared runtime (Tools: Bash)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- feature-dev:code-architect: Designs feature architectures by analyzing existing codebase patterns and conventions, then providing comprehensive implementation blueprints with specific files to create/modify, component designs, data flows, and build sequences (Tools: Glob, Grep, LS, Read, NotebookRead, WebFetch, TodoWrite, WebSearch, KillShell, BashOutput)

- feature-dev:code-explorer: Deeply analyzes existing codebase features by tracing execution paths, mapping architecture layers, understanding patterns and abstractions, and documenting dependencies to inform new development (Tools: Glob, Grep, LS, Read, NotebookRead, WebFetch, TodoWrite, WebSearch, KillShell, BashOutput)

- feature-dev:code-reviewer: Reviews code for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, code quality issues, and adherence to project conventions, using confidence-based filtering to report only high-priority issues that truly matter (Tools: Glob, Grep, LS, Read, NotebookRead, WebFetch, TodoWrite, WebSearch, KillShell, BashOutput)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- project-structure-validator: Validates project structure against co-location and architecture patterns defined in AGENTS.md (Tools: All tools)

- sol: General-purpose subagent running on Codex GPT-5.6 Sol via the claude-all gateway. Use for heavy implementation, deep debugging, second-opinion reviews, or any Task the orchestrator wants executed by GPT-5.6 Sol instead of a Claude model. Accepts any prompt a general-purpose agent would. (Tools: All tools)
- spark: General-purpose subagent running on Codex GPT-5.3 Spark (fast) via the claude-all gateway. Use for quick mechanical subtasks, bulk edits, and speed-over-depth work. (Tools: All tools)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

- understand-anything:architecture-analyzer: Analyzes a codebase's file structure, summaries, and import relationships to identify
logical architectural layers and assign every file to exactly one layer. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:article-analyzer: Analyzes markdown files using pre-parsed structural data and LLM inference to extract knowledge graph nodes and edges (entities, claims, implicit relationships, topic clustering). (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:assemble-reviewer: Reviews the output of merge-batch-graphs.py for semantic issues the script
cannot catch. Recovers dropped nodes/edges and fills cross-batch gaps. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:domain-analyzer: Analyzes codebases to extract business domain knowledge — domains, business flows, and process steps. Produces a domain-graph.json that maps how business logic flows through the code. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:file-analyzer: Analyzes batches of source files to produce knowledge graph nodes and edges.
Extracts file structure, functions, classes, and relationships using a two-phase
approach: structural extraction script followed by LLM semantic analysis. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:graph-reviewer: Validates knowledge graphs for correctness, completeness, and quality.
Runs systematic checks and renders approval or rejection decisions. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:knowledge-graph-guide: Use this agent when users need help understanding, querying, or working
with an Understand-Anything knowledge graph. Guides users through graph
structure, node/edge relationships, layer architecture, tours, and
dashboard usage. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:project-scanner: Scans a codebase directory to produce a structured inventory of all project files,
detected languages, frameworks, import maps, and estimated complexity. (Tools: All tools)

- understand-anything:tour-builder: Designs guided learning tours through codebases, creating 5-15 pedagogical steps
that teach project architecture and key concepts in logical order. (Tools: All tools)

content.text:
Fork session spawned for AGENTS.md work — leaving that to it.

Sidebar chips request understood at a high level:
- 1 tab → upstream-style single dot next to workspace name, no chips.
- 2+ tabs → no dots on the name; per-tab chips (agent icon + dot overlaid), always expanded, labelled with actual tab names, in tab order.

Nailing down edge cases:

questions.question: Where does each chip's status dot go?

options.label: Badge on icon corner (Recommended)
options.description: Small dot overlapping the agent icon's top-right corner, like app notification badges. Label text stays clean.
options.preview:
●
[⚛]  big bang thread    [⚛]  MAR generation fix
(dot sits on icon's top-right corner)

options.label: Dot after tab name
options.description: Icon, then tab name, then dot at the end of the chip — like current tab strip.
options.preview: [⚛] big bang thread ●   [⚛] MAR generation fix ●

options.label: Dot replaces part of icon
options.description: Dot centered over/on the icon itself, icon dimmed behind it.

questions.question: Tab names can be long ("When generating a MAR also send to robot + clinical..."). How should chip labels handle length, and do chips wrap when there are many tabs?

options.label: Truncate ~18ch, wrap rows (Recommended)
options.description: Each label ellipsis-truncated to roughly 18 characters; chips flow onto multiple lines under the workspace row when they don't fit. Full name on tooltip.

options.label: One chip per line
options.description: Vertical list under the workspace row, one tab per line — more room for names, taller sidebar.

options.label: Truncate, single row
options.description: All chips on one row, heavy truncation, no wrapping.

questions.question: Tabs with no agent (plain shell / no Claude or Codex running) — show a chip for them?

options.label: Yes, chip with no dot (Recommended)
options.description: Every tab gets a chip in tab order (terminal icon for agent-less tabs), so chip row mirrors the tab strip exactly. Dot only appears if there's status.

options.label: Only agent tabs
options.description: Chips only for tabs with an agent; shell-only tabs invisible in sidebar. Order/count won't fully match tab strip.

options.label: Yes, with blue dot
options.description: Agent-less tabs get a chip with blue (shell activity) dot when a command is running.

questions.question: What happens when you click a chip?

options.label: Open workspace + that tab (Recommended)
options.description: Clicking a chip focuses the workspace and switches to that specific tab.

options.label: Open workspace only
options.description: Chip click behaves like clicking the workspace row — last active tab shown.

options.label: Not clickable
options.description: Chips are display-only; click passes through to the workspace row.

input.prompt:
Repo: C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64 (Superset desktop fork, Bun+Turbo monorepo, apps/desktop is the Electron app). Search breadth: very thorough.

Goal: map exactly how the sidebar renders (a) the status dot(s) next to a workspace/branch row name, and (b) the per-agent chips under the row (small pills with agent icon like Claude/Codex, currently expanded on hover). A prior fork change tagged CHIP-DOT-UNIFY unified these chips onto a shared per-source status primitive with an "open-pane gate". There is also a "workspace rollup" dot concept.

Find and report (file paths + line numbers + short excerpts):
1. The sidebar row component that renders the workspace/branch name and its status dot(s) — where the dot(s) next to the name come from. Note whether it currently renders ONE rollup dot or MULTIPLE dots (one per tab/agent) next to the name.
2. The agent-chip component(s): where chips are rendered, what data they receive (agent type, status), the hover-expand behavior (CSS/state that collapses to icon and expands on hover), and how CHIP-DOT-UNIFY wired them (search for "CHIP-DOT-UNIFY" marker comments).
3. Where tab metadata lives for a workspace: tab names (user-visible titles) and tab order — which store/query provides the list of tabs/panes for a workspace (v2 pane layout / v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout mentioned in fork notes), and whether tab NAME strings are accessible from the sidebar's data context.
4. The per-source status primitive: where per-terminal/per-source dot status is computed (files like resolveV2AgentStatusTransition, applySourceAxes, sources[key].status) and how a sidebar chip maps to a source key (pane/tab id?).
5. How clicking a sidebar row opens a workspace, and whether any existing code switches to a SPECIFIC tab/pane (so a chip click could focus its tab).
6. Any existing truncation/tooltip utilities used in the sidebar.

Also search FEATURES.md for the CHIP-DOT-UNIFY marker row and quote it.

Return a structured report: file:line anchors per item, data-flow summary (store → row → chip), and note anything that would make "chips labelled with tab names, in tab order, always expanded, dot as badge on icon corner, click focuses that tab" hard.

<output-file>C:\Users\khair\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\C--Users-khair-Code-Projects-fork-superset-windows-arm64\5200da76-d684-4a32-88c4-88931235e866\tasks\a749765af53010b26.output</output-file>

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Complete picture assembled. Report follows.

---

# Sidebar chip/dot rendering map

## Data-flow summary
```
v2-notifications store (sources[key].status + shellRunning/backgroundRunning axes)
  │
  ├─ useV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(id) ──────► workspaceStatus (ONE rollup dot) ─► WorkspaceIcon
  ├─ useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses(id) ───► terminalStatuses[] (N dots) ─────► ExpandedRow name line
  └─ useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents(id) ─► agents[] ─► WorkspaceDetails ─► AgentBadge chips
       (bindings identity) × (openTerminalIds gate) × (terminalStatuses status)

paneLayout (v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout) → openTerminalIds gate + terminalId source keys
```

---

## 1. Sidebar row + name dots — renders MULTIPLE dots (one per terminal), NOT one rollup

`DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx`
- L273-307: name line. `{name || branch}` at L286-293.
- **L299-305: MULTIPLE dots** — `terminalStatuses.map(({terminalId, status}) => <StatusIndicator key={terminalId} status={status}/>)`. One dot per active terminal, keyed by terminalId, inline right of the name.
- L45-51: prop doc — "One display status per active terminal… Rendered as a row of dots inline with the workspace name."
- Separate ROLLUP dot lives on the icon, not the name: `workspaceStatus` prop (L211/225) → `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceIcon`.

Wired in `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx`:
- L54: `workspaceStatus = useV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(id)` — rollup, drives icon dot (comment L52-53 "(AY) Display status merges the agent rollup… Drives the workspace-icon dot").
- L57: `terminalStatuses = useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses(id)` — per-terminal dot array.
- L257-258: both passed to the row.

So today: **icon = 1 rollup dot; name line = N per-terminal dots.**

---

## 2. Agent chips (CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)

Chip component: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx`
- Receives `{workspaceId, agent}` where `agent: DashboardSidebarRunningAgent` (L14-17).
- Icon: L31 `usePresetIcon(agent.agentId)` → Claude/Codex logo (fallback `LuBot`).
- **Dot as corner badge already exists**: L61-73 — icon in a `relative` span, `StatusIndicator` absolutely positioned `-top-0.5 -right-0.5` when `status !== "idle"`.
- **Hover-expand** is CSS-driven by `details-expanded:` variant (defined in globals.css, scoped by `group/item` / `group/details`), NOT React state:
  - Label span L74-82: `max-w-0 opacity-0` → `details-expanded:max-w-28 details-expanded:opacity-100`; uses `truncate`.
  - Facepile overlap L54-56: `-ml-1.5 first:ml-0` → `details-expanded:ml-1 details-expanded:pl-1 details-expanded:pr-1.5`.
- Click L33-40: navigates with `terminalId` + `focusRequestId` (see item 5).
- Tooltip L46/85-93: label + status + "Click to open agent".

Rendered by `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx`:
- L63-69: `runningAgents = useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents(workspaceId, workspaceAgentsRowEnabled)`.
- **L67-69: chips shown ONLY when `runningAgents.length > 1`** (`hasMultipleAgents`). Lone agent → no chips (comment L48-49).
- L102-108: `agents.map(agent => <DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge .../>)`.
- Feature-gated by `useWorkspaceAgentsRowEnabled()` (L57) — experiment toggle.
- Sort order: **by `startedAt`** (binding time), not tab order.

CHIP-DOT-UNIFY hook: `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts`
- L58-87. Combines three sources so chips can't disagree with dots:
  - Identity: `useTerminalAgentBindings` (agentId, startedAt).
  - **Liveness (open-pane gate)**: L63/73 `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` — `if (!openTerminalIds.has(binding.terminalId)) continue`.
  - **Status**: L64/79 `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` → `statusByTerminal.get(terminalId) ?? "idle"`.
- L75-76: **source key = `terminal:${binding.terminalId}`**, `source: {type:"terminal", id: terminalId}`.
- Marker comments L15-19, L42-57.

---

## 3. Tab metadata (names + order)

Types `apps/desktop/src/shared/tabs-types.ts`:
- `BaseTab` L251-257: `{ id, name, userTitle?, workspaceId, createdAt }` — **tab has user-visible `name` and `userTitle`**.
- `Pane` L140-162: `{ id, tabId, type, name, userTitle?, status?, … }`.
- `BaseTabsState` L262-268: `tabs: BaseTab[]` (**order = array order**), `panes`, `activeTabIds`, `focusedPaneIds`.

Store/query providing tabs to the sidebar: `store.ts` L663-696 `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds`:
- L672-679: live query over `collections.v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` (the SAME cross-workspace source the v2 workspace page hydrates from — comment L640-647).
- L684-694: iterates `paneLayout.tabs` → `tab.panes` → `getTerminalIdForPane(pane)`.
- **Tab NAME strings are reachable in this same query context but are currently DISCARDED** — only `terminalId`s are extracted, and the return is a `Set<string>` of ids (no tab name/order preserved). No existing sidebar hook surfaces tab names.

`getTerminalIdForPane` (store.ts L1086-1093): pane→terminalId via `pane.kind === "terminal"` + `pane.data.terminalId`.

---

## 4. Per-source status primitive

`store.ts`:
- L715-721: header — "(AY/BA) THE single display-status derivation. Every dot surface… derives from these two functions… the workspace rollup IS the fold of the per-source dots. Precedence red > yellow > blue > green."
- L759-785 `getSourceDisplayStatus(state, workspaceId, sourceKey)`: reads `state.sources[sourceKey].status` (agent permission/working), else terminal blue axes `state.shellRunningTerminals[terminalId]` / `state.backgroundRunningTerminals[terminalId]`.
- L751-752 `TERMINAL_SOURCE_PREFIX = "terminal:"` — canonical key `${source.type}:${source.id}`.
- L792-806 `selectV2WorkspaceNotificationStatus` = rollup fold, gated by `openTerminalIds`.
- L815-853 `selectV2WorkspaceTerminalDisplayKey` = per-terminal statuses, encoded as sorted `terminalId=status` string; consumed by `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` L855-883.
- `isClosedTerminalSource` L704-713: the open-pane gate.

Chip→source key mapping: chip carries `terminalId`; source key = `terminal:${terminalId}`. **Key is per-TERMINAL (pane), not per-tab.**

Note the DisplayStatus primitive (`StatusIndicator.tsx` L16-65) is a SEPARATE, wider union from `PaneStatus` in tabs-types.ts (adds `shell-running`/`background-running` blue; drops `failed` naming into `permission`). `resolveV2AgentStatusTransition`/`applySourceAxes` live in `.../V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts` (feed `sources[key].status`).

---

## 5. Row click opens workspace; chip click focuses a specific tab (already wired)

- Row open: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem` → `handleClick` (from `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions`), passed as `onClick` (L261).
- Chip click: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx` L33-40 `navigateToV2Workspace(workspaceId, navigate, { search: { terminalId: agent.terminalId, focusRequestId: crypto.randomUUID() } })`.
- Consumed by `useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts` L50-73 → `focusOrAddTerminalPane(store, terminalId)`.
- `focusTerminalPane.ts` L12-39: `findTerminalPaneLocation` scans `state.tabs`→`tab.panes` for the terminalId, then `setActiveTab(tabId)` + `setActivePane`. **So chip-click-focuses-that-tab already works.**
- Search param plumbing: `page.tsx` L24-26 validates `terminalId`/`focusRequestId`; threaded through `V2WorkspaceView.tsx` L50/102/143.

---

## 6. Truncation / tooltip utilities

- `OverflowFadeContainer` (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-container`) — WorkspaceDetails.tsx L1/L79, scroll+fade for the chip strip.
- Tailwind `truncate` — chip label AgentBadge.tsx L76; row name ExpandedRow.tsx L288.
- `Tooltip`/`TooltipContent`/`TooltipTrigger` (`@superset/ui/tooltip`) — AgentBadge L1/L46, ExpandedRow L188.
- `getStatusTooltip` — `StatusIndicator.tsx` L102-104 (status→label).

---

## What makes the target design hard

Target: "chips labelled with tab names, in tab order, always expanded, dot as badge on icon corner, click focuses that tab."

- **Labelled with tab names**: chips are per-TERMINAL (`source: terminal:<terminalId>`, hook L74-82), not per-tab. A tab can hold multiple terminal panes → several chips would share one tab name. Tab name isn't in the chip's data model (`DashboardSidebarRunningAgent` has `label` = agent name only). Would need a `terminalId → tab.name/userTitle` map built from `paneLayout.tabs` — data is reachable in `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` but currently thrown away (Set of ids only).
- **Tab order**: chips sorted by `startedAt` (hook L84), and `openTerminalIds` collapses to an unordered `Set` (L693-695). Tab order (`paneLayout.tabs[]` index) is discarded. Needs the ordering preserved through the pipeline.
- **Always expanded**: current expand is pure CSS `details-expanded:` hover variant (AgentBadge L54-82, WorkspaceDetails UNFOLD_WRAPPER L28-32). "Always expanded" = drop those variants / force expanded state — but the strip shares one animated layer with port pills (L110-157) tuned for collapse-at-rest; always-on labels change the rest-width budget and the facepile overlap logic.
- **Chips only when >1 agent**: WorkspaceDetails L67-69 hides chips for a lone agent — target likely wants them always → remove that gate.
- **Dot as corner badge**: already implemented (AgentBadge L67-72) — no change needed; only shows when `status !== "idle"`.
- **Click focuses tab**: already works (item 5). Chip currently targets terminalId (pane); focusing "the tab" already happens via `setActiveTab` in `focusTerminalPane`. If chips become tab-scoped, click target must switch from terminalId to a tabId/paneId.
- **Redundancy risk**: name line already renders N per-terminal dots (item 1, ExpandedRow L299-305). Tab-labelled chips with corner dots would duplicate that signal — likely want to drop the name-line dots if chips carry per-tab status.</result>

<result>Complete picture assembled. Report follows.

---

# Sidebar chip/dot rendering map

## Data-flow summary
```
v2-notifications store (sources[key].status + shellRunning/backgroundRunning axes)
  │
  ├─ useV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(id) ──────► workspaceStatus (ONE rollup dot) ─► WorkspaceIcon
  ├─ useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses(id) ───► terminalStatuses[] (N dots) ─────► ExpandedRow name line
  └─ useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents(id) ─► agents[] ─► WorkspaceDetails ─► AgentBadge chips
       (bindings identity) × (openTerminalIds gate) × (terminalStatuses status)

paneLayout (v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout) → openTerminalIds gate + terminalId source keys
```

---

## 1. Sidebar row + name dots — renders MULTIPLE dots (one per terminal), NOT one rollup

`DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx`
- L273-307: name line. `{name || branch}` at L286-293.
- **L299-305: MULTIPLE dots** — `terminalStatuses.map(({terminalId, status}) => <StatusIndicator key={terminalId} status={status}/>)`. One dot per active terminal, keyed by terminalId, inline right of the name.
- L45-51: prop doc — "One display status per active terminal… Rendered as a row of dots inline with the workspace name."
- Separate ROLLUP dot lives on the icon, not the name: `workspaceStatus` prop (L211/225) → `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceIcon`.

Wired in `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx`:
- L54: `workspaceStatus = useV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(id)` — rollup, drives icon dot (comment L52-53 "(AY) Display status merges the agent rollup… Drives the workspace-icon dot").
- L57: `terminalStatuses = useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses(id)` — per-terminal dot array.
- L257-258: both passed to the row.

So today: **icon = 1 rollup dot; name line = N per-terminal dots.**

---

## 2. Agent chips (CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)

Chip component: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx`
- Receives `{workspaceId, agent}` where `agent: DashboardSidebarRunningAgent` (L14-17).
- Icon: L31 `usePresetIcon(agent.agentId)` → Claude/Codex logo (fallback `LuBot`).
- **Dot as corner badge already exists**: L61-73 — icon in a `relative` span, `StatusIndicator` absolutely positioned `-top-0.5 -right-0.5` when `status !== "idle"`.
- **Hover-expand** is CSS-driven by `details-expanded:` variant (defined in globals.css, scoped by `group/item` / `group/details`), NOT React state:
  - Label span L74-82: `max-w-0 opacity-0` → `details-expanded:max-w-28 details-expanded:opacity-100`; uses `truncate`.
  - Facepile overlap L54-56: `-ml-1.5 first:ml-0` → `details-expanded:ml-1 details-expanded:pl-1 details-expanded:pr-1.5`.
- Click L33-40: navigates with `terminalId` + `focusRequestId` (see item 5).
- Tooltip L46/85-93: label + status + "Click to open agent".

Rendered by `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx`:
- L63-69: `runningAgents = useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents(workspaceId, workspaceAgentsRowEnabled)`.
- **L67-69: chips shown ONLY when `runningAgents.length > 1`** (`hasMultipleAgents`). Lone agent → no chips (comment L48-49).
- L102-108: `agents.map(agent => <DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge .../>)`.
- Feature-gated by `useWorkspaceAgentsRowEnabled()` (L57) — experiment toggle.
- Sort order: **by `startedAt`** (binding time), not tab order.

CHIP-DOT-UNIFY hook: `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts`
- L58-87. Combines three sources so chips can't disagree with dots:
  - Identity: `useTerminalAgentBindings` (agentId, startedAt).
  - **Liveness (open-pane gate)**: L63/73 `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` — `if (!openTerminalIds.has(binding.terminalId)) continue`.
  - **Status**: L64/79 `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` → `statusByTerminal.get(terminalId) ?? "idle"`.
- L75-76: **source key = `terminal:${binding.terminalId}`**, `source: {type:"terminal", id: terminalId}`.
- Marker comments L15-19, L42-57.

---

## 3. Tab metadata (names + order)

Types `apps/desktop/src/shared/tabs-types.ts`:
- `BaseTab` L251-257: `{ id, name, userTitle?, workspaceId, createdAt }` — **tab has user-visible `name` and `userTitle`**.
- `Pane` L140-162: `{ id, tabId, type, name, userTitle?, status?, … }`.
- `BaseTabsState` L262-268: `tabs: BaseTab[]` (**order = array order**), `panes`, `activeTabIds`, `focusedPaneIds`.

Store/query providing tabs to the sidebar: `store.ts` L663-696 `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds`:
- L672-679: live query over `collections.v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` (the SAME cross-workspace source the v2 workspace page hydrates from — comment L640-647).
- L684-694: iterates `paneLayout.tabs` → `tab.panes` → `getTerminalIdForPane(pane)`.
- **Tab NAME strings are reachable in this same query context but are currently DISCARDED** — only `terminalId`s are extracted, and the return is a `Set<string>` of ids (no tab name/order preserved). No existing sidebar hook surfaces tab names.

`getTerminalIdForPane` (store.ts L1086-1093): pane→terminalId via `pane.kind === "terminal"` + `pane.data.terminalId`.

---

## 4. Per-source status primitive

`store.ts`:
- L715-721: header — "(AY/BA) THE single display-status derivation. Every dot surface… derives from these two functions… the workspace rollup IS the fold of the per-source dots. Precedence red > yellow > blue > green."
- L759-785 `getSourceDisplayStatus(state, workspaceId, sourceKey)`: reads `state.sources[sourceKey].status` (agent permission/working), else terminal blue axes `state.shellRunningTerminals[terminalId]` / `state.backgroundRunningTerminals[terminalId]`.
- L751-752 `TERMINAL_SOURCE_PREFIX = "terminal:"` — canonical key `${source.type}:${source.id}`.
- L792-806 `selectV2WorkspaceNotificationStatus` = rollup fold, gated by `openTerminalIds`.
- L815-853 `selectV2WorkspaceTerminalDisplayKey` = per-terminal statuses, encoded as sorted `terminalId=status` string; consumed by `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` L855-883.
- `isClosedTerminalSource` L704-713: the open-pane gate.

Chip→source key mapping: chip carries `terminalId`; source key = `terminal:${terminalId}`. **Key is per-TERMINAL (pane), not per-tab.**

Note the DisplayStatus primitive (`StatusIndicator.tsx` L16-65) is a SEPARATE, wider union from `PaneStatus` in tabs-types.ts (adds `shell-running`/`background-running` blue; drops `failed` naming into `permission`). `resolveV2AgentStatusTransition`/`applySourceAxes` live in `.../V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts` (feed `sources[key].status`).

---

## 5. Row click opens workspace; chip click focuses a specific tab (already wired)

- Row open: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem` → `handleClick` (from `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions`), passed as `onClick` (L261).
- Chip click: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx` L33-40 `navigateToV2Workspace(workspaceId, navigate, { search: { terminalId: agent.terminalId, focusRequestId: crypto.randomUUID() } })`.
- Consumed by `useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts` L50-73 → `focusOrAddTerminalPane(store, terminalId)`.
- `focusTerminalPane.ts` L12-39: `findTerminalPaneLocation` scans `state.tabs`→`tab.panes` for the terminalId, then `setActiveTab(tabId)` + `setActivePane`. **So chip-click-focuses-that-tab already works.**
- Search param plumbing: `page.tsx` L24-26 validates `terminalId`/`focusRequestId`; threaded through `V2WorkspaceView.tsx` L50/102/143.

---

## 6. Truncation / tooltip utilities

- `OverflowFadeContainer` (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-container`) — WorkspaceDetails.tsx L1/L79, scroll+fade for the chip strip.
- Tailwind `truncate` — chip label AgentBadge.tsx L76; row name ExpandedRow.tsx L288.
- `Tooltip`/`TooltipContent`/`TooltipTrigger` (`@superset/ui/tooltip`) — AgentBadge L1/L46, ExpandedRow L188.
- `getStatusTooltip` — `StatusIndicator.tsx` L102-104 (status→label).

---

## What makes the target design hard

Target: "chips labelled with tab names, in tab order, always expanded, dot as badge on icon corner, click focuses that tab."

- **Labelled with tab names**: chips are per-TERMINAL (`source: terminal:<terminalId>`, hook L74-82), not per-tab. A tab can hold multiple terminal panes → several chips would share one tab name. Tab name isn't in the chip's data model (`DashboardSidebarRunningAgent` has `label` = agent name only). Would need a `terminalId → tab.name/userTitle` map built from `paneLayout.tabs` — data is reachable in `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` but currently thrown away (Set of ids only).
- **Tab order**: chips sorted by `startedAt` (hook L84), and `openTerminalIds` collapses to an unordered `Set` (L693-695). Tab order (`paneLayout.tabs[]` index) is discarded. Needs the ordering preserved through the pipeline.
- **Always expanded**: current expand is pure CSS `details-expanded:` hover variant (AgentBadge L54-82, WorkspaceDetails UNFOLD_WRAPPER L28-32). "Always expanded" = drop those variants / force expanded state — but the strip shares one animated layer with port pills (L110-157) tuned for collapse-at-rest; always-on labels change the rest-width budget and the facepile overlap logic.
- **Chips only when >1 agent**: WorkspaceDetails L67-69 hides chips for a lone agent — target likely wants them always → remove that gate.
- **Dot as corner badge**: already implemented (AgentBadge L67-72) — no change needed; only shows when `status !== "idle"`.
- **Click focuses tab**: already works (item 5). Chip currently targets terminalId (pane); focusing "the tab" already happens via `setActiveTab` in `focusTerminalPane`. If chips become tab-scoped, click target must switch from terminalId to a tabId/paneId.
- **Redundancy risk**: name line already renders N per-terminal dots (item 1, ExpandedRow L299-305). Tab-labelled chips with corner dots would duplicate that signal — likely want to drop the name-line dots if chips carry per-tab status.</result>

`DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx`
- L273-307: name line. `{name || branch}` at L286-293.
- **L299-305: MULTIPLE dots** — `terminalStatuses.map(({terminalId, status}) => <StatusIndicator key={terminalId} status={status}/>)`. One dot per active terminal, keyed by terminalId, inline right of the name.
- L45-51: prop doc — "One display status per active terminal… Rendered as a row of dots inline with the workspace name."
- Separate ROLLUP dot lives on the icon, not the name: `workspaceStatus` prop (L211/225) → `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceIcon`.

Wired in `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx`:
- L54: `workspaceStatus = useV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(id)` — rollup, drives icon dot (comment L52-53 "(AY) Display status merges the agent rollup… Drives the workspace-icon dot").
- L57: `terminalStatuses = useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses(id)` — per-terminal dot array.
- L257-258: both passed to the row.

So today: **icon = 1 rollup dot; name line = N per-terminal dots.**

---

## 2. Agent chips (CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)

Chip component: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx`
- Receives `{workspaceId, agent}` where `agent: DashboardSidebarRunningAgent` (L14-17).
- Icon: L31 `usePresetIcon(agent.agentId)` → Claude/Codex logo (fallback `LuBot`).
- **Dot as corner badge already exists**: L61-73 — icon in a `relative` span, `StatusIndicator` absolutely positioned `-top-0.5 -right-0.5` when `status !== "idle"`.
- **Hover-expand** is CSS-driven by `details-expanded:` variant (defined in globals.css, scoped by `group/item` / `group/details`), NOT React state:
  - Label span L74-82: `max-w-0 opacity-0` → `details-expanded:max-w-28 details-expanded:opacity-100`; uses `truncate`.
  - Facepile overlap L54-56: `-ml-1.5 first:ml-0` → `details-expanded:ml-1 details-expanded:pl-1 details-expanded:pr-1.5`.
- Click L33-40: navigates with `terminalId` + `focusRequestId` (see item 5).
- Tooltip L46/85-93: label + status + "Click to open agent".

Rendered by `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx`:
- L63-69: `runningAgents = useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents(workspaceId, workspaceAgentsRowEnabled)`.
- **L67-69: chips shown ONLY when `runningAgents.length > 1`** (`hasMultipleAgents`). Lone agent → no chips (comment L48-49).
- L102-108: `agents.map(agent => <DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge .../>)`.
- Feature-gated by `useWorkspaceAgentsRowEnabled()` (L57) — experiment toggle.
- Sort order: **by `startedAt`** (binding time), not tab order.

CHIP-DOT-UNIFY hook: `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts`
- L58-87. Combines three sources so chips can't disagree with dots:
  - Identity: `useTerminalAgentBindings` (agentId, startedAt).
  - **Liveness (open-pane gate)**: L63/73 `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` — `if (!openTerminalIds.has(binding.terminalId)) continue`.
  - **Status**: L64/79 `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` → `statusByTerminal.get(terminalId) ?? "idle"`.
- L75-76: **source key = `terminal:${binding.terminalId}`**, `source: {type:"terminal", id: terminalId}`.
- Marker comments L15-19, L42-57.

---

## 3. Tab metadata (names + order)

Types `apps/desktop/src/shared/tabs-types.ts`:
- `BaseTab` L251-257: `{ id, name, userTitle?, workspaceId, createdAt }` — **tab has user-visible `name` and `userTitle`**.
- `Pane` L140-162: `{ id, tabId, type, name, userTitle?, status?, … }`.
- `BaseTabsState` L262-268: `tabs: BaseTab[]` (**order = array order**), `panes`, `activeTabIds`, `focusedPaneIds`.

Store/query providing tabs to the sidebar: `store.ts` L663-696 `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds`:
- L672-679: live query over `collections.v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` (the SAME cross-workspace source the v2 workspace page hydrates from — comment L640-647).
- L684-694: iterates `paneLayout.tabs` → `tab.panes` → `getTerminalIdForPane(pane)`.
- **Tab NAME strings are reachable in this same query context but are currently DISCARDED** — only `terminalId`s are extracted, and the return is a `Set<string>` of ids (no tab name/order preserved). No existing sidebar hook surfaces tab names.

`getTerminalIdForPane` (store.ts L1086-1093): pane→terminalId via `pane.kind === "terminal"` + `pane.data.terminalId`.

---

## 4. Per-source status primitive

`store.ts`:
- L715-721: header — "(AY/BA) THE single display-status derivation. Every dot surface… derives from these two functions… the workspace rollup IS the fold of the per-source dots. Precedence red > yellow > blue > green."
- L759-785 `getSourceDisplayStatus(state, workspaceId, sourceKey)`: reads `state.sources[sourceKey].status` (agent permission/working), else terminal blue axes `state.shellRunningTerminals[terminalId]` / `state.backgroundRunningTerminals[terminalId]`.
- L751-752 `TERMINAL_SOURCE_PREFIX = "terminal:"` — canonical key `${source.type}:${source.id}`.
- L792-806 `selectV2WorkspaceNotificationStatus` = rollup fold, gated by `openTerminalIds`.
- L815-853 `selectV2WorkspaceTerminalDisplayKey` = per-terminal statuses, encoded as sorted `terminalId=status` string; consumed by `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` L855-883.
- `isClosedTerminalSource` L704-713: the open-pane gate.

Chip→source key mapping: chip carries `terminalId`; source key = `terminal:${terminalId}`. **Key is per-TERMINAL (pane), not per-tab.**

Note the DisplayStatus primitive (`StatusIndicator.tsx` L16-65) is a SEPARATE, wider union from `PaneStatus` in tabs-types.ts (adds `shell-running`/`background-running` blue; drops `failed` naming into `permission`). `resolveV2AgentStatusTransition`/`applySourceAxes` live in `.../V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts` (feed `sources[key].status`).

---

## 5. Row click opens workspace; chip click focuses a specific tab (already wired)

- Row open: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem` → `handleClick` (from `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions`), passed as `onClick` (L261).
- Chip click: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx` L33-40 `navigateToV2Workspace(workspaceId, navigate, { search: { terminalId: agent.terminalId, focusRequestId: crypto.randomUUID() } })`.
- Consumed by `useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts` L50-73 → `focusOrAddTerminalPane(store, terminalId)`.
- `focusTerminalPane.ts` L12-39: `findTerminalPaneLocation` scans `state.tabs`→`tab.panes` for the terminalId, then `setActiveTab(tabId)` + `setActivePane`. **So chip-click-focuses-that-tab already works.**
- Search param plumbing: `page.tsx` L24-26 validates `terminalId`/`focusRequestId`; threaded through `V2WorkspaceView.tsx` L50/102/143.

---

## 6. Truncation / tooltip utilities

- `OverflowFadeContainer` (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-container`) — WorkspaceDetails.tsx L1/L79, scroll+fade for the chip strip.
- Tailwind `truncate` — chip label AgentBadge.tsx L76; row name ExpandedRow.tsx L288.
- `Tooltip`/`TooltipContent`/`TooltipTrigger` (`@superset/ui/tooltip`) — AgentBadge L1/L46, ExpandedRow L188.
- `getStatusTooltip` — `StatusIndicator.tsx` L102-104 (status→label).

---

## What makes the target design hard

Target: "chips labelled with tab names, in tab order, always expanded, dot as badge on icon corner, click focuses that tab."

- **Labelled with tab names**: chips are per-TERMINAL (`source: terminal:<terminalId>`, hook L74-82), not per-tab. A tab can hold multiple terminal panes → several chips would share one tab name. Tab name isn't in the chip's data model (`DashboardSidebarRunningAgent` has `label` = agent name only). Would need a `terminalId → tab.name/userTitle` map built from `paneLayout.tabs` — data is reachable in `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` but currently thrown away (Set of ids only).
- **Tab order**: chips sorted by `startedAt` (hook L84), and `openTerminalIds` collapses to an unordered `Set` (L693-695). Tab order (`paneLayout.tabs[]` index) is discarded. Needs the ordering preserved through the pipeline.
- **Always expanded**: current expand is pure CSS `details-expanded:` hover variant (AgentBadge L54-82, WorkspaceDetails UNFOLD_WRAPPER L28-32). "Always expanded" = drop those variants / force expanded state — but the strip shares one animated layer with port pills (L110-157) tuned for collapse-at-rest; always-on labels change the rest-width budget and the facepile overlap logic.
- **Chips only when >1 agent**: WorkspaceDetails L67-69 hides chips for a lone agent — target likely wants them always → remove that gate.
- **Dot as corner badge**: already implemented (AgentBadge L67-72) — no change needed; only shows when `status !== "idle"`.
- **Click focuses tab**: already works (item 5). Chip currently targets terminalId (pane); focusing "the tab" already happens via `setActiveTab` in `focusTerminalPane`. If chips become tab-scoped, click target must switch from terminalId to a tabId/paneId.
- **Redundancy risk**: name line already renders N per-terminal dots (item 1, ExpandedRow L299-305). Tab-labelled chips with corner dots would duplicate that signal — likely want to drop the name-line dots if chips carry per-tab status.</result>
