# Sidebar Workspace Rows & Agent Chips — Rendering Layer

## 1. Workspace/branch row — dots next to the name

Row component: `DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx`
`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/DashboardSidebar/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/components/DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow/DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx`

The row renders TWO distinct dot surfaces — NOT one rollup:

**a) One rollup dot** — on the workspace ICON overlay (left of name), not inline with text. Passed as `workspaceStatus` → `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceIcon` (line 211/225). Source: parent `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx:54` `const workspaceStatus = useV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(id);` (highest-priority merged status). Rendered by `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceIcon.tsx:124-127` — single `<StatusIndicator status={overlayStatus} />`, positioned `-top-0.5 -right-0.5`. Note gate: `working` is suppressed on the overlay (shown as `AsciiSpinner` instead), `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceIcon.tsx:74-75`.

**b) MULTIPLE per-terminal dots** — inline, right of the name. `DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx:299-305`:
```tsx
{terminalStatuses.length > 0 && (
    <div className="flex shrink-0 items-center gap-1">
        {terminalStatuses.map(({ terminalId, status }) => (
            <StatusIndicator key={terminalId} status={status} />
        ))}
    </div>
)}
```
One `StatusIndicator` per active terminal, keyed by `terminalId`. Prop doc, lines 46-51: "One display status per active terminal... Rendered as a row of dots inline with the workspace name."

Dot list source: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx:57` `const terminalStatuses = useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses(id);` → threaded to the row at line 258. That hook (`store.ts:855`) is the shared `(AY)/(DOT-AXES)` per-source primitive, gated by `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds`.

So: **one rollup dot on the icon + N inline dots (one per open terminal) next to the name.** The agent chips (section 2) are a third, separate surface rendered below the row.

## 2. Agent-chip component + hover-expand

Chip: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx`
`.../DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx`

Container/strip: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx` (same tree). Rendered as child of the item, `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx:286`. Strip only appears when >1 agent: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx:67-69`:
```tsx
const hasMultipleAgents = runningAgents.length > 1;
const showAgentChips = workspaceAgentsRowEnabled && hasMultipleAgents;
const agents = showAgentChips ? runningAgents : [];
```

**Markup (chip):** `<button>` → inner `<span>` face (size-3) holding icon + status dot, then a label `<span>`.
- Icon: `usePresetIcon(agent.agentId)` `<img>`, fallback `<LuBot>` (`AgentBadge.tsx:61-66`).
- Status dot: `<StatusIndicator status={agent.status}>` positioned `absolute -top-0.5 -right-0.5`, hidden when `idle` (line 67-72).
- Label: `{agent.label}` (line 74-82).

**Data each chip receives** — `DashboardSidebarRunningAgent` (defined `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:22-36`): `sourceKey`, `source` (V2NotificationSource), `terminalId`, `agentId` (BuiltinAgentId), `status` (`DisplayStatus | "idle"`), `startedAt`, `label`. Click → `navigateToV2Workspace(workspaceId, ..., { search: { terminalId, focusRequestId } })` (AgentBadge.tsx:33-40).

**Hover-expand (collapse to icon-only → expand):** driven by the `details-expanded` Tailwind custom variant, defined `apps/desktop/src/renderer/globals.css:15-18`:
```css
@custom-variant details-expanded (&:is(
    :where(.group\/item:hover, .group\/item:focus-within) *,
    .group\details:has([data-state="open"]) *
));
```
Triggered when the workspace item (`group/item`) is hovered/focus-within, or a strip dropdown (`group/details`) is open. The `group/details` scope is set on the container (`DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx:83`).

Collapse/expand state lives in the chip's classes:
- Chip button collapsed = facepile circle with negative overlap margin `-ml-1.5 first:ml-0`; expanded adds padding/margin: `details-expanded:ml-1 details-expanded:pl-1 details-expanded:pr-1.5 ... details-expanded:first:ml-0` (`AgentBadge.tsx:52-56`).
- Label collapsed to zero-width/invisible, expands on hover — `AgentBadge.tsx:74-79`:
```tsx
"max-w-0 truncate opacity-0",
"transition-[max-width,margin,opacity] duration-500 ease-out motion-reduce:transition-none",
"details-expanded:ml-1 details-expanded:max-w-28 details-expanded:opacity-100 details-expanded:duration-200",
```
So at rest = icon+dot only (label `max-w-0 opacity-0`); on hover = label unfolds to `max-w-28`. Ports pill mirrors this via `UNFOLD_WRAPPER` (`WorkspaceDetails.tsx:28-32`). Strip comment lines 38-49 describe the facepile→pill morph.

## 3. CHIP-DOT-UNIFY wiring

Marker `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` appears in:
- `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:15, 42` (main wiring)
- `store.ts:665, 857` (hook `enabled:false` degradation notes)

The change replaced upstream's independent chip status pipeline with the fork's shared dot primitives (`useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:42-57`):
- **STATUS** now comes from `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` (the shared `(AY)/(DOT-AXES)` per-source `DisplayStatus`) — not the host binding's raw `lastEventType` 4-case map that read fork events (`SubagentActive`, `BackgroundRunning`) as idle. `RunningAgentStatus = DisplayStatus | "idle"` (line 20), so a chip's dot uses the exact same `StatusIndicator` primitive as the tab/pane/rollup dots.
- **LIVENESS = the open-pane gate.** `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` (store.ts:663) derives open terminals from the persisted `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout`, NOT `terminal_sessions.status` (which stays `active` forever for a closed-tab daemon pty and trapped stale permission-red chips). The gate is applied in the merge loop, `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:72-73`:
```ts
for (const binding of bindings.values()) {
    if (!openTerminalIds.has(binding.terminalId)) continue;
```
A closed tab's chip is unrepresentable. Host binding still supplies identity only (`agentId` → logo/label, `startedAt` → order).

The `enabled` flag threads through: when the agents-row experiment is off, `store.ts:665` keeps the hook mounted (rules of hooks) but degrades `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` to a no-match live query, and `store.ts:857` swaps `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses`'s selector for a constant `() => ""` — zero rescans.

## 4. Chip list ordering

Driven by the `agents` array built in `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:66-86`, iterating `bindings.values()` (from `useTerminalAgentBindings`), filtered by the open-pane gate, then sorted:
```ts
agents.sort((a, b) => a.startedAt - b.startedAt);
```
**Ascending `startedAt` → oldest binding first, newest binding last** (`startedAt` = ms-epoch bind time from the host binding). Consumed in render order by `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx:102` `agents.map(...)`, keyed by `agent.sourceKey`.

## 5. Truncation / ellipsis / tooltip patterns in sidebar

- **`truncate`** (CSS ellipsis): row name `ExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx:288`; chip label `AgentBadge.tsx:76` (combined with `max-w-0`/`max-w-28`).
- **`OverflowFadeContainer`** (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-container`): the details strip wrapper, `WorkspaceDetails.tsx:79` — `observeChildren`, `overflow-x-auto hide-scrollbar`, edge-fade for overflowing badges.
- **`max-w-*` unfold** as truncation-in-motion: `UNFOLD_WRAPPER` (`WorkspaceDetails.tsx:28-32`), port pill `max-w-14`/`max-w-44`, chip label `max-w-28`.
- **Tooltip** (`@superset/ui/tooltip`, Radix): chip has `Tooltip delayDuration={700}` with label + status + "Click to open agent" (`AgentBadge.tsx:46, 85-93`); row icon `Tooltip delayDuration={500}` (`ExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx:188`); row action buttons `delayDuration={300}` with `HotkeyLabel`. `getStatusTooltip(status)` (`StatusIndicator.tsx:102`) provides dot tooltip text.
- **Tabular truncation**: diff stats / shortcut labels use `tabular-nums text-[10px]`.

## 6. FEATURES.md — CHIP-DOT-UNIFY and dot rows

Feature table (`FEATURES.md:58`):
> | Sidebar agent chips ride the fork dot pipeline | upstream's workspace-agents-row chips (agent logo + status dot under a sidebar row) originally had their own status pipe (host binding raw `lastEventType`, 4-case map that read fork events like SubagentActive/BackgroundRunning as idle) and a broken liveness filter (`terminal_sessions.status` stays `active` forever for a closed-tab pty → chips trapped permission-red for days). Chips now derive status from the shared (AY)/(DOT-AXES) per-source primitive and liveness from the open-pane layout gate — a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |

Related dot row (`FEATURES.md:57`):
> | Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot... Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |

Also relevant: `(MARKER-RECONCILE)` L54, `(MARKER-INACTIVE)` L55, `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` L56, and "Layered dot axes" L53 (`applySourceAxes`).

Fenced markers block (`FEATURES.md:120-131`, tab-separated `marker → path`):
```
(MARKER-RECONCILE)	apps/desktop/src/main
(MARKER-INACTIVE)	apps/desktop/src/main
(API-ABORT-RELEASE)	apps/desktop/src/main
(DOT-PERSIST)	apps/desktop/src/renderer
(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)	apps/desktop/src/renderer
(REFERR-REPAIR)	.github
(AUTO-RESUME)	apps/desktop/src/main
...
applySourceAxes	apps/desktop/src/renderer
```

---

Extra anchors: shared dot primitive `StatusIndicator.tsx:78` (STATUS_CONFIG colors/pulse, L22-65); running-agent type `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:22-36`; open-pane gate `store.ts:663-696`; per-terminal status selector `store.ts:815-883`.</result>

```css
    .group\details:has([data-state="open"]) *
));
```
Triggered when the workspace item (`group/item`) is hovered/focus-within, or a strip dropdown (`group/details`) is open. The `group/details` scope is set on the container (`DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx:83`).

Collapse/expand state lives in the chip's classes:
- Chip button collapsed = facepile circle with negative overlap margin `-ml-1.5 first:ml-0`; expanded adds padding/margin: `details-expanded:ml-1 details-expanded:pl-1 details-expanded:pr-1.5 ... details-expanded:first:ml-0` (`AgentBadge.tsx:52-56`).
- Label collapsed to zero-width/invisible, expands on hover — `AgentBadge.tsx:74-79`:
```tsx
"max-w-0 truncate opacity-0",
"transition-[max-width,margin,opacity] duration-500 ease-out motion-reduce:transition-none",
"details-expanded:ml-1 details-expanded:max-w-28 details-expanded:opacity-100 details-expanded:duration-200",
```
So at rest = icon+dot only (label `max-w-0 opacity-0`); on hover = label unfolds to `max-w-28`. Ports pill mirrors this via `UNFOLD_WRAPPER` (`WorkspaceDetails.tsx:28-32`). Strip comment lines 38-49 describe the facepile→pill morph.

## 3. CHIP-DOT-UNIFY wiring

Marker `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` appears in:
- `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:15, 42` (main wiring)
- `store.ts:665, 857` (hook `enabled:false` degradation notes)

The change replaced upstream's independent chip status pipeline with the fork's shared dot primitives (`useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:42-57`):
- **STATUS** now comes from `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` (the shared `(AY)/(DOT-AXES)` per-source `DisplayStatus`) — not the host binding's raw `lastEventType` 4-case map that read fork events (`SubagentActive`, `BackgroundRunning`) as idle. `RunningAgentStatus = DisplayStatus | "idle"` (line 20), so a chip's dot uses the exact same `StatusIndicator` primitive as the tab/pane/rollup dots.
- **LIVENESS = the open-pane gate.** `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` (store.ts:663) derives open terminals from the persisted `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout`, NOT `terminal_sessions.status` (which stays `active` forever for a closed-tab daemon pty and trapped stale permission-red chips). The gate is applied in the merge loop, `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:72-73`:
```ts
for (const binding of bindings.values()) {
    if (!openTerminalIds.has(binding.terminalId)) continue;
```
A closed tab's chip is unrepresentable. Host binding still supplies identity only (`agentId` → logo/label, `startedAt` → order).

The `enabled` flag threads through: when the agents-row experiment is off, `store.ts:665` keeps the hook mounted (rules of hooks) but degrades `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` to a no-match live query, and `store.ts:857` swaps `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses`'s selector for a constant `() => ""` — zero rescans.

## 4. Chip list ordering

Driven by the `agents` array built in `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:66-86`, iterating `bindings.values()` (from `useTerminalAgentBindings`), filtered by the open-pane gate, then sorted:
```ts
agents.sort((a, b) => a.startedAt - b.startedAt);
```
**Ascending `startedAt` → oldest binding first, newest binding last** (`startedAt` = ms-epoch bind time from the host binding). Consumed in render order by `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx:102` `agents.map(...)`, keyed by `agent.sourceKey`.

## 5. Truncation / ellipsis / tooltip patterns in sidebar

- **`truncate`** (CSS ellipsis): row name `ExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx:288`; chip label `AgentBadge.tsx:76` (combined with `max-w-0`/`max-w-28`).
- **`OverflowFadeContainer`** (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-container`): the details strip wrapper, `WorkspaceDetails.tsx:79` — `observeChildren`, `overflow-x-auto hide-scrollbar`, edge-fade for overflowing badges.
- **`max-w-*` unfold** as truncation-in-motion: `UNFOLD_WRAPPER` (`WorkspaceDetails.tsx:28-32`), port pill `max-w-14`/`max-w-44`, chip label `max-w-28`.
- **Tooltip** (`@superset/ui/tooltip`, Radix): chip has `Tooltip delayDuration={700}` with label + status + "Click to open agent" (`AgentBadge.tsx:46, 85-93`); row icon `Tooltip delayDuration={500}` (`ExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx:188`); row action buttons `delayDuration={300}` with `HotkeyLabel`. `getStatusTooltip(status)` (`StatusIndicator.tsx:102`) provides dot tooltip text.
- **Tabular truncation**: diff stats / shortcut labels use `tabular-nums text-[10px]`.

## 6. FEATURES.md — CHIP-DOT-UNIFY and dot rows

Feature table (`FEATURES.md:58`):
> | Sidebar agent chips ride the fork dot pipeline | upstream's workspace-agents-row chips (agent logo + status dot under a sidebar row) originally had their own status pipe (host binding raw `lastEventType`, 4-case map that read fork events like SubagentActive/BackgroundRunning as idle) and a broken liveness filter (`terminal_sessions.status` stays `active` forever for a closed-tab pty → chips trapped permission-red for days). Chips now derive status from the shared (AY)/(DOT-AXES) per-source primitive and liveness from the open-pane layout gate — a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |

Related dot row (`FEATURES.md:57`):
> | Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot... Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |

Also relevant: `(MARKER-RECONCILE)` L54, `(MARKER-INACTIVE)` L55, `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` L56, and "Layered dot axes" L53 (`applySourceAxes`).

Fenced markers block (`FEATURES.md:120-131`, tab-separated `marker → path`):
```
(MARKER-RECONCILE)	apps/desktop/src/main
(MARKER-INACTIVE)	apps/desktop/src/main
(API-ABORT-RELEASE)	apps/desktop/src/main
(DOT-PERSIST)	apps/desktop/src/renderer
(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)	apps/desktop/src/renderer
(REFERR-REPAIR)	.github
(AUTO-RESUME)	apps/desktop/src/main
...
applySourceAxes	apps/desktop/src/renderer
```

Extra anchors: shared dot primitive `StatusIndicator.tsx:78` (STATUS_CONFIG colors/pulse, L22-65); running-agent type `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts:22-36`; open-pane gate `store.ts:663-696`; per-terminal status selector `store.ts:815-883`.</result>

<result># DATA Layer Report: Tabs + Per-Source Agent Status

## 1. Per-workspace tab metadata — where tabs/titles/order live

**Truth store:** `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` — a **renderer-local localStorage collection** keyed by `workspaceId`.

- Collection def: `collections.ts:177` (type `Collection<WorkspaceLocalStateRow, string, LocalStorageCollectionUtils,...>`) and `collections.ts:781` — `createIndexedCollection(localStorageCollectionOptions({ id: v2_workspace_local_state-${org}, storageKey: v2-workspace-local-state-${org}, getKey: item.workspaceId }))`.
- Row schema: `dashboardSidebarLocal/schema.ts:139` `workspaceLocalStateSchema` → `paneLayout: paneWorkspaceStateSchema` (schema.ts:35, `z.custom<WorkspaceState<unknown>>()`).

**Shape** (`packages/panes/src/types.ts:19-40`):
```
WorkspaceState<TData> = { version: 1; tabs: Tab<TData>[]; activeTabId: string | null }
Tab<TData> = { id; titleOverride?; createdAt; activePaneId; layout: LayoutNode; panes: Record<string, Pane<TData>> }
Pane<TData> = { id; kind: string; titleOverride?; pinned?; data: TData }
```
- **TAB ORDER** = array order of `WorkspaceState.tabs`. `activeTabId` marks the active tab.
- **TAB NAMES / titles** resolved by `packages/panes/.../resolveTabTitle.ts:26`:
  1. `tab.titleOverride` (user-renamed tab title) →
  2. active/only pane's `pane.titleOverride` (user-renamed pane) ?? `registry[pane.kind].getTitle(pane)` (default per kind) →
  3. fallback `` `Tab ${index+1}` ``.
- Defaults per kind from the pane registry `getTitle` (`usePaneRegistry.tsx`): terminal → `"Terminal"` (line 312, but overridden live by `titleSource` from `terminalRuntimeRegistry.getTitle`, line 313), chat → `"Chat"` (506), diff → `"Changes"` (287), file → filename (217), browser → page title/host (482), comment → author (545), devtools → `"DevTools"` (564).
- On workspace create, tabs get `titleOverride: launch.label` (`appendLaunchesToPaneLayout.ts:60`).

**Read/write hook:** `useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts` — live-queries the row (`:42`), hydrates a volatile `@superset/panes` store (`:78 replaceState`), and writes back on every store change into `draft.paneLayout` (`:97`). `WorkspaceState<PaneViewerData>` where `PaneViewerData` = union in `$workspaceId/types.ts:66` (`TerminalPaneData{terminalId}`, `ChatPaneData{sessionId}`, file/browser/devtools/diff/comment).

**Note (shared/tabs-types.ts):** there is a SECOND, legacy tab model — `BaseTab`/`BaseTabsState`/`Pane` with `name`, `userTitle`, `status` (tabs-types.ts:140-268). This is the old v1 main-screen model, NOT the v2 `@superset/panes` layout. Do not conflate; the v2 sidebar work uses `@superset/panes` `WorkspaceState`.

## 2. Can the sidebar's data context see tab names/order today? — NO

`useDashboardSidebarData.ts` is the sidebar's data source. Its `v2WorkspaceLocalState` live-query **selects only `sidebarState` sub-fields** (`:249-266`: `tabOrder`, `sectionId`, `isHidden`, snooze/archive/etc.) — **`paneLayout` is never selected.** Workspace display name/branch come from `hostWorkspaces` (`:283-284` `workspace.name`, `workspace.branch`), not from tabs.

So the sidebar's context has zero visibility into per-tab titles/order today. What a chip refactor would need:
- Add `paneLayout` to the sidebar's `v2WorkspaceLocalState` select (or a new live-query), then map `paneLayout.tabs` → `{ id, resolveTabTitle(...), order }`.
- `resolveTabTitle` requires the **pane registry** (`registry[kind].getTitle`), which currently lives in the v2-workspace route (`usePaneRegistry.tsx`), not in the sidebar tree — titles depend on registry + live terminal titles (`terminalRuntimeRegistry`), so a pure data-layer read gets `titleOverride` but not the live default title without the registry.
- Precedent for reading `paneLayout` outside the route already exists: `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` (store.ts:663) live-queries `v2WorkspaceLocalState` selecting `paneLayout` and walks `tabs[].panes` to collect terminalIds.

## 3. Per-source status primitive — where per-dot status is computed/stored

**File:** `renderer/stores/v2-notifications/store.ts` (zustand store `useV2NotificationStore`).

- **Source key** = `` `${type}:${id}` `` (`getV2NotificationSourceKey`, store.ts:571). Types: `terminal | chat | manual` (store.ts:37-46).
  - terminal source id = **terminalId** (`getV2TerminalNotificationSource`, :578).
  - chat source id = **sessionId** (`getV2ChatNotificationSource`, :584; extracted from `pane.data.sessionId`, :1100).
  - manual source id = **workspaceId** (:590).
- **Chip/tab → source key mapping:** `getV2NotificationSourcesForPane` (:596) — terminal pane → `terminal:<pane.data.terminalId>` (:1086), chat pane → `chat:<pane.data.sessionId>`. `getV2NotificationSourcesForTab` (:606) unions all panes in a tab. So a tab's dot = fold over the sources of its panes; a per-terminal chip = one `terminal:<id>` key.
- **Per-source storage:** `state.sources: Record<sourceKey, V2NotificationStatusEntry>` (:104). Entry (:82): `{ sourceKey, source, workspaceId, status: ActivePaneStatus, axes: V2AgentStatusAxes, occurredAt }`.
- **DOT-AXES model:** each source holds independent latches `axes: Partial<Record<"permission"|"working"|"review", number>>` (:62). Rendered `status` is DERIVED via `deriveAgentStatus` (:75) = highest of `permission > working > review`.
- **Mutator `applySourceAxes`** (:306) — the funnel every write goes through: applies set/clear latches to one source, re-derives status, removes entry when no axis active. Cross-workspace guard: a foreign-workspace entry is only replaced when THIS workspace `set`s an axis (:312).
- **`resolveV2AgentStatusTransition`** (`V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts:32`) maps host `AgentLifecyclePayload` events → axis set/clear ops on `terminal:<payload.terminalId>`. E.g. `Start` → set working, clear permission+review (:80); `PermissionRequest` → set permission (:91); `Detached` → clear permission+working (:54); turn-end → set review unless visible/was-permission (:105).
- **Two extra blue axes** kept OUT of `sources`, keyed by **terminalId**: `shellRunningTerminals` (OSC 133 foreground command, :98/:256) and `backgroundRunningTerminals` (cloud/background task, :135/:281).

## 4. Workspace-level rollup — precedence red>yellow>blue>green

Two precedence ladders, both in store.ts:

- Agent-only axes (`ActivePaneStatus`): `permission > working > review` — `AGENT_AXIS_PRIORITY` (:69) and `STATUS_PRIORITY` in `shared/tabs-types.ts:44` (used by `getHighestPriorityStatus`).
- Full **display** ladder incl. blue: `DISPLAY_STATUS_PRIORITY = [permission, working, shell-running, background-running, review]` (store.ts:723) → red > yellow > blue(shell) > blue(bg) > green. Folded by `getHighestPriorityDisplayStatus` (:731).

Rollup selectors:
- `selectV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(workspaceId, openTerminalIds)` (:970) — the single status the **workspace icon** renders: folds `getSourceDisplayStatus` over every source of the workspace, PLUS open terminals whose only state is a blue axis (:990). Terminal sources gated to open terminals; chat/manual always count.
- `getSourceDisplayStatus` (:759) — one source's dot: agent permission/working win; else terminal's shell/background blue; else agent review; else null.
- Per-terminal chips: `selectV2WorkspaceTerminalDisplayKey` (:815) / `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` (:855) → array of `{terminalId, status: DisplayStatus}`, derived from the SAME `getSourceDisplayStatus`. Tab dot: `selectV2SourcesDisplayStatus` (:940). Comment at :715-721 states all surfaces derive from the same primitive so rollup == fold of per-source dots — no drift by construction.

**Open-terminal gate:** `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds(workspaceId)` (:663) live-queries `paneLayout` and returns terminalIds present in open panes; closed/never-opened terminals are filtered (`isClosedTerminalSource`, :704) so a lingering entry never tints the workspace. Chat/manual sources are never gated.

## 5. Renderer-local vs synced + persistence (what a refactor must not break)

- **`v2WorkspaceLocalState` (incl. `paneLayout`, `sidebarState`): renderer-local, localStorage**, org-scoped `storageKey: v2-workspace-local-state-${org}` (collections.ts:786). Persists across reload AND full restart. NOT synced to host. Read-time healed by `healWorkspaceLocalState` / `sanitizePaneLayout` (schema.ts:86,401) — corrupt/legacy layouts reset to empty, individually-bad tabs dropped, `activeTabId` repaired.
- **`v2-notifications` store (dots): renderer-memory, persisted to `window.sessionStorage`** under name `"v2-notification-dots"` (store.ts:556-557). **(DOT-PERSIST)** comment (:189-198): sessionStorage is deliberate — survives in-place reload (Ctrl+R / error-boundary Reload) but clears on real app restart so dead terminals can't pin stale dots. `partialize` persists exactly: `sources`, `shellRunningTerminals`, `backgroundRunningTerminals`, `manualUnread`, `terminalSeenAt` (:558). **A chip refactor must preserve this partialize set and the sessionStorage backing** — dropping `backgroundRunningTerminals` (the blue axis with no OSC self-heal) would make a running background task invisible for hours after a reload.
- **Agent bindings (agent TYPE, see §6): host-synced**, React-Query cached, invalidated on lifecycle events — not renderer-persisted.

## 6. Tabs with no agent (plain shell) + agent-type per tab

- **Plain shell tab** is a terminal pane like any other: `pane.kind === "terminal"`, `pane.data = { terminalId }`. There is NO agent flag in the pane layout — a plain shell and an agent terminal are indistinguishable inside `paneLayout`. Its dot appears only via the blue `shell-running` axis (foreground command) or none; `selectV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus` explicitly represents open terminals whose only state is a blue axis with "no agent source entry" (store.ts:988-994).
- **Agent type (Claude/Codex/Gemini/…) is NOT in the pane layout.** It lives in the **host-service DB** table `terminal_agent_bindings` (`packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:46`), keyed by `terminalId`, column `agentId: BuiltinAgentId` (:53). Binding also carries `agentSessionId`, `lastEventAt`, `lastEventType`.
- **Access from renderer:** `useTerminalAgentBindings(workspaceId)` (`renderer/hooks/host-service/useTerminalAgentBindings/useTerminalAgentBindings.ts:19`) → `Map<terminalId, TerminalAgentBinding>`, invalidated on `agent:lifecycle`/`terminal:lifecycle`. `useTerminalAgentBinding(workspaceId, terminalId)` for one (:64).
- **Icon choice today:** `TerminalPaneIcon.tsx:17` reads `binding?.agentId`, resolves icon via `usePresetIcon(agentId)` and label via `BUILTIN_AGENT_LABELS[agentId]`; falls back to generic `TerminalSquare` glyph when no agent bound. Agent ids are `claude|codex|gemini|amp|copilot|...` (`packages/shared/src/builtin-terminal-agents.ts:61,80,90,...`; labels `"Claude"|"Codex"|"Gemini"`).
- **Implication for a sidebar chip:** to pick a per-tab agent icon, the chip must join `paneLayout.tabs[].panes` (terminalId) against `useTerminalAgentBindings(workspaceId)` — the pane layout alone (renderer-local) does not carry agent type; the binding map is a separate host-synced source keyed by terminalId. A tab with a terminal pane but no binding entry = plain shell → generic terminal icon.

### Key file anchors
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../useV2WorkspacePaneLayout/useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts`
- `packages/panes/src/types.ts` (WorkspaceState/Tab/Pane), `.../Workspace/utils/resolveTabTitle.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/v2-notifications/store.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../CollectionsProvider/dashboardSidebarLocal/schema.ts` + `collections.ts:177,781`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../DashboardSidebar/hooks/useDashboardSidebarData/useDashboardSidebarData.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/hooks/host-service/useTerminalAgentBindings/useTerminalAgentBindings.ts` + `packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:46`, `.../terminal-agents/persistence.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../usePaneRegistry/usePaneRegistry.tsx` + `.../TerminalPane/components/TerminalPaneIcon/TerminalPaneIcon.tsx`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/workspace-creates/appendLaunchesToPaneLayout.ts` + `writeWorkspacePaneLayout.ts`</result>

- **Per-source storage:** `state.sources: Record<sourceKey, V2NotificationStatusEntry>` (:104). Entry (:82): `{ sourceKey, source, workspaceId, status: ActivePaneStatus, axes: V2AgentStatusAxes, occurredAt }`.
- **DOT-AXES model:** each source holds independent latches `axes: Partial<Record<"permission"|"working"|"review", number>>` (:62). Rendered `status` is DERIVED via `deriveAgentStatus` (:75) = highest of `permission > working > review`.
- **Mutator `applySourceAxes`** (:306) — the funnel every write goes through: applies set/clear latches to one source, re-derives status, removes entry when no axis active. Cross-workspace guard: a foreign-workspace entry is only replaced when THIS workspace `set`s an axis (:312).
- **`resolveV2AgentStatusTransition`** (`V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts:32`) maps host `AgentLifecyclePayload` events → axis set/clear ops on `terminal:<payload.terminalId>`. E.g. `Start` → set working, clear permission+review (:80); `PermissionRequest` → set permission (:91); `Detached` → clear permission+working (:54); turn-end → set review unless visible/was-permission (:105).
- **Two extra blue axes** kept OUT of `sources`, keyed by **terminalId**: `shellRunningTerminals` (OSC 133 foreground command, :98/:256) and `backgroundRunningTerminals` (cloud/background task, :135/:281).

## 4. Workspace-level rollup — precedence red>yellow>blue>green

Two precedence ladders, both in store.ts:

- Agent-only axes (`ActivePaneStatus`): `permission > working > review` — `AGENT_AXIS_PRIORITY` (:69) and `STATUS_PRIORITY` in `shared/tabs-types.ts:44` (used by `getHighestPriorityStatus`).
- Full **display** ladder incl. blue: `DISPLAY_STATUS_PRIORITY = [permission, working, shell-running, background-running, review]` (store.ts:723) → red > yellow > blue(shell) > blue(bg) > green. Folded by `getHighestPriorityDisplayStatus` (:731).

Rollup selectors:
- `selectV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(workspaceId, openTerminalIds)` (:970) — the single status the **workspace icon** renders: folds `getSourceDisplayStatus` over every source of the workspace, PLUS open terminals whose only state is a blue axis (:990). Terminal sources gated to open terminals; chat/manual always count.
- `getSourceDisplayStatus` (:759) — one source's dot: agent permission/working win; else terminal's shell/background blue; else agent review; else null.
- Per-terminal chips: `selectV2WorkspaceTerminalDisplayKey` (:815) / `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` (:855) → array of `{terminalId, status: DisplayStatus}`, derived from the SAME `getSourceDisplayStatus`. Tab dot: `selectV2SourcesDisplayStatus` (:940). Comment at :715-721 states all surfaces derive from the same primitive so rollup == fold of per-source dots — no drift by construction.

**Open-terminal gate:** `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds(workspaceId)` (:663) live-queries `paneLayout` and returns terminalIds present in open panes; closed/never-opened terminals are filtered (`isClosedTerminalSource`, :704) so a lingering entry never tints the workspace. Chat/manual sources are never gated.

## 5. Renderer-local vs synced + persistence (what a refactor must not break)

- **`v2WorkspaceLocalState` (incl. `paneLayout`, `sidebarState`): renderer-local, localStorage**, org-scoped `storageKey: v2-workspace-local-state-${org}` (collections.ts:786). Persists across reload AND full restart. NOT synced to host. Read-time healed by `healWorkspaceLocalState` / `sanitizePaneLayout` (schema.ts:86,401) — corrupt/legacy layouts reset to empty, individually-bad tabs dropped, `activeTabId` repaired.
- **`v2-notifications` store (dots): renderer-memory, persisted to `window.sessionStorage`** under name `"v2-notification-dots"` (store.ts:556-557). **(DOT-PERSIST)** comment (:189-198): sessionStorage is deliberate — survives in-place reload (Ctrl+R / error-boundary Reload) but clears on real app restart so dead terminals can't pin stale dots. `partialize` persists exactly: `sources`, `shellRunningTerminals`, `backgroundRunningTerminals`, `manualUnread`, `terminalSeenAt` (:558). **A chip refactor must preserve this partialize set and the sessionStorage backing** — dropping `backgroundRunningTerminals` (the blue axis with no OSC self-heal) would make a running background task invisible for hours after a reload.
- **Agent bindings (agent TYPE, see §6): host-synced**, React-Query cached, invalidated on lifecycle events — not renderer-persisted.

## 6. Tabs with no agent (plain shell) + agent-type per tab

- **Plain shell tab** is a terminal pane like any other: `pane.kind === "terminal"`, `pane.data = { terminalId }`. There is NO agent flag in the pane layout — a plain shell and an agent terminal are indistinguishable inside `paneLayout`. Its dot appears only via the blue `shell-running` axis (foreground command) or none; `selectV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus` explicitly represents open terminals whose only state is a blue axis with "no agent source entry" (store.ts:988-994).
- **Agent type (Claude/Codex/Gemini/…) is NOT in the pane layout.** It lives in the **host-service DB** table `terminal_agent_bindings` (`packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:46`), keyed by `terminalId`, column `agentId: BuiltinAgentId` (:53). Binding also carries `agentSessionId`, `lastEventAt`, `lastEventType`.
- **Access from renderer:** `useTerminalAgentBindings(workspaceId)` (`renderer/hooks/host-service/useTerminalAgentBindings/useTerminalAgentBindings.ts:19`) → `Map<terminalId, TerminalAgentBinding>`, invalidated on `agent:lifecycle`/`terminal:lifecycle`. `useTerminalAgentBinding(workspaceId, terminalId)` for one (:64).
- **Icon choice today:** `TerminalPaneIcon.tsx:17` reads `binding?.agentId`, resolves icon via `usePresetIcon(agentId)` and label via `BUILTIN_AGENT_LABELS[agentId]`; falls back to generic `TerminalSquare` glyph when no agent bound. Agent ids are `claude|codex|gemini|amp|copilot|...` (`packages/shared/src/builtin-terminal-agents.ts:61,80,90,...`; labels `"Claude"|"Codex"|"Gemini"`).
- **Implication for a sidebar chip:** to pick a per-tab agent icon, the chip must join `paneLayout.tabs[].panes` (terminalId) against `useTerminalAgentBindings(workspaceId)` — the pane layout alone (renderer-local) does not carry agent type; the binding map is a separate host-synced source keyed by terminalId. A tab with a terminal pane but no binding entry = plain shell → generic terminal icon.

### Key file anchors
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../useV2WorkspacePaneLayout/useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts`
- `packages/panes/src/types.ts` (WorkspaceState/Tab/Pane), `.../Workspace/utils/resolveTabTitle.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/v2-notifications/store.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../V2NotificationController/lib/statusTransitions.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../CollectionsProvider/dashboardSidebarLocal/schema.ts` + `collections.ts:177,781`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../DashboardSidebar/hooks/useDashboardSidebarData/useDashboardSidebarData.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/hooks/host-service/useTerminalAgentBindings/useTerminalAgentBindings.ts` + `packages/host-service/src/db/schema.ts:46`, `.../terminal-agents/persistence.ts`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/.../usePaneRegistry/usePaneRegistry.tsx` + `.../TerminalPane/components/TerminalPaneIcon/TerminalPaneIcon.tsx`
- `apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/workspace-creates/appendLaunchesToPaneLayout.ts` + `writeWorkspacePaneLayout.ts`</result>

Data layer in. Critical findings for the brief: tab order/titles live in `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` (`tabs[]` array order, `titleOverride`), sidebar currently selects only `sidebarState` (paneLayout invisible to it); agent type per tab comes from joining pane terminalIds against `useTerminalAgentBindings`; title resolution normally needs the route-scoped pane registry, so sidebar needs its own lightweight resolver (`titleOverride` → pane override → kind default → `Tab N`). Dots store partialize/sessionStorage must not change.

One explorer left (navigation).

<result># Navigation & Tab-Focus Report

## 1. Sidebar workspace/branch row click → workspace focus

Handler: `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions.handleClick`
`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/DashboardSidebar/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/hooks/useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions/useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions.ts:94`

Chain: click → TanStack Router `navigate` (no store action). Two branches:
```ts
if (onKanban) {
  navigate({ to: "/kanban", search: { cardId: workspaceId } });  // :102
  return;
}
navigate({ to: "/v2-workspace/$workspaceId", params: { workspaceId } });  // :105
```
- No tab/pane specificity — plain route push.
- `isActive` computed via `matchRoute({ to: "/v2-workspace/$workspaceId", params })` (:86).
- Comment (:96-100): click navigates only; per-terminal read-clearing deferred to `useClearActivePaneAttention` on focus.
- Persistence of "last workspace": `navigateToWorkspace` (v1 route) writes `localStorage.lastViewedWorkspaceId` — but the v2 badge path uses `navigateToV2Workspace` which does NOT. See `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/utils/workspace-navigation.ts:28,48`.

## 2. Focus a SPECIFIC tab/pane — existing mechanism

### Deep-link route search params (the cross-route focus path)
Route validates search params `terminalId`, `chatSessionId`, `focusRequestId`, `openUrl`, `openUrlTarget`, `openUrlRequestId`:
`apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/v2-workspace/$workspaceId/page.tsx:22`

Consumed by `useConsumeAutomationRunLink`
`.../v2-workspace/$workspaceId/hooks/useConsumeAutomationRunLink/useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts:25`
- `terminalId` → `focusOrAddTerminalPane(store, terminalId)` (:73)
- `chatSessionId` → `focusOrAddChatPane(store, chatSessionId)` (:101)
- `focusRequestId` = dedup/replay key (`getAutomationRunLinkConsumeKey`, :112). Fresh uuid → re-focus even if pane already consumed once.

### The focus primitive (reuse target)
`focusTerminalPane.ts:28`
```ts
export function focusTerminalPane(store, terminalId): boolean {
  const location = findTerminalPaneLocation(state, terminalId);  // scans tab.panes for data.terminalId
  if (!location) return false;
  state.setActiveTab(location.tabId);
  state.setActivePane(location);   // {tabId, paneId}
  return true;
}
```
`focusOrAddTerminalPane` (:41) focuses if present, else `addTab` with a terminal pane. Chat equivalent inline at `useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts:151` (matches `pane.data.sessionId`, then `setActiveTab` + `setActivePane`).

### EXISTING sidebar chip that does exactly this ("Click to open agent")
`DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx:33`
```ts
const handleClick = () => {
  void navigateToV2Workspace(workspaceId, navigate, {
    search: { terminalId: agent.terminalId, focusRequestId: crypto.randomUUID() },
  });
};
```
`.../DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx` — this is the pattern to clone. Tooltip footer literally reads "Click to open agent" (:91).

### Identifier a chip needs
Domain ids, NOT internal pane/tab ids:
- terminal chip → `terminalId` (matched against `pane.data.terminalId`)
- chat chip → `chatSessionId` (matched against `pane.data.sessionId`)

Internal `tabId`/`paneId` are `generateId()`-generated and volatile — do not use across routes.

### Store reachability constraint
Pane store is created per-mount via `useMemo` keyed by workspaceId inside the route (`useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts:26`) — NOT a global registry. So:
- Workspace mounted → `store.getState().setActiveTab(tabId)` / `focusTerminalPane` reachable only within the route tree.
- Sidebar chip (cross-route) → must go through navigate + deep-link search params (the badge pattern). Layout (incl. `activeTabId`) persists to TanStack DB collection `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` (`useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts:99`).

### Keyboard shortcuts (same primitive)
`useWorkspaceHotkeys.ts`: `PREV_TAB`/`NEXT_TAB` (:146,154), `JUMP_TO_TAB_1..9` → `switchToTab(index)` → `setActiveTab` (:180-197). `OPEN_DIFF_VIEWER` focuses diff pane via `setActiveTab`+`setActivePane` (:92).

### Core store actions (package `@superset/panes`)
`packages/panes/src/core/store/store.ts`:
- `setActiveTab(tabId)` :225 — guards tab exists.
- `setActivePane({tabId, paneId})` :247 — ALSO sets `activeTabId` (focuses tab + pane in one call). Guards pane exists.

## 3. Tab strip component (name + dot + click-to-switch) — the reuse pattern

Chain: `Workspace` → `TabBar` → `TabItem`.

`packages/panes/src/react/components/Workspace/Workspace.tsx:97` wires:
```ts
<TabBar ... onSelectTab={(tabId) => store.getState().setActiveTab(tabId)} ... />
```

`.../TabBar/TabBar.tsx:204` renders each `TabItem` with `onSelect={() => onSelectTab(tab.id)}` (:210), plus `accessory={renderTabAccessory?.(tab)}` (:216).

`.../TabBar/components/TabItem/TabItem.tsx` — the click-to-switch + name + dot:
- Click handler: wrapper `<div onClick={() => onSelect()}>` (:129). Click, not mousedown (comment :126 — drag suppression).
- Name: `useTabTitle(tab, tabs, registry)` (:54) rendered in `<OverflowFadeText>` (:161).
- Dot: `accessory` rendered in a `w-7` slot (:170-175), `opacity-100 group-hover:opacity-0` — the dot is REPLACED by the close (X) button on hover (:176-194).
- Middle-click closes (:152); double-click renames (:158).

The dot's content = `V2NotificationStatusIndicator`, injected by the app via `renderTabAccessory` in `V2WorkspaceView.tsx:280`:
```ts
renderTabAccessory={(tab) => (
  <V2NotificationStatusIndicator sources={getV2NotificationSourcesForTab(tab)} />
)}
```
`.../v2-workspace/$workspaceId/components/V2NotificationStatusIndicator/`. This is the "big bang thread ●" dot.

For a chip click to reuse this: it should do what `onSelectTab` does — `store.getState().setActiveTab(tabId)` (in-route), or `setActivePane({tabId,paneId})` to also land on a specific pane.

## 4. Focus/activation side effects

- `useClearActivePaneAttention` (`.../hooks/useClearActivePaneAttention/useClearActivePaneAttention.ts`): watches the ACTIVE pane; when its notification status is `"review"`, calls `markTerminalSeen(source.id, binding.lastEventAt)` for each terminal source — clears the review dot on focus. Host-clock (`binding.lastEventAt`) only, to keep monotonic seen-comparison sound. Mounted once in `V2WorkspaceView.tsx:121`. Consequence: focusing a pane (via `setActivePane`) auto-clears that pane's review attention; unfocused panes keep dots (matches sidebar per-terminal dots — see `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions.ts:96-100`).
- Manual unread mark (workspace-level): `useV2NotificationStore` `setManualUnread`/`clearManualUnread` + `markWorkspaceTerminalsSeen` (`useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions.ts:51-61`, `clearWorkspaceAttention`). Navigation does NOT clear these — only the per-pane review hook fires.
- Pane-layout persistence effect: `useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts:83` subscribes to store and writes `activeTabId`+tabs to `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` on every change → the focused tab survives reload/remount.
- `useConsumeAutomationRunLink` guards: ignores a `terminalId`/`chatSessionId` that belongs to another workspace (:60-72, :94-100) before focusing.
- Scroll cache: `.../state/paneScrollStateCache/paneScrollStateCache.ts` (per-pane scroll restore) — relevant if a chip forces pane remount.

## 5. Tooltips for hover-name reveal (reuse for truncated chip labels)

Radix/shadcn Tooltip: `packages/ui/src/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` (`import * as TooltipPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-tooltip"`), exported as `@superset/ui/tooltip` → `Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent`, `TooltipProvider`. Each `Tooltip` self-wraps a `TooltipProvider` (own `delayDuration`), so no app-level provider needed.

Existing reuse examples (copy these):
- Truncated tab label reveal — `TabItem.tsx:144-169`: `<Tooltip delayDuration={500} open={isDragging ? false : undefined}>`, `<TooltipContent side="bottom" showArrow={false}>{title}</TooltipContent>`, wrapping the `OverflowFadeText` title.
- Sidebar agent chip — `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx:46`: `<Tooltip delayDuration={700}>` with `TooltipContent side="top" sideOffset={6} showArrow={false}` showing label + status + "Click to open agent".

Truncation helpers already available: `OverflowFadeText` (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-text`, used in TabItem:161) and `OverflowFadeContainer` (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-container`, TabBar:192).

Note: sidebar workspace-row rich hovers use Radix HoverCard instead (`DashboardSidebarWorkspaceHoverCardContent/`, `DashboardSidebarHoverCardOverlay/`) — heavier, for cards not label reveal. For a truncated chip label, reuse Tooltip.</result>

- Pane-layout persistence effect: `useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts:83` subscribes to store and writes `activeTabId`+tabs to `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` on every change → the focused tab survives reload/remount.
- `useConsumeAutomationRunLink` guards: ignores a `terminalId`/`chatSessionId` that belongs to another workspace (:60-72, :94-100) before focusing.
- Scroll cache: `.../state/paneScrollStateCache/paneScrollStateCache.ts` (per-pane scroll restore) — relevant if a chip forces pane remount.

## 5. Tooltips for hover-name reveal (reuse for truncated chip labels)

Radix/shadcn Tooltip: `packages/ui/src/components/ui/tooltip.tsx` (`import * as TooltipPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-tooltip"`), exported as `@superset/ui/tooltip` → `Tooltip`, `TooltipTrigger`, `TooltipContent`, `TooltipProvider`. Each `Tooltip` self-wraps a `TooltipProvider` (own `delayDuration`), so no app-level provider needed.

Existing reuse examples (copy these):
- Truncated tab label reveal — `TabItem.tsx:144-169`: `<Tooltip delayDuration={500} open={isDragging ? false : undefined}>`, `<TooltipContent side="bottom" showArrow={false}>{title}</TooltipContent>`, wrapping the `OverflowFadeText` title.
- Sidebar agent chip — `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx:46`: `<Tooltip delayDuration={700}>` with `TooltipContent side="top" sideOffset={6} showArrow={false}` showing label + status + "Click to open agent".

Truncation helpers already available: `OverflowFadeText` (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-text`, used in TabItem:161) and `OverflowFadeContainer` (`@superset/ui/overflow-fade-container`, TabBar:192).

Note: sidebar workspace-row rich hovers use Radix HoverCard instead (`DashboardSidebarWorkspaceHoverCardContent/`, `DashboardSidebarHoverCardOverlay/`) — heavier, for cards not label reveal. For a truncated chip label, reuse Tooltip.</result>

input.prompt:
You are implementing a sidebar feature in C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64 (Superset desktop fork, Electron app in apps/desktop, Bun monorepo). EDIT FILES ONLY — do NOT commit, push, or touch anything under .github/workflows. Report a precise changed-file list + what you verified at the end.

# Feature: (TAB-CHIPS) — sidebar rows show per-TAB chips instead of name-line dots when a workspace has 2+ tabs

## Exact user-approved spec
- Workspace row with 0 or 1 open tab: keep upstream look — a SINGLE status dot inline right of the workspace name (fold of that tab's sources), NO chip row.
- Workspace row with 2+ open tabs: NO dots next to the name at all. Instead a chip row under the row: one chip PER TAB, in EXACT tab order (paneLayout.tabs array order), ALWAYS expanded (no hover-collapse), each chip = agent icon with the status dot as a badge on the icon's top-right corner + label = the ACTUAL tab title, ellipsis-truncated (~18ch, use max-w-28/32 + `truncate`), full title in a Radix Tooltip. Chips wrap onto multiple lines (flex-wrap), not horizontal scroll.
- Every tab gets a chip, including agent-less plain-shell tabs (generic terminal icon; dot only when there is a status — blue shell-running counts as status).
- Chip click: focus that workspace AND that specific tab.
- Workspace ICON rollup dot: unchanged.

## Current code map (verified by 4 explorers — trust these anchors)

RENDERING
- Row: `apps/desktop/src/renderer/routes/_authenticated/_dashboard/components/DashboardSidebar/components/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/components/DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow/DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow.tsx` — L299-305 currently maps `terminalStatuses` to N `StatusIndicator` dots inline right of the name. This is the surface that becomes: 1 folded dot when tabCount<=1, nothing when tabCount>=2.
- Item wiring: `.../DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx` — L54 `useV2WorkspaceDisplayStatus(id)` (icon rollup, keep), L57 `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses(id)` (feeds the N dots today), L257-258 props, L286 renders `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails`.
- Chip strip: `.../DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails.tsx` — L63-69 `runningAgents` + `hasMultipleAgents` gate (`runningAgents.length > 1`), experiment gate `useWorkspaceAgentsRowEnabled()` L57, chips rendered L102-108 inside an `OverflowFadeContainer` (L79, horizontal scroll) which also hosts port pills (L110-157) sharing an animated "facepile→pill" layer.
- Chip: `.../DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge/DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge.tsx` — icon `usePresetIcon(agent.agentId)` fallback `LuBot` (L61-66); corner-badge dot already exists: `StatusIndicator` absolute `-top-0.5 -right-0.5`, hidden when `idle` (L67-72); label span L74-82 uses hover-collapse via `details-expanded:` Tailwind custom variant (defined `apps/desktop/src/renderer/globals.css:15-18`, triggered by `.group/item:hover` / `.group/details:has([data-state="open"])`); click L33-40 `navigateToV2Workspace(workspaceId, navigate, { search: { terminalId: agent.terminalId, focusRequestId: crypto.randomUUID() } })`; Tooltip L46, L85-93.
- Chip data hook (CHIP-DOT-UNIFY): `.../hooks/useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents/useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents.ts` — merges `useTerminalAgentBindings` (identity) × `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` (open-pane liveness gate) × `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` (status), source key `terminal:${terminalId}`, sorted by `startedAt` (L84). Marker comments L15-19, L42-57 — PRESERVE the CHIP-DOT-UNIFY invariant: chips must derive status from the shared v2-notifications primitive and liveness from the pane layout, never from `terminal_sessions.status` or raw `lastEventType`.

DATA
- Tab truth: `v2WorkspaceLocalState.paneLayout` (renderer-local localStorage TanStack DB collection, keyed workspaceId). Shape `packages/panes/src/types.ts:19-40`: `WorkspaceState = { version:1; tabs: Tab[]; activeTabId }`, `Tab = { id; titleOverride?; createdAt; activePaneId; layout; panes: Record<string,Pane> }`, `Pane = { id; kind; titleOverride?; pinned?; data }`. TAB ORDER = `tabs[]` array order. Terminal pane: `pane.kind === "terminal"`, `pane.data.terminalId`; chat pane: `pane.data.sessionId`.
- Precedent for reading paneLayout outside the route: `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds` in `apps/desktop/src/renderer/stores/v2-notifications/store.ts:663-696` (live query selecting `paneLayout`, walks tabs→panes via `getTerminalIdForPane` L1086-1093). The sidebar's `useDashboardSidebarData.ts` does NOT select paneLayout — do not bloat it; build a NEW dedicated hook.
- Title resolution: route uses `packages/panes/.../resolveTabTitle.ts:26` (needs the route-scoped pane registry — NOT importable from sidebar). Build a small sidebar-local resolver: `tab.titleOverride` → active (or only) pane `titleOverride` → per-kind default map (terminal→"Terminal", chat→"Chat", diff→"Changes", file→filename from pane data if cheaply available else "File", browser→"Browser", devtools→"DevTools", comment→"Comment") → `Tab ${index+1}`. Keep it a pure util with tests colocated per repo structure rules.
- Agent identity per tab: join tab's terminal panes against `useTerminalAgentBindings(workspaceId)` (`apps/desktop/src/renderer/hooks/host-service/useTerminalAgentBindings/...`, Map keyed terminalId, `.agentId` = claude|codex|gemini|...). Icon precedence for a multi-pane tab: active pane's binding, else first terminal pane with a binding, else generic terminal icon (see `TerminalPaneIcon.tsx:17` pattern: `usePresetIcon(agentId)` + fallback `TerminalSquare`).
- Per-tab dot status: fold the tab's pane sources with the SAME primitive as everything else — sources per pane: `terminal:${terminalId}` / `chat:${sessionId}` (`getV2NotificationSourcesForPane` store.ts:596, `getV2NotificationSourcesForTab` :606), fold via `getSourceDisplayStatus` (:759) / `getHighestPriorityDisplayStatus` (:731), precedence permission>working>shell-running>background-running>review. Prefer adding a store selector + hook near `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` (:855) e.g. `useV2WorkspaceTabChips(workspaceId, enabled)` returning ordered `[{tabId, title, agentId|null, status|null, focusTarget}]` — follow the existing memoized display-key pattern (`selectV2WorkspaceTerminalDisplayKey` :815) so re-renders stay cheap. Also reuse its `enabled:false` degradation pattern (store.ts:665, :857 — hooks stay mounted, selectors degrade to constants).
- DO NOT change the v2-notifications persistence: `partialize` set (`sources`, `shellRunningTerminals`, `backgroundRunningTerminals`, `manualUnread`, `terminalSeenAt`) and sessionStorage name `"v2-notification-dots"` (store.ts:556-558) must stay byte-identical in behavior (DOT-PERSIST).

NAVIGATION (chip click)
- Existing deep-link: route search params validated in `.../v2-workspace/$workspaceId/page.tsx:22` (`terminalId`, `chatSessionId`, `focusRequestId`, ...), consumed by `useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts` → `focusOrAddTerminalPane` / `focusOrAddChatPane`; `focusTerminalPane.ts:28` does `setActiveTab` + `setActivePane`. `focusRequestId` = fresh uuid per click (dedup key).
- Chip click impl: if the tab's active pane (else first pane) has a domain id (terminalId or chat sessionId) → clone the existing AgentBadge pattern (`navigateToV2Workspace(workspaceId, navigate, { search: { terminalId | chatSessionId, focusRequestId: crypto.randomUUID() } })`). For tabs with NO domain-id pane (file/browser/diff-only tab): add an optional validated `tabId` search param to page.tsx + consume it in `useConsumeAutomationRunLink` with a guarded `setActiveTab(tabId)` (tab exists in state, else no-op) — do NOT use volatile paneIds. Keep the other-workspace guard pattern (:60-72).
- Focus side effect to keep: `useClearActivePaneAttention` clears review-green on focus — no change needed, just don't bypass it.

## Implementation shape (suggested, adapt as the code dictates)
1. New store hook `useV2WorkspaceTabChips` in v2-notifications `store.ts` (or adjacent file if store.ts is huge): live-query paneLayout (mirror `useV2WorkspaceOpenTerminalIds`'s query), produce ordered per-tab chip models with title-independent parts (tabId, ordered pane descriptors incl. kind + domain ids). Fold statuses from store state.
2. Sidebar hook `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceTabChips` (next to `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents`) joining bindings for agentId + resolving titles with the new util.
3. `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceDetails`: switch gate from `runningAgents.length > 1` to `tabChips.length >= 2`; render chips from tab-chip models in tab order; container becomes flex-wrap (chips-only — keep port pills working; if they share the layer, split chips into their own wrapping row above/beside the ports pill and keep ports behavior untouched). Keep the `useWorkspaceAgentsRowEnabled()` experiment wiring as-is (gate stays ANDed).
4. Chip component: either evolve `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceAgentBadge` or add a sibling `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceTabChip` (one folder per component + index.ts per repo rules; prefer a new component + delete/retire the old one ONLY if nothing else uses it — check). ALWAYS-expanded: remove `details-expanded:` collapse variants from the label (label always `max-w-28`-ish + `truncate` + opacity-100); keep the corner-badge dot exactly as-is; Tooltip shows full title + status.
5. Row dots: in `DashboardSidebarExpandedWorkspaceRow` render single folded dot when tabCount<=1 (fold that tab's sources — when 1 tab with multiple panes, ONE dot, highest-priority status), nothing when >=2. Thread tabCount/folded status from the item. `useV2WorkspaceTerminalStatuses` stays for any other consumers — check who else uses it before removing anything.
6. `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents`: if nothing else consumes it after the switch, retire it; if kanban/other surfaces consume it, leave untouched.

## Hard constraints
- Fork rules: no `any` unless unavoidable; one component per folder + colocated tests for new pure utils; NEVER run root `bun run lint:fix` (biome once EOL-rewrote 4,633 files) — verify with scoped `bunx biome check <changed files>` only, and fix its complaints.
- LF line endings in all edited/new files (no CRLF).
- Do not touch `.github/workflows/**`, FEATURES.md, AGENTS.md (the coordinator handles the FEATURES.md marker row).
- Add `(TAB-CHIPS)` marker comments in the key new/changed code sites (hook + strip + row dot logic) so the nightly marker gate can track the feature — comment style like the existing `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` comments.
- Preserve: CHIP-DOT-UNIFY invariant (shared status primitive + open-pane-layout liveness), DOT-PERSIST partialize, icon rollup dot behavior, ports pill behavior, `useClearActivePaneAttention` flow.
- Verification to run and report: `bunx biome check` on every changed file (0 issues), plus `bunx tsc --noEmit` scoped if practical — the repo carries accepted type debt, so only report NEW errors introduced by your change (compare against the same file set before edits if needed). Run any colocated tests you add via `bun test <path>`.

Deliver: summary of design decisions actually taken (esp. tab-title resolver, ports-pill layout split, tabId deep-link), full changed/created file list with absolute paths, and verification output.

content.text:
Sol dispatched with the full brief. Design locked in the brief:

- **≤1 tab** → single folded dot next to name (one dot even if the tab is split into panes); no chips.
- **2+ tabs** → name dots gone; per-tab chips in `paneLayout.tabs[]` order, always expanded, agent icon (binding join; terminal icon for shell tabs) with existing corner-badge dot, label = real tab title truncated with tooltip, flex-wrap rows.
- **Click** → existing deep-link pattern (`terminalId`/`chatSessionId` + `focusRequestId`), new guarded `tabId` param only for tabs with no domain-id pane.
- Preserves CHIP-DOT-UNIFY invariant, DOT-PERSIST, icon rollup, ports pill.

Will review Sol's diff when it lands, add the `(TAB-CHIPS)` FEATURES.md row, commit to main, then CI build.

content: Redesigning sidebar so 2+ tab workspaces show always-expanded per-tab chips (icon, badge dot, real tab names, tab order) instead of name dots. Sol is implementing now; next I review its diff, commit, and ship via CI build. (disable recaps in /config)

Another Claude session sent a message:
<teammate-message teammate_id="sol-tab-chips" color="blue" summary="TAB-CHIPS implemented and scoped checks pass">
Implemented (TAB-CHIPS) without committing/pushing or touching `.github/workflows`, `FEATURES.md`, or `AGENTS.md`.

Design decisions:

`workspace-agents-row.ts` is a persisted user-facing toggle (ExperimentalSettings, default on). Old behavior with flag off still showed the name-line dots; new behavior shows nothing for ≥2 tabs. Fix (one-liner shape): in `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceItem.tsx`, read `useWorkspaceAgentsRowEnabled()`; when off, pass `tabCount={Math.min(tabChips.length, 1)}` and `tabStatus` = highest-priority fold across ALL `tabChips` statuses (not just `tabChips[0]`), so the flag-off world keeps a single folded dot.

- **Spec fidelity**: ≤1 tab → single folded `StatusIndicator` inline (fold across panes via `getV2NotificationSourcesForTab` → `getHighestPriorityDisplayStatus`); ≥2 tabs → no name dots, chips in exact `paneLayout.tabs[]` order (array order preserved end-to-end), always expanded (zero `details-expanded:` on the chip — label always `max-w-28 truncate`), corner badge `absolute -top-0.5 -right-0.5` hidden when no status, real tab titles + full-title Radix tooltip, `flex flex-wrap` row, shell tabs get `TerminalSquare` chips, click → `navigateToV2Workspace` with `terminalId`/`chatSessionId`/`tabId` + fresh `focusRequestId`.
