content.content: Updated task #2 status
Updated task #3 status

50	| Untagged background-agent tool completions never stomp the red | teammate/fork tool completions (ordinary Read/Bash/Edit/…) stream in attributed to the PARENT session WITHOUT an agent_id, so the SUBTOOL-RED/ASYNC-TOOL-RED checks miss them and they would Start-clear a pending AskUserQuestion red. A `<terminalId>.askq/` DIR holds one marker PER OWNER (the raising agent_id, or `_main`), set synchronously at PreToolUse:AskUserQuestion; while ANY marker exists an untagged ordinary PostToolUse maps to the red-respecting SubagentActive (and refreshes `.bgactive`, since the blocked main loop means that tool is genuine teammate activity). Per-owner so concurrent questions (main + a subagent) are independent. The renderer has ONE permission axis per terminal, so a single CENTRAL GUARD wraps the whole decision and enforces "rendered red ⇔ `.askq` dir non-empty" in one place: it counts owners before and after the inner decision, then (DOWNGRADE) turns any red-CLEARING result (Start, or a green/blue turn-end) into the red-respecting SubagentActive while any owner remains (stamping `.mainstopped` on a held turn-end so the last SubagentStop still finalizes green), and (UPGRADE) turns a NON-clearing result (SubagentActive yellow/agent/codex hold, or a None no-op) into Start when this event removed the LAST owner — so a just-answered/cancelled/reaped question's red can't latch behind a yellow hold. One choke point, so NO branch — answer, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SubagentStop (incl. its cancelled-with-question and still-other-work paths), the compact paths — can drop a co-pending red OR strand a resolved one. (The native-notification manager likewise only chimes "complete" on Stop and "awaiting" on permission, never on SubagentActive.) An owner's marker is cleared by its own answer (PostToolUse:AskUserQuestion), its SubagentStop, or the main turn boundary (Stop/UserPromptSubmit clear only `_main`); dead non-`_main` owners are reaped against `background_tasks` ids on Stop/SubagentStop (MARKER-RECONCILE-style) so a subagent that died without a clean stop can't pin red; the whole dir clears on session end / API abort / fresh SessionStart (its hook is registered UNSCOPED so the non-compact source is reached), and a main interrupt clears only `_main` (detached teammates survive). The JSONL watcher emits its direct interrupt/abort `Stop` as `SubagentActive` instead while a live `.askq` owner remains (it bypasses the Python guard). Synchronous + per-owner = no timing window and no cross-question clearing; a tool-PERMISSION red (no `.askq` marker) still clears on its approved tool's main-loop Start — no approval-clear regression. SubagentStop of a subagent that stopped WITH its own question still open emits Start (central guard keeps any other owner) so a cancelled subagent question's red can't latch; the watcher's held interrupt also stamps `.mainstopped` so a later SubagentStop still finalizes green; and an askq owner is NEVER reaped from run-marker age alone (a subagent legitimately BLOCKED on an open question has no activity, so age-reaping it would drop a LIVE red) — when `background_tasks` is absent a leaked owner just errs YELLOW until a session/abort/fresh-start boundary clears it. Accepted limits (all inherent to the fire-and-forget hook→POST→last-writer-wins pipeline, not specific to this feature): a generic Notification (tool-permission) red is unguarded (its approval is indistinguishable from a teammate tool without a request id); a sub-ms TOCTOU window (a Start in flight before a question opens); and a stale watcher transcript chunk carrying an old API-abort can clear a question raised during the lag (genuine aborts kill the shared-API subagents, so this is only the narrow abort→recover→new-question-within-one-poll window). Fully closing the last two would need pipeline-wide event sequencing | `(UNTAGGED-BG-RED)` |
51	| Working-cadence events unhooked from notify.sh | upstream's raw `notify.sh` passthrough is NOT registered on Claude's UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse / PostToolUseFailure either (same fix as CLAUDE-STOP-UNHOOKED). The host maps all three to `Start`, and notify.sh bypasses superset-notify.py's central red guard — in a forks/teammates run the forks' PostToolUse completions stream in every 1-5s and each raw `Start` cleared the main loop's pending AskUserQuestion red to yellow. superset-notify.py owns these for Claude (with agent_id discrimination + the guard), so dropping the raw duplicates loses nothing; only binding (SessionStart/SessionEnd) + the native PermissionRequest stay | `(CLAUDE-WORKING-UNHOOKED)` |
52	| Subagent StopFailure stays scoped | a StopFailure carrying an `agent_id` is ONE subagent/fork aborting on a rate-limit/API error, not the whole Claude session dying — it is handled by a SELF-SCOPED inline branch that drops ONLY that fork's run + question markers and never the shared session state (sibling markers, the main `_main` question owner, the agent-bg snapshot), and (like the main-abort branch) never trusts the abort payload's `background_tasks`; the remaining run-marker count + the `.mainstopped` sentinel decide the hold and the central red guard re-holds any still-open question. The full-abort StopFailure branch (clears run_dir + every marker + the `.askq` guard, then greens) would otherwise stomp the main loop's still-pending AskUserQuestion red when a fork hit a limit. Also: superset-notify.py now OWNS `PostToolUseFailure` (aliased to PostToolUse through the red guard) since notify.sh stopped raw-posting it | `(STOPFAIL-SUBAGENT)` |
53	| Layered dot axes | a source's dot status is DERIVED as the highest-precedence active axis (permission > working > review, + the separate blue axes) — events latch/unlatch axes they have evidence about, so a lower assert can never overwrite a higher active state | `applySourceAxes` |
54	| Leaked yellow-hold markers self-heal | a SubagentStop arriving with a mismatched/missing agent_id leaks its run-dir marker and pins the dot yellow with nothing running; at every Stop/SubagentStop the payload's background_tasks[] (ground truth) reaps any marker not listed as still running | `(MARKER-RECONCILE)` |
55	| Subagent markers track per-marker activity | each subagent's run-dir marker is touched on every PostToolUse it fires (not just SubagentStart), so its mtime reflects last activity and the conservative 12h leaked-marker backstop can't false-green a still-working subagent; the COMMON orphan-on-API-error leak is handled fast by (API-ABORT-RELEASE) instead of a flaky short timer | `(MARKER-INACTIVE)` |
56	| Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |
57	| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |
58	| Sidebar agent chips ride the fork dot pipeline | upstream's workspace-agents-row chips (agent logo + status dot under a sidebar row) originally had their own status pipe (host binding raw `lastEventType`, 4-case map that read fork events like SubagentActive/BackgroundRunning as idle) and a broken liveness filter (`terminal_sessions.status` stays `active` forever for a closed-tab pty → chips trapped permission-red for days). Chips now derive status from the shared (AY)/(DOT-AXES) per-source primitive and liveness from the open-pane layout gate — a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |
59	| Nightly self-repairs ReferenceError resolutions | the nightly merge job runs the build's cannot-find-name gate itself (pure-JS `--ignore-scripts` install, linux-safe) right after conflict resolution; on failure Opus gets the exact diagnostics and repairs ONLY those (restore dropped imports/declarations), 2 attempts, re-gated each time, still-dirty aborts as before. Converts the import-dropping-resolution class (desktop-v1.15.1 useChangesTab incident) from red-night+manual-recovery to self-healing; the build's own gate stays as defense in depth | `(REFERR-REPAIR)` |
60	| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |
61	| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet. The missing-worktree error page offers Archive (not Delete) for mains; the delete/complete guards resolve the workspace type host-record-first and FAIL LOUD (refuse) when the type is unresolvable instead of failing open on a cold cache; Empty Recycle Bin defensively skips mains | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |
62	| Recycle Bin (soft delete) | the default "Delete" for a worktree/branch workspace (sidebar + kanban, incl. unbound Queued cards) is now a lossless, recoverable move-to-bin (silent — no dialog/toast; worktree + branch + terminals untouched, exactly like Archive). Restore always returns to ACTIVE; nothing is ever auto-purged. Permanent git destroy is relocated to "Delete permanently" inside the bin (the old destroy dialog) + a one-confirm "Empty Recycle Bin". A renderer-only retention setting (default 30 days) is a DISPLAY filter only — the bin shows items deleted within the last N days, older ones collapse behind a per-bin "Show all" toggle with an "N hidden by filter" footer. Per-project sidebar "Recycle Bin (N)" section + per-column kanban sub-section, sorted by `deletedAt` DESC. Mains stay archive-only (never deletable) | `(RECYCLE-BIN)` |
63	| Auto-resume stuck chats | when a Claude chat dies on an API failure the JSONL watcher classifies it (deny-list-first: rate-limit-with-reset-time / transient-rate-limit / server-error / connection-drop / half-stop are resumable; auth/policy/bad-image/model-unavailable are notify-only) after a finality/quiescence gate, and a durable MAIN-side registry auto-sends "resume from exactly where everything was left" — fire-at-parsed-reset-time (+30s, DST-correct, fire-now-if-already-past, ≤8d sane cap) for usage limits, else a 60s→×3 backoff capped at 5 sends / 24h. Every send is gated at fire time (toggle on, still the last meaningful transcript line, host-service `commandRunning`=idle + agent-session binding match) so it can never auto-confirm a menu, land mid-turn, or run at a bare shell. Any trusted interaction with that terminal cancels (persisted tombstone survives reload/restart). Account-wide cohorts are jittered + concurrency-capped. Global toggle (default on). Claude-only in v1 — Codex auto-resume is deferred (the on-disk corpus has no validated standalone usage-limit signal). Dots untouched (separate IPC variant + toasts) | `(AUTO-RESUME)`, `autoResumeManager`, `writeInputIfIdleSession`, `useAutoResumeActivity`, `AutoResumeController` |
64	| Dot-decision log + codex pid-reuse cap | superset-notify.py writes an always-on bounded `~/.superset/logs/dot-decisions.log` line at each terminal turn-end decision (the POSTed eventType + WHY a hold won — codex job / background_tasks / subagent markers / GREEN / BLUE / RED), and `_codex_job_active` age-caps pid-bearing `running` codex job records at 6h so a recycled PID can't pin the dot yellow forever (a cap-skipped stale record is logged, so a premature-green is auditable) | `_decision_log` |
65	| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |
66	| Idle teammates green deterministically | Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's background_tasks[] as `status:"running"` FOREVER (captured live: 33 teammate entries, all "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had all finished hours earlier) — and the (BG-STALE) reap only runs inside hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while the set was <15 min idle latched yellow indefinitely (nothing ever re-evaluated; 9 terminals observed stuck). superset-notify.py now keeps a per-terminal teammate LEDGER parsed incrementally from the lead session transcript (payload `transcript_path`; offset+state cached in `<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, keyed by transcript path so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an `idle_notification` teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when EVERY tracked teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped BEFORE the split/snapshot/hold decision — the lead greens (or blues on a shell remainder) at its actual final turn end instead of holding forever. Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates seen / any parse error / any teammate not idle → entries kept (old behaviour + (BG-STALE) unchanged behind it); raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate (forks have real hook lifecycles and would never idle-notify); subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through untouched; bg_ids marker reconciliation still uses the RAW list. Decision log carries a `[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]` suffix for auditability | `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)` |
67	| ReferenceError-class build gate | the build (and therefore every nightly, whose build job calls the same workflow) fails on cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics ONLY (TS2304/2552/2662/2663/18004) — bare identifiers esbuild bundles fine but that throw ReferenceError at runtime, exactly what a CLEAN-merging upstream hunk can leave in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx incident: upstream renamed the workspace source variable in a non-conflicting hunk; fork's cache-first hold kept referencing it; every v2 workspace view crashed). Per-package direct tsc (turbo graph is debt-blocked); the accepted type debt has zero of these codes so the gate can't false-abort | `(REFERR-GATE)` |
68	| Nightly dependency-consistency + lock regen | the nightly merge can introduce dependency-state drift that only the build discovers (after a windows-arm64 runner spins up): a stale fork root `overrides` entry that pins a package to a version different from the merged workspace pin (electron-builder "production dependency not found …"), or a `bun.lock` left stale by an upstream package.json bump (frozen-install fails). The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). No full `--frozen-lockfile` install runs here: the merge runner is linux, so a platform-specific install quirk would be a false abort, and the regenerated lock is consistent with the manifests by construction. The fork carries NO mastra `overrides` and pins `@mastra/core`/`mastracode` to upstream's exact versions in all three workspaces (upstream keeps mastra consistent via identical exact pins; the seed commit's redundant override + a recovery merge that resolved the pins DOWN to 0.18.0 were the recurring breakage, both corrected) | `(LOCK-REGEN)` |
69	| Fork-owned workflow dir | `.github/workflows/` carries ONLY the fork's own workflows — upstream CI files were dropped at fork creation (they target upstream's infra/secrets) and the Actions `GITHUB_TOKEN` can NEVER push a workflow-file change anyway (no `workflows` scope exists for it: "refusing to allow a GitHub App to create or update workflow"). Upstream desktop-v1.14.3 added `deploy-relay.yml`, which merged CLEANLY and then killed the candidate push after every gate had already passed. The nightly now deterministically restores the fork's exact workflow set mid-merge (`git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows`) BEFORE conflict detection, so upstream workflow additions/edits never enter the fork, never reach the AI resolver, and never hit the push restriction — and the dropped upstream CI delta is SURFACED, not silently erased (job summary + log + handed to the semantic review, which appends advisory `CI-NOTE:` lines when upstream moves build-relevant logic into its CI), with a pre-commit postcondition proving the committed tree carries zero workflow-dir changes. Wanting an upstream workflow = deliberate local commit with a user token | `(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED)` |
70	| Nightly pipeline integrity | three cheap deterministic guards on the merge→build→advance hand-offs: a fatal (non-conflict) `git merge` failure is caught via missing MERGE_HEAD instead of masquerading as a clean merge (the `set +e` wrapper accepts every nonzero exit); the build + advance jobs consume the merge commit's immutable SHA — not the mutable `nightly/*` branch name — under a workflow-level `concurrency` group (queue, never cancel), and advance refuses to fast-forward main if the candidate ref moved; `gh release create` tags the exact built commit via `--target` + a post-publish remote-tag assert (without `--target` gh tags the DEFAULT branch tip, which during a nightly is pre-advance main — every nightly-published release tag pointed one commit behind the installer's true source until 2026-07-11) | `(NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY)` |

| Untagged background-agent tool completions never stomp the red | teammate/fork tool completions (ordinary Read/Bash/Edit/…) stream in attributed to the PARENT session WITHOUT an agent_id, so the SUBTOOL-RED/ASYNC-TOOL-RED checks miss them and they would Start-clear a pending AskUserQuestion red. A `<terminalId>.askq/` DIR holds one marker PER OWNER (the raising agent_id, or `_main`), set synchronously at PreToolUse:AskUserQuestion; while ANY marker exists an untagged ordinary PostToolUse maps to the red-respecting SubagentActive (and refreshes `.bgactive`, since the blocked main loop means that tool is genuine teammate activity). Per-owner so concurrent questions (main + a subagent) are independent. The renderer has ONE permission axis per terminal, so a single CENTRAL GUARD wraps the whole decision and enforces "rendered red ⇔ `.askq` dir non-empty" in one place: it counts owners before and after the inner decision, then (DOWNGRADE) turns any red-CLEARING result (Start, or a green/blue turn-end) into the red-respecting SubagentActive while any owner remains (stamping `.mainstopped` on a held turn-end so the last SubagentStop still finalizes green), and (UPGRADE) turns a NON-clearing result (SubagentActive yellow/agent/codex hold, or a None no-op) into Start when this event removed the LAST owner — so a just-answered/cancelled/reaped question's red can't latch behind a yellow hold. One choke point, so NO branch — answer, UserPromptSubmit, Stop, SubagentStop (incl. its cancelled-with-question and still-other-work paths), the compact paths — can drop a co-pending red OR strand a resolved one. (The native-notification manager likewise only chimes "complete" on Stop and "awaiting" on permission, never on SubagentActive.) An owner's marker is cleared by its own answer (PostToolUse:AskUserQuestion), its SubagentStop, or the main turn boundary (Stop/UserPromptSubmit clear only `_main`); dead non-`_main` owners are reaped against `background_tasks` ids on Stop/SubagentStop (MARKER-RECONCILE-style) so a subagent that died without a clean stop can't pin red; the whole dir clears on session end / API abort / fresh SessionStart (its hook is registered UNSCOPED so the non-compact source is reached), and a main interrupt clears only `_main` (detached teammates survive). The JSONL watcher emits its direct interrupt/abort `Stop` as `SubagentActive` instead while a live `.askq` owner remains (it bypasses the Python guard). Synchronous + per-owner = no timing window and no cross-question clearing; a tool-PERMISSION red (no `.askq` marker) still clears on its approved tool's main-loop Start — no approval-clear regression. SubagentStop of a subagent that stopped WITH its own question still open emits Start (central guard keeps any other owner) so a cancelled subagent question's red can't latch; the watcher's held interrupt also stamps `.mainstopped` so a later SubagentStop still finalizes green; and an askq owner is NEVER reaped from run-marker age alone (a subagent legitimately BLOCKED on an open question has no activity, so age-reaping it would drop a LIVE red) — when `background_tasks` is absent a leaked owner just errs YELLOW until a session/abort/fresh-start boundary clears it. Accepted limits (all inherent to the fire-and-forget hook→POST→last-writer-wins pipeline, not specific to this feature): a generic Notification (tool-permission) red is unguarded (its approval is indistinguishable from a teammate tool without a request id); a sub-ms TOCTOU window (a Start in flight before a question opens); and a stale watcher transcript chunk carrying an old API-abort can clear a question raised during the lag (genuine aborts kill the shared-API subagents, so this is only the narrow abort→recover→new-question-within-one-poll window). Fully closing the last two would need pipeline-wide event sequencing | `(UNTAGGED-BG-RED)` |
| Working-cadence events unhooked from notify.sh | upstream's raw `notify.sh` passthrough is NOT registered on Claude's UserPromptSubmit / PostToolUse / PostToolUseFailure either (same fix as CLAUDE-STOP-UNHOOKED). The host maps all three to `Start`, and notify.sh bypasses superset-notify.py's central red guard — in a forks/teammates run the forks' PostToolUse completions stream in every 1-5s and each raw `Start` cleared the main loop's pending AskUserQuestion red to yellow. superset-notify.py owns these for Claude (with agent_id discrimination + the guard), so dropping the raw duplicates loses nothing; only binding (SessionStart/SessionEnd) + the native PermissionRequest stay | `(CLAUDE-WORKING-UNHOOKED)` |
| Subagent StopFailure stays scoped | a StopFailure carrying an `agent_id` is ONE subagent/fork aborting on a rate-limit/API error, not the whole Claude session dying — it is handled by a SELF-SCOPED inline branch that drops ONLY that fork's run + question markers and never the shared session state (sibling markers, the main `_main` question owner, the agent-bg snapshot), and (like the main-abort branch) never trusts the abort payload's `background_tasks`; the remaining run-marker count + the `.mainstopped` sentinel decide the hold and the central red guard re-holds any still-open question. The full-abort StopFailure branch (clears run_dir + every marker + the `.askq` guard, then greens) would otherwise stomp the main loop's still-pending AskUserQuestion red when a fork hit a limit. Also: superset-notify.py now OWNS `PostToolUseFailure` (aliased to PostToolUse through the red guard) since notify.sh stopped raw-posting it | `(STOPFAIL-SUBAGENT)` |
| Layered dot axes | a source's dot status is DERIVED as the highest-precedence active axis (permission > working > review, + the separate blue axes) — events latch/unlatch axes they have evidence about, so a lower assert can never overwrite a higher active state | `applySourceAxes` |
| Leaked yellow-hold markers self-heal | a SubagentStop arriving with a mismatched/missing agent_id leaks its run-dir marker and pins the dot yellow with nothing running; at every Stop/SubagentStop the payload's background_tasks[] (ground truth) reaps any marker not listed as still running | `(MARKER-RECONCILE)` |
| Subagent markers track per-marker activity | each subagent's run-dir marker is touched on every PostToolUse it fires (not just SubagentStart), so its mtime reflects last activity and the conservative 12h leaked-marker backstop can't false-green a still-working subagent; the COMMON orphan-on-API-error leak is handled fast by (API-ABORT-RELEASE) instead of a flaky short timer | `(MARKER-INACTIVE)` |
| Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |
| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |
| Sidebar agent chips ride the fork dot pipeline | upstream's workspace-agents-row chips (agent logo + status dot under a sidebar row) originally had their own status pipe (host binding raw `lastEventType`, 4-case map that read fork events like SubagentActive/BackgroundRunning as idle) and a broken liveness filter (`terminal_sessions.status` stays `active` forever for a closed-tab pty → chips trapped permission-red for days). Chips now derive status from the shared (AY)/(DOT-AXES) per-source primitive and liveness from the open-pane layout gate — a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |
| Nightly self-repairs ReferenceError resolutions | the nightly merge job runs the build's cannot-find-name gate itself (pure-JS `--ignore-scripts` install, linux-safe) right after conflict resolution; on failure Opus gets the exact diagnostics and repairs ONLY those (restore dropped imports/declarations), 2 attempts, re-gated each time, still-dirty aborts as before. Converts the import-dropping-resolution class (desktop-v1.15.1 useChangesTab incident) from red-night+manual-recovery to self-healing; the build's own gate stays as defense in depth | `(REFERR-REPAIR)` |
| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |
| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet. The missing-worktree error page offers Archive (not Delete) for mains; the delete/complete guards resolve the workspace type host-record-first and FAIL LOUD (refuse) when the type is unresolvable instead of failing open on a cold cache; Empty Recycle Bin defensively skips mains | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |
| Recycle Bin (soft delete) | the default "Delete" for a worktree/branch workspace (sidebar + kanban, incl. unbound Queued cards) is now a lossless, recoverable move-to-bin (silent — no dialog/toast; worktree + branch + terminals untouched, exactly like Archive). Restore always returns to ACTIVE; nothing is ever auto-purged. Permanent git destroy is relocated to "Delete permanently" inside the bin (the old destroy dialog) + a one-confirm "Empty Recycle Bin". A renderer-only retention setting (default 30 days) is a DISPLAY filter only — the bin shows items deleted within the last N days, older ones collapse behind a per-bin "Show all" toggle with an "N hidden by filter" footer. Per-project sidebar "Recycle Bin (N)" section + per-column kanban sub-section, sorted by `deletedAt` DESC. Mains stay archive-only (never deletable) | `(RECYCLE-BIN)` |
| Auto-resume stuck chats | when a Claude chat dies on an API failure the JSONL watcher classifies it (deny-list-first: rate-limit-with-reset-time / transient-rate-limit / server-error / connection-drop / half-stop are resumable; auth/policy/bad-image/model-unavailable are notify-only) after a finality/quiescence gate, and a durable MAIN-side registry auto-sends "resume from exactly where everything was left" — fire-at-parsed-reset-time (+30s, DST-correct, fire-now-if-already-past, ≤8d sane cap) for usage limits, else a 60s→×3 backoff capped at 5 sends / 24h. Every send is gated at fire time (toggle on, still the last meaningful transcript line, host-service `commandRunning`=idle + agent-session binding match) so it can never auto-confirm a menu, land mid-turn, or run at a bare shell. Any trusted interaction with that terminal cancels (persisted tombstone survives reload/restart). Account-wide cohorts are jittered + concurrency-capped. Global toggle (default on). Claude-only in v1 — Codex auto-resume is deferred (the on-disk corpus has no validated standalone usage-limit signal). Dots untouched (separate IPC variant + toasts) | `(AUTO-RESUME)`, `autoResumeManager`, `writeInputIfIdleSession`, `useAutoResumeActivity`, `AutoResumeController` |
| Dot-decision log + codex pid-reuse cap | superset-notify.py writes an always-on bounded `~/.superset/logs/dot-decisions.log` line at each terminal turn-end decision (the POSTed eventType + WHY a hold won — codex job / background_tasks / subagent markers / GREEN / BLUE / RED), and `_codex_job_active` age-caps pid-bearing `running` codex job records at 6h so a recycled PID can't pin the dot yellow forever (a cap-skipped stale record is logged, so a premature-green is auditable) | `_decision_log` |
| Idle/zombie teammate hold reaped | an agent set Claude Code still reports `running` in background_tasks[] but that has produced NO activity (SubagentStart / subagent-scoped PostToolUse refresh a `.bgactive` timer) for 15 min stops pinning the lead's dot yellow — the harness never flips an idle long-lived teammate (or one that died without a clean SubagentStop) to a finished status, so the hold previously lasted until the team was disbanded / the session ended. The JSONL watcher re-asserts yellow if a reaped set is in fact still writing | `(BG-STALE)` |
| Idle teammates green deterministically | Claude Code reports finished teammates in the Stop payload's background_tasks[] as `status:"running"` FOREVER (captured live: 33 teammate entries, all "running", for a session whose 44 teammates had all finished hours earlier) — and the (BG-STALE) reap only runs inside hook events, so a session whose LAST event landed while the set was <15 min idle latched yellow indefinitely (nothing ever re-evaluated; 9 terminals observed stuck). superset-notify.py now keeps a per-terminal teammate LEDGER parsed incrementally from the lead session transcript (payload `transcript_path`; offset+state cached in `<terminalId>.teamstate.json`, keyed by transcript path so /resume self-invalidates): Agent-spawn / SendMessage-wake / non-idle teammate- or agent-messages mark a name active, an `idle_notification` teammate-message marks it idle. At Stop/SubagentStop, when EVERY tracked teammate is provably idle, unfinished teammate-type entries are dropped BEFORE the split/snapshot/hold decision — the lead greens (or blues on a shell remainder) at its actual final turn end instead of holding forever. Positive proof only: no transcript / no teammates seen / any parse error / any teammate not idle → entries kept (old behaviour + (BG-STALE) unchanged behind it); raw fork agentIds ("a"+hex) are excluded from the predicate (forks have real hook lifecycles and would never idle-notify); subagent/workflow/shell entries always pass through untouched; bg_ids marker reconciliation still uses the RAW list. Decision log carries a `[teammate-idle: dropped N ...]` suffix for auditability | `(TEAMMATE-IDLE)` |
| ReferenceError-class build gate | the build (and therefore every nightly, whose build job calls the same workflow) fails on cannot-find-name tsc diagnostics ONLY (TS2304/2552/2662/2663/18004) — bare identifiers esbuild bundles fine but that throw ReferenceError at runtime, exactly what a CLEAN-merging upstream hunk can leave in fork code the AI resolver never sees (v1.14.0 layout.tsx incident: upstream renamed the workspace source variable in a non-conflicting hunk; fork's cache-first hold kept referencing it; every v2 workspace view crashed). Per-package direct tsc (turbo graph is debt-blocked); the accepted type debt has zero of these codes so the gate can't false-abort | `(REFERR-GATE)` |
| Nightly dependency-consistency + lock regen | the nightly merge can introduce dependency-state drift that only the build discovers (after a windows-arm64 runner spins up): a stale fork root `overrides` entry that pins a package to a version different from the merged workspace pin (electron-builder "production dependency not found …"), or a `bun.lock` left stale by an upstream package.json bump (frozen-install fails). The merge now runs an override-consistency gate (fails loud on any exact override↔pin mismatch) + regenerates `bun.lock` to match the merged manifests with the build's exact bun version (which also fails loud, in the cheap merge job, if the merged manifests don't resolve or a pin is age-blocked) — so this class of build failure is caught/fixed before the windows-arm64 build spins up, baseline untouched on failure (recovery loop). No full `--frozen-lockfile` install runs here: the merge runner is linux, so a platform-specific install quirk would be a false abort, and the regenerated lock is consistent with the manifests by construction. The fork carries NO mastra `overrides` and pins `@mastra/core`/`mastracode` to upstream's exact versions in all three workspaces (upstream keeps mastra consistent via identical exact pins; the seed commit's redundant override + a recovery merge that resolved the pins DOWN to 0.18.0 were the recurring breakage, both corrected) | `(LOCK-REGEN)` |
| Fork-owned workflow dir | `.github/workflows/` carries ONLY the fork's own workflows — upstream CI files were dropped at fork creation (they target upstream's infra/secrets) and the Actions `GITHUB_TOKEN` can NEVER push a workflow-file change anyway (no `workflows` scope exists for it: "refusing to allow a GitHub App to create or update workflow"). Upstream desktop-v1.14.3 added `deploy-relay.yml`, which merged CLEANLY and then killed the candidate push after every gate had already passed. The nightly now deterministically restores the fork's exact workflow set mid-merge (`git checkout --no-overlay HEAD -- .github/workflows`) BEFORE conflict detection, so upstream workflow additions/edits never enter the fork, never reach the AI resolver, and never hit the push restriction — and the dropped upstream CI delta is SURFACED, not silently erased (job summary + log + handed to the semantic review, which appends advisory `CI-NOTE:` lines when upstream moves build-relevant logic into its CI), with a pre-commit postcondition proving the committed tree carries zero workflow-dir changes. Wanting an upstream workflow = deliberate local commit with a user token | `(WORKFLOW-FORK-OWNED)` |
| Nightly pipeline integrity | three cheap deterministic guards on the merge→build→advance hand-offs: a fatal (non-conflict) `git merge` failure is caught via missing MERGE_HEAD instead of masquerading as a clean merge (the `set +e` wrapper accepts every nonzero exit); the build + advance jobs consume the merge commit's immutable SHA — not the mutable `nightly/*` branch name — under a workflow-level `concurrency` group (queue, never cancel), and advance refuses to fast-forward main if the candidate ref moved; `gh release create` tags the exact built commit via `--target` + a post-publish remote-tag assert (without `--target` gh tags the DEFAULT branch tip, which during a nightly is pre-advance main — every nightly-published release tag pointed one commit behind the installer's true source until 2026-07-11) | `(NIGHTLY-INTEGRITY)` |

input.old_string: a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |
input.new_string:
a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |
| Sidebar per-tab chips | a sidebar workspace row with 2+ open tabs replaces its inline name-line dots with one chip PER TAB — always expanded (no hover-collapse), in exact `paneLayout.tabs[]` order, labelled with the real tab title (override → pane override → per-kind default → `Tab N`; `max-w-28` truncate + full-title tooltip), agent icon from the tab's terminal-pane binding (active pane first, generic terminal glyph for plain-shell tabs), status dot as a corner badge on the icon folded from the tab's pane sources via the shared display-status primitive, flex-wrap rows. ≤1 tab keeps the upstream look: a single folded name dot, no chips. Chip click deep-links to the workspace and focuses that tab (`terminalId`/`chatSessionId`, or a validated `tabId` param for tabs with no domain-id pane, guarded on pane-layout readiness + tab existence). With the workspace-agents-row experiment off, the row falls back to a single name dot folded across ALL tabs (never zero status surfaces) | `(TAB-CHIPS)` |

toolUseResult.oldString: a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |
toolUseResult.newString:
a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |
| Sidebar per-tab chips | a sidebar workspace row with 2+ open tabs replaces its inline name-line dots with one chip PER TAB — always expanded (no hover-collapse), in exact `paneLayout.tabs[]` order, labelled with the real tab title (override → pane override → per-kind default → `Tab N`; `max-w-28` truncate + full-title tooltip), agent icon from the tab's terminal-pane binding (active pane first, generic terminal glyph for plain-shell tabs), status dot as a corner badge on the icon folded from the tab's pane sources via the shared display-status primitive, flex-wrap rows. ≤1 tab keeps the upstream look: a single folded name dot, no chips. Chip click deep-links to the workspace and focuses that tab (`terminalId`/`chatSessionId`, or a validated `tabId` param for tabs with no domain-id pane, guarded on pane-layout readiness + tab existence). With the workspace-agents-row experiment off, the row falls back to a single name dot folded across ALL tabs (never zero status surfaces) | `(TAB-CHIPS)` |

lines: | Orphaned subagent markers reaped on API abort | a main Claude session's stream abort (`API Error: Stream idle timeout - partial response received`, an isApiErrorMessage line) ends the turn and ORPHANS in-flight subagents WITHOUT firing their SubagentStop, leaking run-dir markers that pin the dot yellow; no hook fires on the abort, so the JSONL watcher (the only thing that reads the transcript) reaps the orphaned per-subagent markers and emits review — deterministic, event-driven, no timer (survivors stay held via the next Stop's background_tasks[]) | `(API-ABORT-RELEASE)` |

| Dot state survives renderer reloads | the v2-notifications dot store persists to sessionStorage — an in-place window reload (Ctrl+R / error boundary / crash recovery) no longer wipes every dot; the background-running blue has no self-heal until the next turn end, so a reload used to hide a running background task for hours. Clears on real app restart (no stale dots across launches) | `(DOT-PERSIST)` |

| Sidebar agent chips ride the fork dot pipeline | upstream's workspace-agents-row chips (agent logo + status dot under a sidebar row) originally had their own status pipe (host binding raw `lastEventType`, 4-case map that read fork events like SubagentActive/BackgroundRunning as idle) and a broken liveness filter (`terminal_sessions.status` stays `active` forever for a closed-tab pty → chips trapped permission-red for days). Chips now derive status from the shared (AY)/(DOT-AXES) per-source primitive and liveness from the open-pane layout gate — a closed tab's chip is unrepresentable, and a chip can never disagree with the tab/pane/rollup dots | `(CHIP-DOT-UNIFY)` |

+| Sidebar per-tab chips | a sidebar workspace row with 2+ open tabs replaces its inline name-line dots with one chip PER TAB — always expanded (no hover-collapse), in exact `paneLayout.tabs[]` order, labelled with the real tab title (override → pane override → per-kind default → `Tab N`; `max-w-28` truncate + full-title tooltip), agent icon from the tab's terminal-pane binding (active pane first, generic terminal glyph for plain-shell tabs), status dot as a corner badge on the icon folded from the tab's pane sources via the shared display-status primitive, flex-wrap rows. ≤1 tab keeps the upstream look: a single folded name dot, no chips. Chip click deep-links to the workspace and focuses that tab (`terminalId`/`chatSessionId`, or a validated `tabId` param for tabs with no domain-id pane, guarded on pane-layout readiness + tab existence). With the workspace-agents-row experiment off, the row falls back to a single name dot folded across ALL tabs (never zero status surfaces) | `(TAB-CHIPS)` |

| Nightly self-repairs ReferenceError resolutions | the nightly merge job runs the build's cannot-find-name gate itself (pure-JS `--ignore-scripts` install, linux-safe) right after conflict resolution; on failure Opus gets the exact diagnostics and repairs ONLY those (restore dropped imports/declarations), 2 attempts, re-gated each time, still-dirty aborts as before. Converts the import-dropping-resolution class (desktop-v1.15.1 useChangesTab incident) from red-night+manual-recovery to self-healing; the build's own gate stays as defense in depth | `(REFERR-REPAIR)` |

| Agent hook fork-diet | every agent lifecycle hook parses + escapes JSON with bash builtins (read, `[[ =~ ]]`, parameter expansion); the old grep/tr + sed pipelines forked ~30 subprocesses per call and crashed the x64-emulated msys2 runtime on Windows ARM64 (the add_item errno-1 fork cascade that wedged every chat's hooks). Wire payload unchanged | `(HOOK-FORK-DIET)` |

| Master cards archive-only | a repo's master / non-git master card (`type === "main"`) can never be hard-removed from the sidebar — "Remove from Sidebar" archives it (recoverable under the project's Archived section) instead of hiding it into oblivion. `archiveWorkspace` inserts an archived row for an auto-included main that has none yet. The missing-worktree error page offers Archive (not Delete) for mains; the delete/complete guards resolve the workspace type host-record-first and FAIL LOUD (refuse) when the type is unresolvable instead of failing open on a cold cache; Empty Recycle Bin defensively skips mains | `(MASTER-ARCHIVE-ONLY)` |

Angle: React/state correctness in the new/changed renderer code.
- Rules of hooks: every new hook (`useV2WorkspaceTabChips`, `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceTabChips`) unconditionally mounted; enabled/disabled degradation paths never change hook call counts across renders.

- Memoization: useMemo dep arrays complete and minimal; unstable identities passed as props causing child re-renders; `crypto.randomUUID()` calls placed so they don't run per-render.
- Stale closures in click handlers/effects; effect cleanup in the tabId deep-link consumer (component unmount mid-wait for layout readiness).
- The sidebar renders MANY workspace rows — quantify any per-row cost regressions vs the deleted useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceRunningAgents path.

The user-approved spec: ≤1 open tab → single folded name dot, no chips; ≥2 tabs → NO name dots, one chip per tab in exact `paneLayout.tabs[]` order, always expanded, agent icon with corner status badge, label = real tab title (~max-w-28 truncate + full-title tooltip), flex-wrap, shell tabs included (dot only when status), chip click focuses workspace + that tab.

Hunt these edges in the actual code:
- 0 tabs; corrupted/empty paneLayout row missing; `sanitizePaneLayout` healing mid-render; workspace never opened in v2 (no local-state row at all) — chips/dots behavior in each.
- 1 tab with MULTIPLE panes (split): exactly ONE name dot, folded highest-priority across all panes incl. chat panes (`chat:<sessionId>`).

- Chip click while the target tab was JUST closed (paneLayout raced) — navigation lands, focus no-ops silently? acceptable?
- Very many tabs (10+): flex-wrap growth pushes sidebar row content — any max-height/overflow handling; and label truncation actually bounded (`truncate` requires min-w-0/flex constraints — verify the chip's flex context actually truncates rather than overflowing).
- Tooltip content correctness (full title + status text), and the corner badge hidden-when-idle logic (shell tab with shell-running blue must SHOW).
- Kanban-mode sidebar rows (`onKanban` click branch) — do chips render there and does chip click's navigate conflict with kanban routing?

Review of commit 5fd855071: implementation invariants are clean; one LOW regression-protection finding.

message.content:
Another Claude session sent a message:
<teammate-message teammate_id="sol-review-edges" color="purple" summary="Found three TAB-CHIPS behavior issues">
Review of 5fd855071 found 3 behavioral issues. (1) HIGH: sidebar titles are not the real tab titles. `resolveDashboardSidebarTabTitle.ts:6-14,28-32` hardcodes Browser/Comment/Terminal and the descriptor omits browser pageTitle/url, comment authorLogin, and terminal live title. Multiple browser tabs all read “Browser” although the tab bar shows page title/host; comment tabs show “Comment”; terminal live titles are lost. Empty pane override also diverges: actual `useTabTitle` falls to `Tab N`, this resolver falls to kind/file title. Fix: derive/subcribe through the same title semantics/data as `useTabTitle` (including titleSource/static data), not a parallel hardcoded map. (2) MED: `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceTabChip.tsx:27-35` always calls `navigateToV2Workspace`, bypassing the established `/kanban?cardId=` collapse-split branch in `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceItemActions.ts:78-108`; chips render on Kanban and clicking one exits the board rather than focusing the mounted split tab. Fix: make chip navigation Kanban-aware and pass the focus request into `V2WorkspaceMount`/`V2WorkspaceView`. (3) MED: stale terminal/chat chip click can resurrect a just-closed tab. `getFocusTarget` chooses terminalId/chatSessionId (`useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceTabChips.ts:24-37`), and consumers add a tab when no pane exists (`useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts:93,121`); chat sessions persist, so a close→immediate chip click reliably reopens chat rather than stale no-op. Fix: include/validate tabId with domain focus requests, abort if that tab no longer exists. Verified clean: 0/missing/corrupt layouts yield no chips/name dot; one split tab folds all terminal+chat sources into one name dot; file/browser/diff fallback+tabId path; chat uses sessionId; Windows basename; array order/reorder fallback index; drag moves (does not duplicate) and React key is tabId; wrapping/truncation/tooltip/status badge including shell blue.
</teammate-message>

message.content:
Another Claude session sent a message:
<teammate-message teammate_id="sol-review-react" color="green" summary="Found pane-layout hydration race">
React/state review of 5fd855071: one HIGH finding.

1. HIGH — `C:\Users\khair\Code Projects\fork-superset-windows-arm64\apps\desktop\src\renderer\routes\_authenticated\_dashboard\v2-workspace\$workspaceId\hooks\useConsumeAutomationRunLink\useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts:70-93,103-121` (with persistence consequence at `...\hooks\useV2WorkspacePaneLayout\useV2WorkspacePaneLayout.ts:81-100`). Offending code: `if (!terminalId) return; if (!terminalSessionsQuery.isSuccess) return; ... focusOrAddTerminalPane(store, terminalId);` and the equivalent chat effect, neither checking the newly supplied `paneLayoutReady`. Failure scenario: click a terminal/chat chip for a different workspace while its local pane-layout live query is still hydrating, but the terminal query or chat collection is already ready/cached. The store still contains `EMPTY_STATE`; `focusOrAdd*` adds a fresh one-pane tab instead of finding the existing pane. The pane-layout subscription may persist that replacement over the DB row, or later hydration replaces it and `consumedRef` prevents retry, so the chip fails to focus. Fix: gate terminal and chat effects on `paneLayoutReady` too and add it to both dependency arrays (the layout replacement effect was registered earlier and will run first once ready).

Verified clean: new hooks are unconditionally mounted; enabled/disabled paths retain hook counts and use constant selectors/no-match query safely. `useLiveQuery` dependencies are primitive/stable and paneLayout replacement updates titles/order. The comma display-key selector re-renders exactly on per-tab status changes and avoids object/array selector storms. Memo deps and `randomUUID()` placement are correct; handlers have no stale closures. Tab effect has no asynchronous resource needing cleanup; unmount before readiness is safe.

Per-row cost versus deleted path: no regression in subscription counts. Collapsed remains 2 local live queries + 2 Zustand selectors. Expanded drops from 4 to 3 local live queries, remains 3 Zustand selectors, and retains 1 host binding query. New tab selectors scan only the row's tab sources rather than all global terminal sources/blue maps.
</teammate-message>

input.message:
xhigh review round on commit 5fd855071 pooled 4 real items. Apply these fixes (edit only, no commit/push, same constraints as before):

A. HIGH — `useConsumeAutomationRunLink.ts`: the terminal effect (L70-101) and chat effect (L103-129) must gate on `paneLayoutReady` (early return + add to dep arrays) exactly like the tab effect. Failure today: deep-link lands while pane-layout live query is still hydrating but terminal/chat query is ready → store is EMPTY_STATE → `focusOrAdd*` creates a duplicate one-pane tab and the layout subscription can persist it over the DB row; `consumedRef` blocks retry.

B. MED — chips always focus by tabId: in `useDashboardSidebarWorkspaceTabChips.ts`, replace `getFocusTarget` with a constant `{ type: "tab", tabId }` target (delete the terminal/chat branches + the now-unneeded union members if nothing else uses them) and update `DashboardSidebarWorkspaceTabChip.tsx` click to pass ONLY `tabId` + `focusRequestId` search params. Rationale: the tabId consumer is guarded (readiness + tab existence, setActiveTab only, no add) — this kills the stale-chip-click-resurrects-closed-chat-tab class and matches the spec semantic "focus that tab". Automation-run links (terminalId/chatSessionId) keep their focusOrAdd behavior — they're for sessions that may not have panes yet.

C. MED — richer static titles: check the browser and comment pane data types in `$workspaceId/types.ts` (`PaneViewerData` union); extend `V2WorkspaceTabPaneDescriptor` + the store.ts descriptor builder (L857-864) + `resolveDashboardSidebarTabTitle.ts` with whatever static fields exist: browser → page title if stored in pane data, else URL host (strip scheme/www) as the default instead of "Browser"; comment → author login/name if present, else keep "Comment". Only surface data actually persisted in `pane.data` — do NOT reach into route-scoped runtime registries. Update the resolver tests for the new cases (incl. malformed/missing url).

D. LOW — behavioral tests for the deep-link hook: a test that exercises the effects against a minimal store proving (1) no focus/add before `paneLayoutReady` for all three link types, (2) tabId link: missing tab → no-op + NOT consumed (retries when tab appears), existing tab → exactly one `setActiveTab`, same focusRequestId not re-consumed, new focusRequestId re-consumed, (3) terminal link after readiness still focusOrAdds. Use whatever test seam is practical (extract pure effect-body helpers if mounting hooks under bun-test is impractical — keep behavior identical).

NOT doing: kanban-aware chip click (chip = open workspace + that tab, by spec) and live terminal titles in sidebar (route-scoped runtime, accepted).

Verify: scoped `bunx biome check` on touched files, `bun test` colocated suites, no NEW tsc errors, LF audit. Report changed files + results.

content.subject: Explore sidebar chip/tab/nav code (3 Opus + 1 base agent)
content.description: Map rendering (row dots + chips + hover-expand), data (tab names/order, per-source status primitive), navigation (focus workspace + specific tab). Output feeds Sol's implementation brief.

content.subject: Write Sol brief + Sol implements sidebar tab-chips feature
content.description: Spec: 1 tab → single dot next to workspace name, no chips. 2+ tabs → no name dots; per-tab chips always expanded, agent icon with status-dot badge on icon top-right corner, label = actual tab name truncated ~18ch with tooltip, chips wrap rows, tab order = tab strip order, shell tabs get chip (terminal icon, no dot unless status), chip click opens workspace + that tab. Sol edits only, no commit.

content.subject: Review, marker, commit, push, /code-review low
content.description: Fable review of Sol's diff; add FEATURES.md marker row in same commit (every feature needs a marker); LF-only staged blobs (0 CR); commit direct to main; push; run /code-review low.

content.subject: Build via CI + notify user
content.description: Ship via full gh build (--ref main), never hot-patch. Monitor run; on green ask user (AskUserQuestion) re install timing — never install on own initiative.

content.subject: Inspect tab-chip integration points
content.description: Read the notification store, sidebar components/hooks, pane types, and navigation consumer needed for TAB-CHIPS.

content.subject: Implement tab-chip data and UI
content.description: Add ordered tab models/status folding, sidebar title and binding hook, chip UI, row-dot behavior, and tabId navigation.

content.subject: Verify tab-chip changes
content.description: Run colocated tests, scoped Biome over every changed file, and scoped/practical TypeScript validation; report only introduced errors.

content.subject: Harden tab deep-link behavior
content.description: Gate all automation link effects on pane-layout readiness and make sidebar chips navigate only by tabId.

content.subject: Enrich static tab titles
content.description: Add persisted browser page-title/URL and comment-author fields to descriptors, resolver, and tests.

content.subject: Test deep-link readiness semantics
content.description: Add behavioral tests covering readiness gates, tab retry/dedup, and terminal focus-or-add.

content.subject: Verify xhigh review fixes
content.description: Run scoped Biome, relevant Bun tests, TypeScript comparison, LF audit, and diff checks.

content.text: main.log doesn't capture the child's stderr. Windows Event Log will name the faulting module — querying:

input.command: Get-WinEvent -FilterHashtable @{LogName='Application'; ProviderName='Application Error'; StartTime=(Get-Date).AddHours(-6)} -MaxEvents 20 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object { $_.TimeCreated.ToString('HH:mm:ss') + ' | ' + (($_.Message -split "`n" | Select-String 'Faulting application name|Faulting module name|Exception code|Faulting application path|Faulting module path') -join ' | ') }
input.description: Query Application Error events for faulting module of the crash

content.content: Exit code 1

The table is managed outside this repo\'s `migrations/`, so there is intentionally no migration file.\\n\\n**Server** (`specials_ordering_server.py`)\\n- `set_specials_price` takes + validates `pack_size` (required, fails loud) and writes it.\\n- `fetch_specials_ready_to_endorse` + `fetch_specials_history` select + return it.\\n- `send_back_to_outstanding` clears it alongside price/invoice/batch.\\n- Endorse/history field mapping factored into a shared `_apply_supplier_detail_fields` helper.\\n\\n**Client** (3 specials tables)\\n- New required **Pack size** input on the outstanding receipt row; Save stays disabled until it\'s filled.\\n- New filterable **Pack Size** column in the endorse + history tables.\\n- The **Total ex VAT** (price) column is now also **filterable** in the endorse + history tables (it previously had no filter, unlike invoice/batch/pack).\\n- **\\"Price\\" → \\"Total ex VAT\\"** across header, placeholder, and responsive `data-label` in all three tables.\\n\\n> Note: \\"manufacturer\\" from the original request is the existing **Supplier** dropdown — nothing added there.\\n\\n## Review\\n- `/code-review max` (10 finder angles + verify + sweep): no correctness bugs.\\n- `/simplify max`: shared helper + arg-order alignment + completed the relabel.\\n- `/pr-swarm-review`: validated every comment from all 8 review bots (CodeRabbit, CodeAnt, Qodo, Codacy, Gemini, Kilo, Claude, Codex) — no valid code issues (all were unreachable guards, forbidden defensive shims, house-rule conflicts, or out-of-scope). Applied one approved UX improvement: the price-column filter above. Re-reviewed with `/code-review medium` + `/simplify medium` (clean).\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\\n\\nhttps://claude.ai/code/session_01LW5Y2vPFFBoxds5yzG7Wyi\\n\\n\\n___\\n\\n## **CodeAnt-AI Description**\\nAdd pack size capture and filterable price search on specials receipts\\n\\n### What Changed\\n- The receipt flow now requires a pack size before a price can be saved, and clears it again if the order is moved back to outstanding\\n- Specials tables now show pack size in endorse and history views, and the price column is relabelled to “Total ex VAT” throughout\\n- Endorse and history views can now search by both Total ex VAT and Pack Size\\n- The outstanding entry form now shows the updated “Total ex VAT” label and includes a Pack size input alongside invoice and batch fields\\n\\n### Impact\\n`✅ Fewer incomplete special order receipts`\\n`✅ Clearer price entry for pharmacy staff`\\n`✅ Faster lookup of priced specials by amount or pack size`\\n\\n\\n[🔄 Retrigger CodeAnt AI Review](https://api.codeant.ai/pr/retrigger_review?REDACTED&org=khairm)
💡 Usage Guide\\n\\n### Checking Your Pull Request\\nEvery time you make a pull request, our system automatically looks through it. We check for security issues, mistakes in how you\'re setting up your infrastructure, and common code problems. We do this to make sure your changes are solid and won\'t cause any trouble later.\\n\\n### Talking to CodeAnt AI\\nGot a question or need a hand with something in your pull request? You can easily get in touch with CodeAnt AI right here. Just type the following in a comment on your pull request, and replace \\"Your question here\\" with whatever you want to ask:\\n
\\n@codeant-ai ask: Your question here\\n
\\nThis lets you have a chat with CodeAnt AI about your pull request, making it easier to understand and improve your code.\\n\\n#### Example\\n
\\n@codeant-ai ask: Can you suggest a safer alternative to storing this secret?\\n
\\n\\n### Preserve Org Learnings with CodeAnt\\nYou can record team preferences so CodeAnt AI applies them in future reviews. Reply directly to the specific CodeAnt AI suggestion (in the same thread) and replace \\"Your feedback here\\" with your input:\\n
\\n@codeant-ai: Your feedback here\\n
\\nThis helps CodeAnt AI learn and adapt to your team\'s coding style and standards.\\n\\n#### Example\\n
\\n@codeant-ai: Do not flag unused imports.\\n
\\n\\n### Retrigger review\\nAsk CodeAnt AI to review the PR again, by typing:\\n
\\n@codeant-ai: review\\n
\\n\\n### Check Your Repository Health\\nTo analyze the health of your code repository, visit our dashboard at [https://app.codeant.ai](https://app.codeant.ai). This tool helps you identify potential issues and areas for improvement in your codebase, ensuring your repository maintains high standards of code health.\\n\\n

Adds an **AIV (Average Item Value = Revenue ÷ Items)** column to the GP monthly summary and per-home drill tables, with responsive column-priority so the table stays readable. **No GP numbers change** — this is display + a net-zero cleanup.\\n\\n## What changed\\n\\n- **AIV column** (`Revenue ÷ Items`) after Revenue in both the monthly table and the per-home drill table. Sortable; em-dash for projected / NuCare-only / zero-item rows (no divide-by-zero).\\n- **Responsive column priority:** when the monthly table\'s container is too narrow (`@container ≤1300px`, viewport ≥769px) the two NuCare detail columns (`NC Rev` + `NC Cost`, class `gp-nc-detail`) drop out so AIV fits; **NC Profit stays** as the summary. The `<769px` card layout shows every field (vertical room).\\n- **Drill modal** now scrolls horizontally (`overflow-x:auto`, scoped to the modal) instead of silently clipping its rightmost column — fixes both the AIV-triggered desktop clip and a pre-existing narrow-band clip.\\n\\n## Cleanups (from `/code-review high` + `/simplify high`, proven net-zero)\\n\\n- Shared **`gpCurrencyCell(label, value, present, extraClass)`** + `gpAivCell` helpers in the parent form; every currency `` in the monthly, drill and home tables routes through them (one place for the empty glyph / sort fallback / cell shell).\\n- Sort column index now derived from each header\'s DOM **`cellIndex`**, so inserting/removing a column no longer needs `data-sort-col` hand-renumbering.\\n- `container-type` scoped to a dedicated `.gp-col-priority-host` class (drill-modal wrap no longer an idle query container); responsive hide simplified to a nested `@media → @container` (no card-mode counter-rule).\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\n- **138/138 byte-identical** check (node): `gpCurrencyCell`/`gpAivCell` output matches the original inline cells across present/absent, ±, zero, and null-AIV inputs.\\n- All three `form_template.yaml` files parse as YAML.\\n- Reviewed via `/code-review high` (workflow-backed: 8 finders → verifiers; 0 correctness bugs — findings were the approved cramped-band hide, the existing em-dash sort convention, and a pre-existing shared-helper pattern) and `/simplify high`.\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Re-reviewed with `/code-review medium` + `/simplify medium` (clean).\\n\\n🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)\\n\\nhttps://claude.ai/code/session_01LW5Y2vPFFBoxds5yzG7Wyi\\n\\n\\n___\\n\\n## **CodeAnt-AI Description**\\nAdd pack size capture and filterable price search on specials receipts\\n\\n### What Changed\\n- The receipt flow now requires a pack size before a price can be saved, and clears it again if the order is moved back to outstanding\\n- Specials tables now show pack size in endorse and history views, and the price column is relabelled to “Total ex VAT” throughout\\n- Endorse and history views can now search by both Total ex VAT and Pack Size\\n- The outstanding entry form now shows the updated “Total ex VAT” label and includes a Pack size input alongside invoice and batch fields\\n\\n### Impact\\n`✅ Fewer incomplete special order receipts`\\n`✅ Clearer price entry for pharmacy staff`\\n`✅ Faster lookup of priced specials by amount or pack size`\\n\\n\\n[🔄 Retrigger CodeAnt AI Review](https://api.codeant.ai/pr/retrigger_review?REDACTED&org=khairm)
💡 Usage Guide\\n\\n### Checking Your Pull Request\\nEvery time you make a pull request, our system automatically looks through it. content.subject: Explore sidebar chip/tab/nav code (3 Opus + 1 base agent)
content.description: Map rendering (row dots + chips + hover-expand), data (tab names/order, per-source status primitive), navigation (focus workspace + specific tab). Output feeds Sol's implementation brief.

content.subject: Write Sol brief + Sol implements sidebar tab-chips feature
content.description: Spec: 1 tab → single dot next to workspace name, no chips. 2+ tabs → no name dots; per-tab chips always expanded, agent icon with status-dot badge on icon top-right corner, label = actual tab name truncated ~18ch with tooltip, chips wrap rows, tab order = tab strip order, shell tabs get chip (terminal icon, no dot unless status), chip click opens workspace + that tab. Sol edits only, no commit.

content.subject: Review, marker, commit, push, /code-review low
content.description: Fable review of Sol's diff; add FEATURES.md marker row in same commit (every feature needs a marker); LF-only staged blobs (0 CR); commit direct to main; push; run /code-review low.

content.subject: Build via CI + notify user
content.description: Ship via full gh build (--ref main), never hot-patch. Monitor run; on green ask user (AskUserQuestion) re install timing — never install on own initiative.

content.subject: Inspect tab-chip integration points
content.description: Read the notification store, sidebar components/hooks, pane types, and navigation consumer needed for TAB-CHIPS.

content.subject: Implement tab-chip data and UI
content.description: Add ordered tab models/status folding, sidebar title and binding hook, chip UI, row-dot behavior, and tabId navigation.

content.subject: Verify tab-chip changes
content.description: Run colocated tests, scoped Biome over every changed file, and scoped/practical TypeScript validation; report only introduced errors.

content.subject: Harden tab deep-link behavior
content.description: Gate all automation link effects on pane-layout readiness and make sidebar chips navigate only by tabId.

content.subject: Enrich static tab titles
content.description: Add persisted browser page-title/URL and comment-author fields to descriptors, resolver, and tests.

content.subject: Test deep-link readiness semantics
content.description: Add behavioral tests covering readiness gates, tab retry/dedup, and terminal focus-or-add.

content.subject: Verify xhigh review fixes
content.description: Run scoped Biome, relevant Bun tests, TypeScript comparison, LF audit, and diff checks.

content.subject: Explore sidebar chip/tab/nav code (3 Opus + 1 base agent)
content.description: Map rendering (row dots + chips + hover-expand), data (tab names/order, per-source status primitive), navigation (focus workspace + specific tab). Output feeds Sol's implementation brief.

content.subject: Write Sol brief + Sol implements sidebar tab-chips feature
content.description: Spec: 1 tab → single dot next to workspace name, no chips. 2+ tabs → no name dots; per-tab chips always expanded, agent icon with status-dot badge on icon top-right corner, label = actual tab name truncated ~18ch with tooltip, chips wrap rows, tab order = tab strip order, shell tabs get chip (terminal icon, no dot unless status), chip click opens workspace + that tab. Sol edits only, no commit.

content.subject: Review, marker, commit, push, /code-review low
content.description: Fable review of Sol's diff; add FEATURES.md marker row in same commit (every feature needs a marker); LF-only staged blobs (0 CR); commit direct to main; push; run /code-review low.

content.subject: Build via CI + notify user
content.description: Ship via full gh build (--ref main), never hot-patch. Monitor run; on green ask user (AskUserQuestion) re install timing — never install on own initiative.

content.subject: Inspect tab-chip integration points
content.description: Read the notification store, sidebar components/hooks, pane types, and navigation consumer needed for TAB-CHIPS.

content.subject: Implement tab-chip data and UI
content.description: Add ordered tab models/status folding, sidebar title and binding hook, chip UI, row-dot behavior, and tabId navigation.

content.subject: Verify tab-chip changes
content.description: Run colocated tests, scoped Biome over every changed file, and scoped/practical TypeScript validation; report only introduced errors.

content.subject: Harden tab deep-link behavior
content.description: Gate all automation link effects on pane-layout readiness and make sidebar chips navigate only by tabId.

content.subject: Enrich static tab titles
content.description: Add persisted browser page-title/URL and comment-author fields to descriptors, resolver, and tests.

content.subject: Test deep-link readiness semantics
content.description: Add behavioral tests covering readiness gates, tab retry/dedup, and terminal focus-or-add.

content.subject: Verify xhigh review fixes
content.description: Run scoped Biome, relevant Bun tests, TypeScript comparison, LF audit, and diff checks.

109		"pg-native",

111		// native binding is loaded via a dynamic `require` that @rollup/plugin-commonjs
112		// can't resolve at bundle time. Externalizing lets Node handle the require at
